IN THE CHAIR: ROBERTA METSOLA
President

Â 
1. Opening of the sitting
Video of the speeches
Â Â 

(The sitting opened at 09:03)

Â 

2. Negotiations ahead of Parliamentâs first reading (Rule 72) (action taken)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â President. â I have received separate requests for a vote in Parliament from The Left, the Greens, the ECR, the PfE, the ESN and individual Members reaching the medium threshold on the decision of the EURI Committee to enter into negotiations on the following file: 'Corporate sustainability reporting and due diligence requirements'.

The vote will be held today by secret ballot.

Concerning the joint decision of INTA and ITRE committees to enter into interinstitutional negotiations pursuant to RuleÂ 72Â (1), announced that the opening of the session on Monday 20 October, I have received no request for a vote in Parliament by Members of political groups reaching the medium threshold.

The INTA and ITRE committees may therefore start negotiations.

Â 

3. Preparation of the European Council meeting of 23 October 2025 (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â President. â The next item is the debate on the Council and Commission statements on the preparation of the European Council meeting of 23 October 2025 (2025/2669(RSP)).

I would like to remind you that this will be in the format that we've been putting into practice, where there will be no speakers' list.

So after the first round of speakers on behalf of the groups, Members who have received speaking time from their political groups or the Non-Attached Secretariat will be called to speak and will be reminded of their allotted speaking time.

Please keep an eye on the screens, and the normal rules regarding blue cards and the catch-the-eye procedure will apply during the debate.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â Madam President, honourable Members, Commissioners, on Thursday 23 October, leaders will meet in Brussels for the autumn European Council. Their meeting will, as always, start with an exchange with the President of the European Parliament.

A full agenda is expected for the European Council, with issues from foreign policy and defence and security to competitiveness and the green transition. Leaders will also talk about housing and migration issues that are particularly important for European citizens.

The European Council will discuss the situation in Ukraine as Russia continues its relentless attacks against Ukraine, civilians and civil infrastructure. Leaders will focus on stepping up support to Ukraine, including financial support for the coming years. They will also discuss efforts to further increase pressure on Russia and reaffirm the EU support for Ukraine's accession path.Â President Zelensky is expected to intervene at the beginning of the discussions about Ukraine.

Leaders will assess the latest developments in the Middle East. Peace seems to be within reach in Gaza after two years of war and human suffering. Leaders will discuss the latest developments, including the US 20-point peace plan, the catastrophic humanitarian situation and famine in Gaza, and the worsening situation in the West Bank.

On European security and defence, leaders will build on the fruitful discussions at the informal meeting in Copenhagen earlier this month. The European Council is expected to take decisions on speeding up work to ramp up Europe's defence readiness, including commitments and concrete decisions on capability projects and governance. The Roadmap on Defence Readiness 2030, which was presented on Thursday by the Commission and the High Representative, will inform leaders discussions.

Hybrid attacks and drone sightings near our critical infrastructures and hostile incursions into our airspace remind us that we must speed up our work to achieve European common readiness by 2030.

Turning now to competitiveness and the transition, leaders will focus on three main topics. First, stepping up our efforts to deliver on simplification. There has been progress so far, but we need to move up a gear in cutting bureaucratic burdens and preventing new ones. This agenda is a high political priority. The Council has mandates ready for the core remainder of Omnibus 1 on sustainability reporting and due diligence, and for Omnibus 4. And the Council stands ready to start the negotiations as soon as the European Parliament adopts its position. We should conclude work quickly. The expectation is that the European Parliament shares the sense of urgency for the simplification agenda.

Second, leaders will have a strategic debate on how to reach our climate objectives and at the same time strengthen Europe's competitiveness. The focus will be on supporting our industries and citizens on the path to carbon neutrality in 2050. As you know, enabling a successful green transition is one of the Danish Presidency's core priorities.

Third, the digital transition, which is key to creating resilient and a competitive solutions for citizens and businesses, and crucial in today's global context. To ensure Europe's digital competitiveness and sovereignty, we need regulatory and technological autonomy, reflecting our interests and values while using the full potential of new technologies. The topic of housing will be on the European Council agenda for the first time. Affordable housing has become a growing social and economic concern across the Union. Also for middle income citizens and younger generations. Leaders will assess what can be done at European level to support efforts at national, regional and local levels to improve access to affordable and decent housing. That discussion will also provide guidance for the Commission's upcoming European Affordable Housing Plan. Migration is another important topic for both European citizens and leaders. The European Council will get back to this topic to ensure that we work fast on all priority files.

Finally, the European Council will reflect on the recent elections in Moldova, which were held against the backdrop of Russia's many hybrid attacks to weaken the country's democratic institutions. The European Council will also reaffirm the EU's support for Moldova's accession path, as well as for the country's resilience and stability. All in all, we expect a substantial European Council meeting where leaders will address many crucial and interlinked challenges facing Europe today.

Ensuring our security and competitiveness goes hand in hand with increasing our efforts to harness the digital and green transformation. This is also a key priority for the Danish Presidency, and we continue to work with the European Parliament in resolving these important issues.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ursula von der Leyen, President of the Commission. â Madam President, dear Roberta, Madam Minister, honourable Members, one topic of the European Council will be defence. We discussed it two weeks ago here, in this hemicycle, and we discussed it at the informal meeting of the leaders in Copenhagen a few weeks ago. We listened to you, we listened to the leaders. This all went into the Readiness Roadmap 2030. Therefore the HR/VP and I will present our new Defence Readiness Roadmap to this European Council. We will also discuss, as we have done two weeks ago here, the reparations loan for Ukraine.

But today I would like to focus on another topic that will be at the centre of tomorrow's European Council, and that is the issue of competitiveness and decarbonisation.

Let me start by observing simple facts. Globally, the clean transition is in full swing. If you look at some figures from last year, more than 580 gigawatts in renewable energy capacity were added globally â a new record for the third year in a row. Clean tech markets around the world are booming. The global market for batteries is expected to double in size over the next five years. The global market for wind turbines continues to rise by over 10Â % every year. And in developing economies across Asia, Africa and Latin America, last year the sales of electric cars have risen by 60Â %.

This is not just good news for the climate â the rise of clean tech in Europe is also good news for our energy security, and it is a great economic opportunity. There is a reason why China is so massively investing in electric vehicles, batteries and clean tech. Of course, they see the economic opportunity and they see what the future markets will be. There is a reason why in the Middle East, which is actually the global home of fossil fuels, renewables increased fivefold in the last five years. The Middle East is dominant today on the energy market, and they tell me they want to be dominant on the energy markets in the future. And there is a reason why the Global South is choosing clean energy, because they want to get rid of choking dependencies and bring in energy that is not just clean but home-grown and, for them, affordable.

So the question for us is whether we want to reap the benefits of this global clean tech boom, or just let others profit from it. I have not the slightest doubt about the answer. Europe is a global leader in clean innovation. Let me give you some figures. For instance, 40Â % of all wind technology companies worldwide are here in the European Union. We have a very skilled workforce and a long tradition of industrial primacy. Last year, Europe's clean tech exports reached EURÂ 80Â billion. This means they have almost tripled in just six years.

But others are not sleeping. China exports almost twice as much clean tech as we do, and this risks pushing our industry out of the strategic markets â including our own. We cannot afford to fall into new and dangerous dependencies. And in recent years, we have all witnessed what happens when a single country gains control over the supply of a critical product or technology. At any moment, those dependencies can be turned into instruments of pressure. Export restrictions can be imposed overnight. Supply routes can be disrupted. And factories here in Europe can be forced to slow down or even shut down.

So I think we should be very clear and very vigilant. Europe must control the technologies that will shape our future, the manufacturing that underpins our prosperity and the critical raw materials. We should also be very clear in the determination that no country should hold the ability to undermine our economic security.

It is important for me to address this here in the hemicycle, because in the coming days, I will also engage very closely with European leaders and international partners, as a crisis in the supply of critical raw materials is no longer a distant risk. It is on our doorstep. We have already taken important steps. But now we must accelerate, decisively and urgently. We need a faster, more reliable supply of critical raw materials, both here in Europe and with trusted partners. I will be ready to propose further measures to ensure Europe's economic security, and I will accelerate what we have already put in motion.

So, to be very clear among us: Europe must be in control of strategic clean technologies and manufacturing, because clean tech is not just a tool for cutting emissions, it is key to our competitiveness and key to the independence of the European Union, and we really have to fight for it.

This is why we must refocus on supporting clean products that are made here in Europe. As the International Energy Agency reminds us, there is a cautionary tale that we must not forget. It is the story of our solar industry at the beginning of this century. Europe was a global leader in solar, but heavily subsidised Chinese competitors started to outprice Europe's young industry, and today China controls 90Â % of the global market.

This time we should learn our lesson. And let me quickly address three ways of doing so. First, we will introduce a 'made in Europe' criteria for public procurement in certain strategic sectors â just keep in mind public procurement in Europe amounts to 14Â % of the European GDP. This is massive financial firepower controlled by European governments, and we should make better use of it in order to create stable demand for clean, 'made in Europe' products.

My second point: we should ensure that new foreign investments in our industry are truly in Europe's interest. These investments are much needed, without any question, but they should create good jobs here for European workers and they should add value to Europeans right here in the European Union â not just for others.

And my third point is we will step up support for some strategic sectors. For instance, with a battery booster to support production in Europe and with a new set of initiatives for the automotive sector. We will send a very clear political signal to these industries. And that is: you are critical to Europe's future, and your future will be made in Europe. That is critical for us.

Honourable Members, a final point on competitiveness. We all know that high energy prices continue to put a strain on our competitiveness. And we all know the main reason for that: it is our dependency on imported fossil fuels and, therefore, our dependency on a volatile global market. Just look at the data across Europe; the lowest prices for energy are in countries that produce abundant low carbon energy â be it in solar, wind, hydropower, geothermal or nuclear.Â So the way ahead is clear: we must continue to invest in home-grown clean energy.

But besides our dependency on fossil fuels, there are also other reasons that prevent energy prices from going down, and we have to look at these causes too. We have, for example, to improve our energy infrastructure. Therefore, we will come with the grids package and the new energy highways initiative, and you know that we reviewed the rules for cohesion. Cohesion funding can also be invested for grids and interconnectors to improve the energy situation in the European Union. All this requires action at European level â and we are on it.

But another action is required at Member State level. Yesterday, the Commission presented a set of new, very concrete actions to cut energy bills in Europe. Let me just give you one example, and that is taxes. While energy costs are going down, taxes on energy are going up again. Look at the taxes our industry is paying on electricity; these taxes are 15 times higher than taxes on gas. And that cannot be. Or I give you two other figures: if you look at the energy bills on average for our industry in the European Union, 34Â % of those bills are taxes; and if you look at the energy bills of our households, on average in the European Union, 42Â % of those bills are taxes. So let's work together to bring these taxes down and allow our businesses and households to switch to using domestically produced electricity rather than imported fuels.

To be clear, I know, of course, that Europe will continue to use oil and gas for years to come as a basis, without any question, but that will never be a source of advantage for Europe's industry, for its consumers or for the planet. And with that in mind, we will also propose to cooperate to speed up permitting, to make full use of our State Aid rules and, of course, as I said, of our cohesion funds. Our industries and households cannot wait longer. They need affordable energy in this difficult geoeconomic climate, and they need it now.

Honourable Members, competitiveness has been a central focus of this Commission from day one. But our competitiveness agenda needs every one of us â governments and industry, Member States and European institutions. We all need the political will to move forward, because inaction is simply not an option. We all need faster decisions, because the clean industrial race will only get faster. And the choice is very clear: we can either stand still and fall behind, or we can build the jobs of the future right here in Europe. So let's make it happen together.

Thank you very much, and long live Europe!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Manfred Weber, on behalf of the PPE Group. â Madam President, European Commission, Council, dear colleagues, ladies and gentlemen, when we open the windows of this plenary today, then we see what is happening in Europe. So, for example, in Germany, half of the job losses in the last year was in the car sector: 50Â 000 well-paid jobs. It's the same in France, Romania, Spain, Italy. A big challenge. This summer, another reality in Europe, we had record-breaking wildfires in Portugal and Spain.

Meanwhile, Trump and Xi are stepping out of global actions. Fight against climate change is now on our shoulders, but we are quite isolated on global level. And Putin is sending his drones towards Europe, another reality. Poland is already shielding our borders in the east. Ukrainian warns us more are coming and Europe must be prepared.

That is, in a way, a short picture about where we are: Ukraine, climate change, the future of our economy. Though the next European Council must lead, there is no excuse anymore, it has to deliver.

It is a moment of truth in front of us in this autumn, and it is a moment of truth on Europe's global roll. So Donald Trump managed to have now a peace agreement in Gaza, and we hope that it will stay. But let's be honest. Europe was in the whole process of the Gaza talks an observer. We didn't play a real role there. And now the question for the European Council is on the table, do we now play a role when we continue to talk about Ukraine?

To be concrete, from the European Council, we expect that we are clear in financing the weapons, that the Russian assets must be used for this, and also the state, Ukraine, needs further support from our side to survive, and we have to find ways on the Council side to speak with one voice. We know on European, on global level, we only have one voice or no voice.

And just one aspect on the latest development on this, when we see the postponement of possible talks between Trump and Putin, the Budapest consideration, you know, in my country, the AfD is heavily attacking us, that we need more diplomatic efforts to approach Putin and talk with him and find a way to talk with him.

Again, we can underline that more red carpet than in Alaska is not possible. Even Trump tried it now to please and to invite Putin to come to the negotiation table. So even the right extreme in Europe must understand that Putin has only one interest, and that is destroying our European way of life.

The moment of truth is also in front of us when it is about defence. We spend in the next ten years EUR 6.4 trillion as Europeans. That is the same amount of money as the Americans will invest. And that is why I think the expectation from our citizens is that, after ten years, we have the same military capacity as Europeans, as the Americans have. That means we have to explore the European added value, the drones initiative, the missile defence, the sky shield, all the initiatives now presented by the Commission.

I also want to thank Andrius Kubilius for his efforts are really great steps forwards. I want to underline that for me, it's also the moment now to come back to the idea of Adenauer, De Gasperi and Schuman to talk really about the European Army, to do the necessary steps together.

And it is also a moment of truth for our competitiveness. Omnibus 1 will be voted this week in this House. We do it together, but we have to do much, much more. We have to do things that our businesses feel is well, that we are attractive for the best minds on global level, to come to Europe, we need urgently growth on European level. That is why single market and also the idea of an ambitious trade policy is at the centre of the EPP. And that's why I thank also the Commission for the Commission Work Programme 26, where you are covering these issues.

Finally, the link to decarbonisation â the 2040 target. Dear colleagues, it's Macron, it's Donald Tusk who are going this week to the European Council table and are asking the questions are we ready for the 2040 target, 90Â %? Yes or no? I saw yesterday a French/Spanish initiative to take the combustion engine question again on the table. So as the EPP leader I can only say welcome in the club, we always asked for this. We have to be ambitious but pragmatic in the implementation of the climate change, and that is what we want to see also from the leaders there, the workers in Europe, and also those who are socially not as strong as we are, need a proper answer, so that the transition in front of us will be not on the cost of social and of job losses in the European Union.

That is the moment of truth in front of us. And that is why we feel that it is not business as usual. We as European People's Party, we are ready to deliver. We do so and I hope that the Council will do the same this week.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Iratxe GarcÃ­a PÃ©rez, en nombre del Grupo S&D. â SeÃ±ora presidenta, nos encontramos ante un momento decisivo. En un mundo sacudido por guerras, por la crisis climÃ¡tica y por el aumento de las desigualdades, la ciudadanÃ­a europea espera algo mÃ¡s que diagnÃ³sticos: espera respuestas; respuestas que solo una Europa social y unida puede dar.

Y maÃ±ana, por primera vez, el Consejo Europeo se prepara para pedir a la ComisiÃ³n que presente un plan europeo de vivienda asequible. Es un paso histÃ³rico, y no habrÃ­a sido posible sin el trabajo incansable del Grupo SocialdemÃ³crata, porque ha sido la socialdemocracia la que ha puesto la vivienda en el centro de la agenda europea, con un principio claro: tener un hogar es un derecho, no un privilegio.

Entre 2010 y 2025, los precios de la vivienda en Europa subieron un 60Â %. MÃ¡s de la mitad de los residentes en zonas urbanas dicen que el acceso a la vivienda es su principal preocupaciÃ³n y mÃ¡s de un millÃ³n de europeos siguen sin techo; por eso proponemos una respuesta ambiciosa: 300Â 000 millones de euros en inversiÃ³n europea para viviendas dignas, una reforma de las ayudas de Estado para facilitar el acceso a las familias de renta media y baja, la regulaciÃ³n de los alquileres de corta duraciÃ³n, el refuerzo de los derechos de los inquilinos y un prÃ©stamo europeo para la renovaciÃ³n sostenible con criterios sociales. Porque una casa no es solo un tejado, es donde creamos una familia, donde cuidamos a nuestros seres queridos y construimos esperanza.

Y esa esperanza debe guiar tambiÃ©n nuestra acciÃ³n frente a la emergencia climÃ¡tica. La UniÃ³n Europea solo serÃ¡ fuerte si lidera la transiciÃ³n verde con justicia social. Por eso defendemos mantener el objetivo del 90Â % de reducciÃ³n de las emisiones para 2040, transformarlo en ley y garantizar que nadie se quede atrÃ¡s.

Queremos una ley europea de adaptaciÃ³n que nos prepare ante las olas de calor, las sequÃ­as o los incendios; queremos una Directiva de transiciÃ³n justa que proteja a los trabajadores con formaciÃ³n, con diÃ¡logo y con anticipaciÃ³n, y un fondo social para el clima mÃ¡s ambicioso, que conecte la justicia climÃ¡tica con la justicia social.

Y mientras construimos un futuro sostenible, no podemos ignorar el presente dolor y destrucciÃ³n en Gaza. El alto el fuego y la liberaciÃ³n de los rehenes son imprescindibles, pero la paz solo serÃ¡ posible si se conduce a un Estado palestino viable. Y la paz tampoco puede construirse sobre la impunidad: el Consejo debe actuar suspendiendo el Acuerdo de AsociaciÃ³n con Israel, porque Netanyahu debe rendir cuentas por su genocidio, y la justicia no es negociable.

Y frente al criminal de Putin debemos ser igualmente firmes: sabemos que no quiere la paz y solo la derrota lo sentarÃ¡ a negociar; por eso debemos aprobar ya el 19.Âº paquete de sanciones, confiscar los 200Â 000 millones en activos rusos congelados y prohibir, definitivamente, las importaciones de petrÃ³leo y gas, tal y como este Parlamento ha propuesto. El Consejo no puede seguir retrasando lo inevitable.

SeÃ±orÃ­as, construyamos una Europa que vuelva a mirar a los ojos a sus ciudadanos y ciudadanas, una Europa que ofrezca techo a quien no lo tiene, paz a quien sufre la guerra, trabajo a quien busca dignidad y futuro a quienes solo conocen la incertidumbre.

Porque cuando Europa se atreve a ser valiente, vuelve a ser creÃ­ble; porque cuando Europa demuestra ser humana, vuelve a ser grande.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jordan Bardella, au nom du groupe PfE. â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, Madame la Ministre, Madame la PrÃ©sidenteÂ vonÂ derÂ Leyen, 15Â heures par semaine, c'est le temps qu'un agriculteur consacre Ã  remplir des papiers plutÃ´t qu'Ã  cultiver sa terre. 130Â jours par an, c'est ce qu'un chef de petite et moyenne entreprise passe Ã  affronter la bureaucratie plutÃ´t qu'Ã  dÃ©velopper son activitÃ©. 200Â millions d'euros chaque annÃ©e, c'est ce que coÃ»te Ã  Michelin, fleuron industriel franÃ§ais et europÃ©en, le bavardage rÃ©glementaire du pacte vert.

Ã tous les chefs d'entreprise, artisans, commerÃ§ants, Ã  tous les travailleurs, je veux aujourd'hui parler d'un autre type de fiscalitÃ©, d'une taxe invisible qui ne dit pas son nom, celle des normes que l'on vous impose par milliers. Cet impÃ´t papier, c'est l'arme d'une bureaucratie sans visage qui complexifie la vie de ceux qui produisent, qui les immobilise derriÃ¨re un bureau des heures durant. Ce fardeau normatif, fabriquÃ© Ã  Bruxelles, puis surtransposÃ© dans nos capitales, coÃ»te prÃ¨s de 7Â % de la richesse crÃ©Ã©e en Europe, soit 1Â 200Â milliards d'euros chaque annÃ©e.

Ces textes Ã  rallonge tuent la compÃ©titivitÃ© de nos entreprises et de nos producteurs, minent le pouvoir d'achat des familles et dÃ©sincite chacun Ã  investir et Ã  innover. Disons-le, l'Europe est devenue un fardeau normatif: CSRD, CS3D, RED, REACH, GSR, IED, DSA et j'en passe. Toutes nos usines s'en vont. Mais l'industrie Ã  normes, elle, ne connaÃ®t pas la crise.

Dans l'Europe d'UrsulaÂ vonÂ derÂ Leyen, dans l'Europe de Macron, la croissance est devenue taboue et la dÃ©croissance, elle, est sur toutes les lÃ¨vres. Nos entrepreneurs, nos agriculteurs, nos artisans, nos industriels sont Ã  tort considÃ©rÃ©s comme des suspects par une technocratie qui entend remettre notre avenir dans la dÃ©pendance aux puissances Ã©trangÃ¨res. Ã force de poser des verrous sur la production nationale, vous prenez le risque de nous faire sortir de l'histoire. Pendant que les Ãtats-Unis, la Chine ou l'Inde avancent, l'Europe, elle, regarde le train passer.

Il est temps de rÃ©armer notre continent et nos nations dans la guerre Ã©conomique mondiale. Il est temps de libÃ©rer nos entreprises, de libÃ©rer nos forces productives, de suspendre sans dÃ©lai le pacte vert, de simplifier l'Ã©conomie nationale, de redonner fiÃ¨rement Ã  nos entrepreneurs les conditions du succÃ¨s, de la croissance et de la rÃ©ussite. Vivre libre ou disparaÃ®tre, tel est le dÃ©fi existentiel qui se pose Ã  l'Europe. Les Patriotes ont dÃ©jÃ  fait leur choix.

Â

Â Â ValÃ©rie Hayer, au nom du groupe Renew. â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, Madame la Ministre, Madame la PrÃ©sidente, j'ai choisi ce matin de me focaliser sur la dÃ©fense, mais je voudrais Ã©videmment rÃ©agir au cÅur de votre discours de ce matin, le lien entre compÃ©titivitÃ© et dÃ©carbonation, parce qu'il faut parfois, souvent, rappeler dans cet hÃ©micycle que la dÃ©carbonation et la compÃ©titivitÃ© ne s'opposent pas. Bien au contraire.

La dÃ©carbonation est la clÃ© de notre compÃ©titivitÃ©. La dÃ©carbonation est la clÃ© de notre sÃ©curitÃ© Ã©conomique et de notre autonomie stratÃ©gique. Donc ce n'est certainement pas le moment de ralentir, et vous trouverez Ã©videmment le groupe Renew Ã  vos cÃ´tÃ©s pour dÃ©velopper et conforter cet agenda.

Chers collÃ¨gues, Â«les forts font ce qu'ils peuvent et les faibles subissent ce qu'ils doiventÂ»: cette citation de Thucydide nous rappelle que le dÃ©bat qui anime l'Union europÃ©enne aujourd'hui n'est pas nouveau. C'est celui auquel ont Ã©tÃ© confrontÃ©es toutes les puissances depuis l'AntiquitÃ©. Comment se protÃ©ger? Comment assurer la sÃ©curitÃ© de nos peuples? PrÃ©parer la guerre est-il vraiment la condition de la paix? Les menaces contre l'Europe nous dÃ©montrent que oui. Oui, nous devons prÃ©parer la guerre pour avoir la paix. Et oui, nous devons assumer nous-mÃªmes cette responsabilitÃ©.

Ce que j'Ã©nonce ici comme une Ã©vidence ne l'a pas Ã©tÃ© pendant de trop longues annÃ©es. Parce que, chers collÃ¨gues, nous avons failli sur deux points essentiels. D'abord, nous n'avons pas vu venir la menace russe. Par ignorance et par faiblesse. Et parce que nous avons cru que rÃ©pondre aux provocations ne ferait qu'aggraver les choses. Nous avons eu tort. Ensuite, parce que notre sÃ©curitÃ© a trop longtemps reposÃ© sur les Ãtats-Unis ou sur les capacitÃ©s de nos membres. Or, la rÃ©alitÃ©, dans ce cas-lÃ  plus brutale, nous le rappelle: si nous renonÃ§ons Ã  la puissance, nous renonÃ§ons Ã  notre libertÃ©.

Alors oui, chers collÃ¨gues, il est temps pour l'Europe d'assumer sa puissance. Alors oui, bien sÃ»r, on soutient Ã©videmment l'agenda de la Commission 2030 sur la dÃ©fense et ce qu'on propose, c'est une totale souverainetÃ© europÃ©enne sur le sujet: maÃ®trise de l'usage de nos armes, plus d'investissements dans les infrastructures pour le dÃ©ploiement de matÃ©riel militaire, bouclier aÃ©rien au-dessus de l'Union europÃ©enne et du territoire ukrainien, mobilisation des avoirs russes gelÃ©s comme garantie de prÃªts de rÃ©paration pour l'Ukraine. Ce sont les conditions sine qua non d'une vraie Europe de la dÃ©fense, gage de notre indÃ©pendance.

Chers collÃ¨gues, une derniÃ¨re chose. L'Europe ne se protÃ©gera de l'extÃ©rieur que par son unitÃ©. Or, la Hongrie de M.Â OrbÃ¡n est devenue une menace inacceptable contre nos propres intÃ©rÃªts. On a eu des rÃ©vÃ©lations sur un scandale d'espionnage dans nos institutions et on sait tous que ViktorÂ OrbÃ¡n est le cheval de Troie du Kremlin. Alors, je le redis ici: la paix en Ukraine ne sera dÃ©cidÃ©e que par les Ukrainiens, avec les EuropÃ©ens, et sans plier d'aucune faÃ§on aux conditions de VladimirÂ Poutine.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Bas Eickhout, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. â MadamÂ President, thank you to the Commission President and also the Council for being here at your own discussion.

First of all, of course, there are many topics on the agenda that need to be addressed and we're very happy with all that, especially, of course, the attention to housing, as has already been mentioned. Just saying that if I then read the Council conclusions on housing, it's quite marginal, right? I mean, the Council has discussed it and asked the Commission to take it further. Thank you, but we expect a bit more if the if the Council is going to discuss housing.

But I think the biggest discussion â and I'm also thanking you, Madam President, for the main topic â is about competitiveness and the Green Deal and our climate targets. And let's be very clear: tomorrow we will see the biggest attack on the Green Deal by the heads of state. That is very concerning, because for Europe's future, we need a long-term vision, we need stability, we need certainty. That is what the Green Deal is providing, and questioning the Green Deal is just throwing away Europe's future.

If I then look at the simplification agenda that is being mentioned in the Council conclusions over and over, but also by you, Madam President, it's not helping because it is creating uncertainty. Which law is still there? Which law is going to be there in a couple of months' time? But it's also deregulation. What we are doing to the due diligence law, what we are doing to chemicals, it is deregulation and not simplification. If you want to compete with the US on deregulation, we will always lose from the US. It's the most stupid thing to do to compete with the US on deregulation. It's not in our advantage of competitiveness.

Secondly, the attention to clean tech is very important, and thank you also for raising that in your speech very clearly, because that is where we are still losing from China, and that is also where Draghi was putting most of the attention, which I think people have forgotten. Clean tech is essential for electrification, and I am happy that you are mentioning taxation â that needs to shift from a taxation on electricity to gas because that's where the taxation needs to be.

But if we're talking about new technologies and with the cars, we are every time coming back to the old technologies like the combustion engine or, in your letter mentioning biofuels, that is not clean tech. That is the old industry. If we are not changing that, we will lose from China also on the car industry, and as you have said, we want to make that in Europe, but then also be consistent in your policies on the cars â because, in the end, the only way to be competitive in the future is to maintain our Green Deal and to keep our targets up also for 2040.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Manon Aubry, au nom du groupe The Left. â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, Madame la PrÃ©sidente de la commission, Madame la Ministre, Madame von der Leyen, le gÃ©nocide Ã  Gaza n'est pas terminÃ©. Ã l'heure oÃ¹ je vous parle, le gouvernement israÃ©lien continue de tuer et de bloquer l'approvisionnement en eau, en nourriture, en mÃ©dicaments dans la bande de Gaza, alors que les Palestiniens meurent de faim, qu'au moins 20Â 000Â blessÃ©s sont dans un Ã©tat d'urgence absolue et que l'Organisation mondiale de la santÃ© alerte sur des Ã©pidÃ©mies hors de contrÃ´le.

Non, Madame von der Leyen, quoi que vous en disiez, ceci n'est pas une paix. Et je suis d'ailleurs sidÃ©rÃ©e de voir Ã  quel point aujourd'hui la situation dans la bande de Gaza semble avoir complÃ¨tement disparu des radars de ce Parlement europÃ©en. Parce qu'en rÃ©alitÃ©, IsraÃ«l a dÃ©jÃ  violÃ© le cessez-le-feu et assassinÃ© 100 Palestiniens depuis le 10 octobre. Tout comme il avait dÃ©jÃ  brisÃ© le cessez-le-feu en mars dernier. Parce que Netanyahou utilisera le moindre prÃ©texte pour, je cite ses mots, Â«finir le boulotÂ». Parce qu'il n'y a aucune, je dis bien aucune, garantie du retrait effectif de l'armÃ©e israÃ©lienne de la bande de Gaza, ni de calendrier contraignant.

Alors, je vous le demande, Madame von der Leyen, que vaut pour vous la vie d'un Palestinien? Chers collÃ¨gues, le concert d'acclamations qu'on a entendu jusqu'ici en faveur d'un prÃ©tendu plan de paix de Donald Trump, et derriÃ¨re lequel beaucoup d'entre vous s'abritent, ne m'inspire rien d'autre que de la honte et du mÃ©pris. Alors comme Ã§a, il faudrait tresser des lauriers au prÃ©sident amÃ©ricain pour avoir forcÃ© Netanyahou Ã  nÃ©gocier? La vÃ©ritÃ©, c'est qu'il a prÃ©fÃ©rÃ©, pendant plus de deux ans, livrer des armes et des missiles Ã  l'armÃ©e israÃ©lienne et donner des tapes dans le dos de son grand ami Bibi. Il faudrait Ã©galement remercier Netanyahou de suspendre momentanÃ©ment l'anÃ©antissement de la population gazaouie, comme si une pause toute relative des bombardements pouvait l'absoudre de tous ses crimes. Comme si les 68Â 000Â morts et les 170Â 000Â blessÃ©s Ã©taient dÃ©jÃ  oubliÃ©s. Comme si le traumatisme incommensurable des 2 millions de personnes qui ont cÃ´toyÃ© la mort et la peur chaque heure et chaque jour pendant plus de deux ans appartenait dÃ©jÃ  au passÃ©.

Madame von der Leyen, ce que vous avez appelÃ© un plan de paix sÃ¨me en vÃ©ritÃ© les graines du chaos. Il installe une tutelle nÃ©ocoloniale sur la bande de Gaza et sonne le glas de l'autodÃ©termination du peuple palestinien. Il entÃ©rine l'occupation israÃ©lienne Ã  Gaza et la colonisation en Cisjordanie. Il livre l'enclave gazaouie aux vautours qui veulent transformer la terre des Palestiniens en opportunitÃ© pour promoteurs immobiliers. Il foule aux pieds le droit international. Il ne prÃ©voit aucune justice pour les criminels israÃ©liens. Et vous le savez, Madame von der Leyen, sans justice, il n'y aura pas de paix.

Et malgrÃ© cela, la Commission europÃ©enne, les gouvernements europÃ©ens, une grande partie de cet hÃ©micycle applaudissent en chÅur. L'Histoire retiendra votre responsabilitÃ© et votre complicitÃ©. La responsabilitÃ© de celles et ceux qui ont continuÃ© de traiter l'Ãtat d'IsraÃ«l comme un partenaire privilÃ©giÃ©, en refusant de rompre l'accord d'association qui le lie Ã  l'Union europÃ©enne. La complicitÃ© de celles et ceux qui ont constamment et dÃ©libÃ©rÃ©ment refusÃ© de prendre la moindre sanction contre le gouvernement gÃ©nocidaire de Benjamin Netanyahou. Ni sanctions Ã©conomiques, ni financiÃ¨res, ni commerciales, ni diplomatiques, ni application des mandats d'arrÃªt de la Cour pÃ©nale internationale. L'impunitÃ© totale au mÃ©pris du droit international. Madame von der Leyen, le gÃ©nocide Ã  Gaza n'est pas terminÃ©. Votre complicitÃ©, elle, doit enfin cesser. Agissez.

Â Â RenÃ© Aust, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin! Wieder einmal trifft sich der EuropÃ¤ische Rat, und wieder einmal stehen dieselben Themen auf der Tagesordnung, dieselben Reden, dieselben Versprechen â und am Ende bleibt alles beim Alten. WÃ¤hrend Amerika und Asien investieren, diskutieren Sie Ã¼ber immer neue Richtlinien und Vorschriften. WÃ¤hrend dort gehandelt wird, wird hier bÃ¼rokratisiert. So verliert Europa StÃ¼ck fÃ¼r StÃ¼ck seinen Wohlstand und seine WettbewerbsfÃ¤higkeit, auch wenn Sie stÃ¤ndig von WettbewerbsfÃ¤higkeit sprechen.

Meine Damen und Herren, Europa fehlt es nicht an Ideen, aber es fehlt an mutigen Politikern, die das Notwendige tun: das Verbrennerverbot zurÃ¼cknehmen, den Green Deal rÃ¼ckabwickeln. Sie reden Ã¼ber kommende Generationen, aber zerstÃ¶ren die Zukunft unserer Industrie. Sie kÃ¼ndigen Innovationen an, aber viele unserer talentierten Forscher und Entwickler verlassen mittlerweile unseren Kontinent. Und wÃ¤hrend unsere BÃ¼rger immer hÃ¶here Preise zahlen, verliert diese EuropÃ¤ische Union das Vertrauen der Menschen, weil sie ihre Sorgen ignoriert.

Doch â und das ist die gute Nachricht â die Zeit Ã¤ndert sich. In vielen LÃ¤ndern Ã¼bernehmen endlich wieder Regierungen Verantwortung, die Patriotismus mit gesundem Menschenverstand verbinden. Tschechien, Italien, die Slowakei, Ungarn, hoffentlich bald Deutschland mit der AfD in der Regierung â sie alle zeigen: Patriotische KrÃ¤fte kÃ¶nnen Europa erneuern. Darum sage ich Ihnen: Europa braucht keine grÃ¼nen Ideologen, sondern ein Europa der Vernunft, ein Europa, das auf Leistung setzt, auf Wissenschaft, Unternehmertum, auf Freiheit, weniger Steuern, weniger BÃ¼rokratie, keine Chatkontrolle, also mehr Vertrauen in die Menschen, die unsere Nationalstaaten und dieses Europa tragen, statt Vertrauen in immer neue Verwaltung.

Wir mÃ¼ssen uns von den Fesseln der Ãberregulierung befreien, zurÃ¼ck zu einem Europa souverÃ¤ner Nationen, einem Europa der Innovation, der Arbeit, der Freiheit und des Fortschritts. Denn, meine Damen und Herren, die Zukunft Europas entscheidet sich nicht in Diskussionsrunden von Politikern, sondern in den Werkhallen, in den Laboren und auf den HÃ¶fen, wo unser aller Wohlstand geschaffen wird.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â SeÃ¡n Kelly (PPE). â A UachtarÃ¡in Metsola, a UachtarÃ¡in von der Leyen, affordable housing has become one of the most urgent social and economic challenges across Europe. Just last week, Eurostat reported that house prices in the EU have risen by an average of 53.4Â % over the past decade. And in Ireland, that figure sits well above average at a staggering 83.4Â %. For many, owning or even renting a home is out of reach. But the housing crisis is also a direct threat to Europe's economy. The shortage of housing is making it increasingly difficult for businesses to attract and retain workers. This exacerbates labour shortages and undermines competitiveness.

The upcoming European affordable housing plan is a crucial opportunity to deliver a practical, business-friendly and future-orientated framework; one that mobilises public and private investment, promotes innovation and digitalisation and, above all, simplifies regulation and planning â the bane of our lives. This is our chance to guide the Commission's affordable housing plan. Let us not let the opportunity slip.

Az EurÃ³pai UniÃ³ vezetÅinek fanyalgÃ³ hozzÃ¡Ã¡llÃ¡sa Ã©s a hÃ¡borÃºs javaslatok szajkÃ³zÃ¡sa helyett mindent meg kellene tenni a diplomÃ¡ciai megoldÃ¡s tÃ¡mogatÃ¡sÃ¡ra. Egy potenciÃ¡lis budapesti Trump-Putyin csÃºcstalÃ¡lkozÃ³ tÃ¶rtÃ©nelmi lehetÅsÃ©g lenne, esÃ©ly a bÃ©kÃ©re UkrajnÃ¡ban. Ez EurÃ³pa egyetlen Ã©rdeke ma.

PRESIDENZA: PINA PICIERNO

Â Â Carlo Fidanza (ECR). â Signora Presidente, signor Ministro, signor Commissario, onorevoli colleghi, diciamocelo: la vera novitÃ  di questo Consiglio Ã¨ la lettera sulla competitivitÃ  che la Presidente von der Leyen ha inviato lunedÃ¬ ai leader.

Una novitÃ , anche perchÃ©, per la prima volta, leggiamo una autocritica sul Green Deal, nella quale, oltre finalmente a un'apertura sui biocarburanti per le auto, si riconosce che molte norme rischiano di aggravare la dipendenza strategica dalla Cina e far perdere competitivitÃ  alle nostre industrie. Finalmente ci si Ã¨ accorti che il re Ã¨ nudo!

Ma, mentre con la mano destra si scrive una lettera per andare finalmente incontro a imprese e cittadini, sapendo di avere a che fare con governi sempre meno disposti a seguire queste follie, come se ci fosse ancora Timmermans, con la mano sinistra si stringe la corda al collo di imprese e cittadini, con la nefasta proposta della legge sul clima e della riduzione delle emissioni al 90% nel 2040. E si va avanti con soluzioni deboli, come il timido rinvio del regolamento sulla deforestazione, che invece dovrebbe essere radicalmente modificato.

Purtroppo, il messaggio Ã¨ chiaro: la strada dei compromessi al ribasso con la sinistra Ã¨ un vicolo cieco che porta l'Europa verso un irreversibile declino.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Gerben-Jan Gerbrandy (Renew). â MadamÂ President, MadamÂ Minister, Commissioner, colleagues, security, prosperity and climate action. The European Council agenda focuses on the big challenges facing Europe right now. Indeed, we cannot take chances with Putin, nor can we gamble with the living standards of future generations. Uncomfortable risks that need addressing.

But there's just one 100Â % risk here, and that's climate change. It's 100Â % sure that it's coming for us and for our European way of life â not tomorrow, but today. Floods, heatwaves, droughts, crop failures, broken lives. Today, not tomorrow.

Six years ago, PresidentÂ von der LeyenÂ presented the Green Deal and a new model for growth and prosperity. A model to stop importing EURÂ 450Â billion worth of fossil fuels each year. A model that chooses to buy European cars in the future instead of Chinese. A model that leads the world in clean technology. A model that needs, and I quote President von der Leyen, 'relentless focus and effort'. That is what we need from this week's European Council: relentless focus and effort at the single 100Â % threat that we're facing â climate change.

Strong leadership means looking ahead and leading people to a better future, having the courage to take difficult decisions â principles that too often yield to political convenience. Therefore I call upon our heads of state to do what scientists advise us to do: adopt ambitious climate policies, including the necessary social and industrial policies.

But this House should also look at itself. As directly elected politicians with a clear mandate from Europeans who rely on us, we also have to show the necessary courage and responsibility. Our European citizens deserve it, our planet demands it and our economy needs it.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Diana Riba i Giner (Verts/ALE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, la UniÃ³n Europea ha desempeÃ±ado histÃ³ricamente un papel clave en la reducciÃ³n de las desigualdades territoriales. Este principio ha sido fundamental en la construcciÃ³n europea y la base del estrecho vÃ­nculo que mantienen las regiones con nuestro proyecto polÃ­tico comÃºn.

Pero, lamentablemente, la propuesta del presupuesto para la UniÃ³n Europea y los nuevos planes nacionales van en la direcciÃ³n contraria. La fusiÃ³n de fondos y su recentralizaciÃ³n hacia las capitales amenazan seriamente la transparencia y el equilibrio regional: asÃ­ lo advierte claramente la opiniÃ³n del ComitÃ© de las Regiones. AdemÃ¡s, la falta de implicaciÃ³n de las autoridades locales y regionales, especialmente en la polÃ­tica de cohesiÃ³n, pone en riesgo la legitimidad democrÃ¡tica del presupuesto europeo.

Por estas razones, desde la Alianza Libre Europea pedimos la retirada de esta propuesta. Europa merece un proyecto mejor, que reconozca y potencie su mayor fortaleza: las regiones y los ciudadanos.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Irene Montero (The Left). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, Consejo, la presidenta Von der Leyen se ha ido. Total, quÃ© mÃ¡s da, si aquÃ­ el que manda no es este Parlamento, sino Donald Trump, Â¿verdad? Porque silba Trump y no falta ni uno de ustedes para dejarse humillar, uno por uno.

Israel estÃ¡ bombardeando Gaza de nuevo: ni un solo dÃ­a se ha cumplido el alto el fuego. La foto de los presidentes europeos dando la mano a Trump con una sonrisa en Egipto les perseguirÃ¡ siempre. Â¿Por quÃ© sonreÃ­an? Â¿Por quÃ© han hecho creer a la gente que habrÃ­a paz? Â¿Pensaban que con ese show de Trump la gente dejarÃ­a de movilizarse en Europa contra el genocidio? Â¿O es que ustedes tambiÃ©n quieren hacer negocios en Gaza?

SeÃ±ora Von der Leyen ausente, igual que hizo Europa con los nazis, el exterminio contra el pueblo palestino solo puede acabar con los genocidas desarmados y entre rejas. Dejen de enviar ya armas a Israel, rompan de una vez las relaciones comerciales y diplomÃ¡ticas, que si algÃºn europeo debe acabar en la cÃ¡rcel no estÃ¡ en los barcos de la Flotilla, estÃ¡ en este Consejo Europeo, y es por su apoyo al genocidio.

Â Â Fabio De Masi (NI). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin! Sicherlich sollten wir nicht naiv sein. Jetzt wird jede Woche in der Ãffentlichkeit eine neue Sau durchs Dorf getrieben. Â Â Dolors Montserrat (PPE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, Europa afronta, al mismo tiempo, un problema de vivienda joven, un desafÃ­o de defensa y una crisis migratoria.

Sin acceso a la vivienda no hay proyecto de vida para los jÃ³venes. Necesitamos mÃ¡s vivienda asequible, menos impuestos, mÃ¡s facilidades para acceder a una hipoteca y luchar contra la okupaciÃ³n. Porque los jÃ³venes europeos no quieren discursos, quieren soluciones y oportunidades reales.

Al mismo tiempo, apoyamos a los lÃ­deres europeos en su negociaciÃ³n por el final de la guerra en Ucrania, a pesar de la sonora ausencia de SÃ¡nchez. La mayorÃ­a de la sociedad espaÃ±ola apoya una paz justa y duradera, que garantice el futuro de Ucrania y frene el plan imperialista de Putin contra Europa. Los ciudadanos ya no ven la guerra como algo lejano, y lo que esperan de Europa es una respuesta a la altura de la amenaza rusa: por eso necesitamos unidad y acelerar el plan de defensa para 2030.

Y el tercer desafÃ­o: la inmigraciÃ³n, que no resolveremos con las regularizaciones masivas de la izquierda ni los discursos de odio de los extremos, sino con una polÃ­tica firme, vinculada a un empleo y respetuosa con los valores europeos; con una Directiva de retorno que expulse al que delinca y acuerdos con terceros paÃ­ses para que las pateras de las mafias no salgan de los puertos.

Solo Europa ây no los criminalesâ deben decidir quiÃ©n entra en Europa. Si no lo hacemos, Sanchez seguirÃ¡ ignorando a Europa, polarizando a la sociedad y usando a menores e inmigrantes como mercancÃ­a electoral para desestabilizar a Gobiernos rivales.

Lo que necesitamos son leyes firmes y fronteras seguras.

Â Â Paolo Borchia (PfE). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, Presidente Von der Leyen, se lei fosse rimasta in Aula, Le avrei detto che domani sera, come tutti i colleghi, sarei rientrato sul mio territorio per confrontarmi con agricoltori, con studenti, con sindaci, con il Paese reale, con quanti chiederanno come Ã¨ andata la sessione plenaria.

Un contesto dove il potere d'acquisto inizia a diventare sempre piÃ¹ preoccupante, dove gli studenti vogliono scappare all'estero per avere stipendi piÃ¹ dignitosi, dove i sindaci faticano sempre piÃ¹ a garantire i servizi per i propri cittadini: ma io cosa potrÃ² raccontare a questo spaccato di societÃ ? Che la presidente della Commissione europea Ã¨ venuta in Aula compiacendosi che il mercato delle turbine elettriche eoliche sta aumentando del 10%? Che il Sud del mondo â che fatica ad avere da mangiare â richiede energia pulita?

Quest'Aula e questa Commissione sono complici di un'Europa che non sa dare risposte: dall'ossessione per gli armamenti, all'ansia per la decarbonizzazione a tutti i costi, a un'impostazione cervellotica e ostinata che non si Ã¨ mai messa in discussione, che soprattutto non ha mai voluto ascoltare chi la pensa diversamente.

Questa Ã¨ la realtÃ , questo Ã¨ quanto sta succedendo. Ã un'impostazione che distrugge chi produce: -32% per il mercato automobilistico in Italia. Noi non saremo mai vostri complici!

Â Â Charlie Weimers (ECR). â Madam President, Commissioner, Minister, the centre-right majority is ready to work with you on combating illegal migration. You had our support on the visa suspension mechanism. Now we expect results on the Return Regulation. So why are you opening the door to migration from Morocco, Tunisia and Egypt with so-called talent partnership and student visas?

Who abuses our welfare systems? Who commits crime? Who become the terrorists? Is it the Europeans or could it be migrants from North Africa and the Middle East? And when they are ordered home, their governments refuse to take them back. Twenty years of negotiations with Morocco: still no readmission deal.

When will you understand? We don't want more migration â not illegal, and certainly not from North Africa and the Middle East, legal or not!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Martin HojsÃ­k (Renew). â Madam President, now, there is this unusual custom that actually spread around many of the Member States, and that is to blame Brussels, to blame Europe for every hardship, for every problem there is. This is something we often see here from the extreme right.

Yet often the problems start at home. We are having a discussion about the European Council meeting, and what we talk about is a Commission where, dear Council, actually the problem is very often you. If you look at the Letta report, if you look at the IMF data, the bureaucracy starts on a national level. What we see is much more than Trump's threat of tariffs. We see that the fact that we have 40Â % tariffs on the goods and more than 120Â % tariffs on the services within the European Union. We are suffocating our companies. We talk about the need for clean tech, which is really, really big, but we are actually putting obstacles in front of the companies who want to grow in Europe.

So please, dear Council, put one important thing on your agenda that's missing: how to stop putting obstacles between the Member States. Everybody talks about cutting back bureaucracy on the European level. What about stopping unnecessary bureaucracy you put it on top of the European rules because people face hardship. Despite all the blaming of Brussels, people trust Europe more than ever and we need to deliver. And it's time for the Council to actually start delivering. We need stronger Europe. We need a super Europe that actually cares about the people, cares about the competitiveness, that housing is a right, not a commodity, that really gets independent from the imported fossil energy. All that âÂ and it was great to hear it from the President â is the right way forward.

And I think the majority of this House is ready to support it. But what we see often is Council putting obstacles. So please, for example, on the ETS, make sure all the money goes back to people and businesses and just doesn't end up in the state coffers. And I could go on. What we need is a stronger Europe, not a fragmented one.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Reinhold Lopatka (PPE). â Madam President, dear colleagues, the European Council this week must demonstrate unity. For me, this is the most important thing. Europe cannot afford indecision; we need determined action.

First, Ukraine: our support must continue politically, financially and also through reconstruction. Europe must stay united here to help Ukraine, and we have to do everything to secure a just and lasting peace. And that can only be achieved, as I see it, on Ukraine's terms.

Second, security and resilience: Europe must better protect its citizens by safeguarding critical infrastructure and countering hybrid threats.

Third, the Middle East: The European Union must play a role here. We have to play a role in diplomatic, political and economics. We have to work here and to continue to work on a two-state solution.

Finally, migration: Austria supports a balanced implementation of the new pact on migration and asylum, with strong borders, effective returns and deepening partnerships with countries of origin and transit.

So this European Council must send a clear message: Europe delivers with unity.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Gilles Pennelle (PfE). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, l'immigration sera une nouvelle fois Ã  l'ordre du jour du Conseil europÃ©en. Mais ses dirigeants Ã©coutent-ils les peuples europÃ©ens? Ils le disent partout. Les peuples europÃ©ens ne veulent plus d'immigration massive, ils n'en peuvent plus. Cette immigration massive menace leur libertÃ©, leur identitÃ©, leur portefeuille bien Ã©videmment, et surtout leur sÃ©curitÃ©. Parce que c'est aujourd'hui le procÃ¨s en France, je voudrais Ã©voquer le calvaire de la petite fille, Lola, de douze ans, torturÃ©e Ã  mort par une immigrÃ©e algÃ©rienne que le gouvernement franÃ§ais a Ã©tÃ© incapable d'expulser. Et moi, quand je pense Ã  Lola, je me dis que finalement, les vrais criminels sont les immigrationnistes, ceux qui organisent cette immigration. Il faut mettre fin au pacte asile et immigration qui veut nous imposer encore plus d'immigration. Ce pacte asile immigration dÃ©montre une chose trÃ¨s claire: que pour l'Union europÃ©enne et ses dirigeants, l'immigration n'est pas un problÃ¨me mais un projet.

Â 
Â Â

ÎÎ¿ÏÏÏÎ±Î¯Î¿ Î¸Î­Î¼Î± Î±ÏÏÎ±Î»ÎµÎ¯Î±Ï ÎµÎ¯Î½Î±Î¹ Î· ÏÏÎ¿ÏÎ¸Î·ÏÎ· ÏÎ·Ï Î¹ÏÏÎ¿ÏÎ¹ÎºÎ®Ï Î±Î»Î®Î¸ÎµÎ¹Î±Ï Î³Î¹Î± ÏÎ·Î½ Î¤Î¿ÏÏÎºÎ¯Î± Î´Î¹ÎµÎ¸Î½ÏÏ.

Â Â Hannah Neumann (Verts/ALE). â MadamÂ President, dear colleagues, we are no longer at peace, and Putin moves us closer to war week after week. Drones shut down our airports. Russian fighter jets cross our skies. Disinformation divides our societies. And the US? A shaky ally at best. This challenge is far too big for any Member State to face alone.

Whenever national governments feel overwhelmed, they call on the EU to fix the problem. Strategic autonomy, European serenity, a true defence union â the speeches of heads of states are grand. The actions? Not so much.

Transfer the necessary powers to the EU? No. Provide the budget we need? A sure no. But when Europe then can't perform miracles with this non-support, the same capitals are the first ones to point the fingers.

Dear colleagues in the Council, we don't have time for these games anymore. Together, we are 500 million people with one of the world's strongest economies. Either we defend every inch of the European project together or there will be no more European project. And when that happens, by the way, your handy European scapegoat will be gone as well.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â DanuÅ¡e NerudovÃ¡ (PPE). â Madam President, Madam Minister, dear colleagues, how can an ordinary family make it to the end of the month when they need 15Â annual salaries to buy an apartment? How can a young person survive when housing costs are rising by a quarter, year after year? There is one issue fuelling populism everywhere: the housing crisis.

In the Netherlands, the far right won on the promise to fix the housing shortage. In Portugal, the far right gained power by pledging to fight soaring home prices. The reality is that the housing market is really going crazy.

We need a clear plan for how to boost construction and fight inflation, but we don't need another empty declaration. This is my concrete call to all the leaders today and to you, Madam Minister.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marta Temido (S&D). â Senhora Presidente, Senhora ComissÃ¡ria, Senhora Ministra, na agenda do prÃ³ximo Conselho Europeu hÃ¡ dois temas que algumas forÃ§as polÃ­ticas nesta Casa consideram prioridades incompatÃ­veis: a defesa e a habitaÃ§Ã£o. Ã bom ver que o Conselho Europeu nÃ£o abdica de nenhuma delas.

NÃ³s sabemos que vivemos, hoje, numa situaÃ§Ã£o hÃ­brida, em que nenhum dos nossos EstadosâMembros estÃ¡ em guerra, mas em que a UniÃ£o Europeia vive num novo contexto e as ameaÃ§as com que nos confrontamos nÃ£o sÃ£o apenas militares, sÃ£o digitais, sanitÃ¡rias, energÃ©ticas e comerciais.

NÃ³s nÃ£o podemos ignorar os desejos imperialistas que nos rodeiam. Por isso, a UniÃ£o Europeia tomou a decisÃ£o de investir na sua defesa, e apoiamos este caminho. Mas nÃ£o queremos ficar refÃ©ns do complexo industrial militar americano. NÃ£o aceitamos isso.

Mas sabemos, tambÃ©m, que a maioria dos nossos EstadosâMembros vive com problemas em termos de acesso Ã  habitaÃ§Ã£o. O direito Ã  habitaÃ§Ã£o Ã© um direito fundamental e precisamos de um plano europeu para a habitaÃ§Ã£o acessÃ­vel, que traga respostas concretas, com a reforma das ajudas de Estado, a regulaÃ§Ã£o do alojamento de curta duraÃ§Ã£o e, sobretudo, o investimento em habitaÃ§Ã£o pÃºblica.

Caras e caros colegas, proteger a UniÃ£o Europeia Ã© defender as suas fronteiras, mas sem as pessoas estarem unidas, sem terem um teto, de nada nos servem as fronteiras.

(A oradora aceita responder a uma pergunta Â«cartÃ£o azulÂ»)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â JoÃ£o Oliveira (The Left), Pergunta segundo o procedimento Â«cartÃ£o azulÂ». â Senhora Deputada Marta Temido, ouviu certamente a intervenÃ§Ã£o da presidente da ComissÃ£o, Ursula von der Leyen, em que falou precisamente sobre estes dois aspetos que referiu na sua intervenÃ§Ã£o. E a questÃ£o que lhe quero colocar Ã© se acha que esse equilÃ­brio estÃ¡ alcanÃ§ado.

A senhora presidente da ComissÃ£o falou detalhadamente sobre planos, sobre pacotes de investimento, sobre calendÃ¡rios para fazer o investimento no militarismo, na corrida aos armamentos e nos gastos militares. Sobre as questÃµes da habitaÃ§Ã£o, nem compromisso polÃ­tico, nem pacote de investimento, nem calendÃ¡rio para garantir o acesso Ã  habitaÃ§Ã£o.

A senhora deputada considera que estas matÃ©rias vÃ£o mesmo ser tratadas de forma equilibrada na reuniÃ£o do Conselho Europeu, ou acha que a questÃ£o da habitaÃ§Ã£o estÃ¡ apenas incluÃ­da na agenda para disfarÃ§ar os outros tÃ³picos?

Â

Â Â Marta Temido (S&D), Resposta segundo o procedimento Â«cartÃ£o azulÂ». â Senhor Deputado, obrigada pela sua questÃ£o, jÃ¡ sentÃ­amos falta dos seus cartÃµes azuis.

NÃ£o tenho da polÃ­tica uma leitura cÃ­nica, portanto quero mesmo crer que estes dois tÃ³picos estÃ£o incluÃ­dos na agenda, porque o presidente do Conselho Europeu, que teve em boa hora a decisÃ£o de escrever, em nome de Portugal, uma carta Ã  presidente da ComissÃ£o apelando Ã  intervenÃ§Ã£o europeia no tema da habitaÃ§Ã£o, tambÃ©m terÃ¡ a mesma iniciativa quando conduzir os trabalhos do Conselho.

Acredito que a atenÃ§Ã£o Ã s ruas da Europa faÃ§a perceber que o tema da habitaÃ§Ã£o Ã© tÃ£o divisivo como outras ameaÃ§as externas.

Â Â Martin HojsÃ­k (Renew), blue-card question. â Dear colleague, you come from Estonia, a country that faced through its history quite a bit of trouble, to say the least, from Russia. I'm wondering: do you see the dependency on imported fossil fuels as something that is a problem, and how would you replace it?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jaak Madison (ECR), blue-card answer. â Actually, it is a very good question. The problem with the Green Deal and with Russian energy is that Estonia has been always independent with energy, with oil shale. We have produced more than we need. We have exported to Finland, Latvia, Sweden. But now, because of the Green Deal â because the ideology is saying that there is too much CO2 â the plan is to shut it down by 2030 and to import energy, because unfortunately sun and wind is not enough in Estonia, especially in February.

So we have to rely on imports from the Nordics, from other countries. And then, when we are connected with the import, we face a much bigger risk because of the connections between Estonia and Finland. Once the Russian ships go there and something happens in February when it's -20Âº, we are in trouble. So that is why the Green Deal energy independence is a huge problem, because it is connected to be independent from the Russian energy, actually. But Estonia is a different case and that is why we are protecting the oil shale. It is not very good for the Green Deal, but it is for our energy security and independence.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Kathleen Funchion (The Left). â Madam President, as the Council meets, its agenda is clear: militarisation, above all else. The lines between NATO and the EU are going from blurred to non-existent. The EU is openly talking about coordinating the armies of Europe. Yet the Taoiseach, MicheÃ¡l Martin, among others, says we can't question this trajectory â asking questions apparently makes us anti-European.

I am not anti-European, but I am 100Â % pro-Irish neutrality, pro-Irish interests in the world, and our interests do not lie in a militarised Europe, where our social and agricultural funds are stolen to be spent on tanks and drones. Irish neutrality will not go quietly into the night, even if the Irish Government may want this. Irish people do not want this and will resist it!

The Council also must not â having been dragged kicking and screaming to the point of proposing the most minor sanctions on Israel â let Israel off the hook. The murder and savagery of the Netanyahu regime continues and must be punished.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Paulo Cunha (PPE). â Senhora Presidente, num mundo em constante mutaÃ§Ã£o, a Europa enfrenta um dos maiores testes Ã  sua coesÃ£o e Ã  sua capacidade de proteger quem nela vive: a gestÃ£o das migraÃ§Ãµes.

Porque Ã© que precisamos de falar de migraÃ§Ãµes no prÃ³ximo Conselho Europeu? Porque este Ã© o tema que define a capacidade da UniÃ£o Europeia de proteger as suas fronteiras, afirmar a sua soberania e gerir a confianÃ§a dos cidadÃ£os.

O equilÃ­brio entre humanidade e seguranÃ§a Ã© hoje decisivo. Precisamos de uma polÃ­tica comum que assegure vias legais e seguras de entrada, controlando as fronteiras externas e combatendo as redes criminosas que exploram o desespero humano. SÃ³ com rigor no acesso, acolhendo os que cumprem as regras e exigindo a saÃ­da a quem nÃ£o o faz, poderemos assegurar aos que ficam a dignidade que queremos para nÃ³s mesmos.

Portugal tem mostrado que Ã© possÃ­vel conciliar a responsabilidade e a solidariedade. Tem acolhido, integrado e participado ativamente em missÃµes europeias de patrulhamento e proteÃ§Ã£o das fronteiras. Mas nenhum EstadoâMembro pode enfrentar sozinho esta realidade. A Europa precisa de mais coordenaÃ§Ã£o e de uma visÃ£o estratÃ©gica partilhada, que una polÃ­ticas de migraÃ§Ã£o, defesa e seguranÃ§a.

Essa Ã© a verdadeira resposta europeia. Uma Europa que protege, acolhe e age em conjunto.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Estelle Ceulemans (S&D). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, aujourd'hui, le Conseil europÃ©en se rÃ©unit avec, en tÃªte de son agenda, compÃ©titivitÃ© et simplification. Alors soyons clairs, personne n'aime la bureaucratie et ne s'oppose Ã  une saine simplification. Mais ce qui est en marche en Europe est tout autre chose, avec une dÃ©rÃ©gulation en mode Far West, avec en ligne de mire la destruction d'importantes rÃ©glementations sociales et environnementales, des normes qui sont pourtant essentielles pour contrer les effets nÃ©fastes du tout au marchÃ©.

MadameÂ vonÂ derÂ Leyen, cette dÃ©rÃ©gulation que vous prÃ´nez, c'est une menace pour l'Europe. Elle nourrit le sentiment anti-europÃ©en et fait le lit de l'extrÃªme droite. Ce que les travailleurs et les citoyens veulent, c'est une Europe qui veille Ã  l'intÃ©rÃªt collectif et non au profit des multinationales; une Europe qui protÃ¨ge, avec des emplois de qualitÃ© et des salaires dÃ©cents; une Ã©conomie au service de l'humain et pas l'inverse. C'est notre sÃ©curitÃ© intÃ©rieure et notre sÃ©curitÃ© d'existence qui sont en jeu.

C'est pourquoi aujourd'hui je voterai contre la mise Ã  sac du devoir de vigilance et donc pour une Europe qui respecte les droits humains et les droits sociaux.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Roman Haider (PfE). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, meine Damen und Herren! Ich sage es ganz klar und deutlich: Dieses Klimaziel 2040 â 90Â Prozent EmissionsabbauÂ â ist nicht ehrgeizig, sondern ein Ãbergriff der Kommission, der unseren Lebensstil bedroht. Billionen Euro Kosten, Industrien, die ins Ausland fliehen, ArbeitsplÃ¤tze, die verloren gehen, Familien, die in Armut geraten â wer hat das entschieden? Nicht die Arbeiter, nicht die Bauern, nicht das Volk â nein, es ist eine ungewÃ¤hlte Blase von BÃ¼rokraten, die peinlich darauf achten, dass sie nur ja von irgendwelchen internationalen grÃ¼nen Klima-NGOs genug Applaus bekommen. Das ist anscheinend wichtiger als die Probleme, die wir tÃ¤glich in unseren Mitgliedstaaten erleben. Hunderte Regulierungen zum ZÃ¤hlen, Verteuern und Vermeiden von CO2âMolekÃ¼len, aber keine Regulierungen gegen die ungehemmte Massenmigration, die Ãberlastung der Sozialsysteme, die Teuerung, die Massenvergewaltigungen oder den aggressiven politischen Islam. Alles, was zÃ¤hlt, ist das gute grÃ¼ne GefÃ¼hl von den paar BÃ¼rokraten in ihrem Elfenbeinturm. Das ist keine FÃ¼hrung, das ist Arroganz.

Diese Politik schwÃ¤cht Europa. Ursula von der Leyen verspricht Wohlstand und liefert Not. Sie preist die Freiheit, aber legt die BÃ¼rger in Ketten. Wir brauchen ein Europa der Hoffnung, der Chancen, der Freiheit und nicht diesen grÃ¼nen Sozialismus, der Europa zerstÃ¶rt. Mein Appell richtet sich nicht an die Kommission â die ist unter dieser FÃ¼hrung ohnehin verloren â, mein Appell richtet sich an die Staatsâ und Regierungschefs. Sagen Sie Ursula von der Leyen: Es ist genug! Lehnen Sie diesen Wahnsinn ab! KÃ¤mpfen Sie fÃ¼r das Europa, das Sie von Ihren VorgÃ¤ngern Ã¼bernommen haben, das die Freiheit stÃ¤rkt, anstatt die BÃ¼rger mit BÃ¼rokratie niederzudrÃ¼cken.

(Der Redner lehnt eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der âblauen Karteâ von Lukas Sieper ab.)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marion MarÃ©chal (ECR). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, chers collÃ¨gues, aujourd'hui encore, trop de chiffres rÃ©vÃ¨lent notre faiblesse: 70Â % des modÃ¨les d'intelligence artificielle sont dÃ©veloppÃ©s aux Ãtats-Unis, 80Â % des produits et services numÃ©riques utilisÃ©s en Europe sont produits Ã  l'Ã©tranger. La fuite des cerveaux s'accÃ©lÃ¨re et seuls trois des cinquante meilleurs instituts de recherche sont europÃ©ens. Alors nous attendons bien plus qu'un nouvel omnibus qui va corriger Ã  la marge les erreurs du passÃ©, et notamment celles du pacte vert.

Nous voulons une rupture, un changement de logiciel sur trois points majeurs: une stratÃ©gie europÃ©enne de production et pas uniquement dÃ©carbonÃ©e, pour atteindre la souverainetÃ© et le maintien des emplois; un plan europÃ©en pour l'Ã©nergie nuclÃ©aire et une stratÃ©gie Ã©nergÃ©tique globale qui nous sortent enfin de la coÃ»teuse utopie du tout renouvelable; la prioritÃ© europÃ©enneÂ au cÅur de nos dÃ©cisions dans le rapport de force douanier, dans l'accÃ¨s aux marchÃ©s publics et en matiÃ¨re de dÃ©fense.

Nous avons trop souvent une Commission du diagnostic et de la communication. Les EuropÃ©ens attendent toujours cette Union de l'action qui nous a Ã©tÃ© promise.

Â Â

Â Â Karlo Ressler (PPE). â PoÅ¡tovana predsjedavajuÄa, kolegice i kolege, nakon godina moralnih ucjena, godina uspavanosti, uljuljkanosti, Europa se budi i preokreÄu se neki loÅ¡i trendovi. Ali Europskoj uniji joÅ¡ uvijek nedostaje i treba malo viÅ¡e garda, samopoÅ¡tovanja, zdravog razuma i inovacija.

Zato Europsko vijeÄe, nakon novih hibridnih nasrtaja s istoka, nakon provokacija dronova u Äetvrtoj godini rata na europskom kontinentu, ima odgovornost vratiti Europu zdravijim ekonomskim temeljima, konkurentnosti i energetskoj neovisnosti. To je nemoguÄe uz dogmatizam bilo koje vrste, bez nuklearne energije i uz slijepo oslanjanje na bilo koga. To je nemoguÄe bez sigurnih granica i bez vjerodostojne politike povrataka, bez rastereÄivanja gospodarstva i pojednostavljivanja regulative.

ViÅ¡e nego ikad, meÄutim, nameÄe se apsolutna nuÅ¾nost jedinstva. I izmeÄu drÅ¾ava Älanica, ali isto tako, kolege, i ovdje u Europskom parlamentu. I naÅ¡a ozbiljnost Äe se testirati u sljedeÄim mjesecima da se odmaknemo od propalih ekonomskih druÅ¡tvenih pokusa, da se vratimo naÅ¡im temeljima i onome Å¡to je Europu i uÄinilo velikom i gospodarskom silom i zbog toga Äemo svi zajedno morati pokazati da smo zaista na razini zadatka.

Â Â

Â Â Dan Nica (S&D). â DoamnÄ preÈedintÄ, domnule comisar Å efÄoviÄ, Consiliul European ar trebui sÄ aibÄ ca temÄ principalÄ competitivitatea economiei europene.

Dar, din pÄcate, Ã®n RomÃ¢nia, Ã®n Èara mea, ceea ce constatÄm cu toÈii este faptul cÄ avem cele mai mari, sau printre cele mai mari, preÈuri la energie electricÄ din Uniunea EuropeanÄ. Ieri, 150 de euro pe megawatt/orÄ, de cinci ori mai mare decÃ¢t Ã®n Spania, de trei ori mai mare decÃ¢t Ã®n FranÈa, cu 40% mai mare decÃ¢t Ã®n Germania. Acest lucru face ca economia RomÃ¢niei sÄ sufere foarte mult, pentru cÄ aceste lucruri se regÄsesc Ã®n preÈuri, lipsÄ de competitivitate, pierderi de locuri de muncÄ Èi o situaÈie grea.

De ce acest lucru? Pentru cÄ se refuzÄ interconectarea RomÃ¢niei cu partea de vest, situaÈie pe care o Ã®ntÃ¢lnim Èi pentru Bulgaria Èi Grecia. Interconectorul Ã®ntre Ungaria Èi Austria Ã®ncÄ nu se face, Ã®ntre Austria Èi Slovacia, la fel Èi aceastÄ lipsÄ de acÈiune din partea Comisiei Europene aduce prejudicii imense RomÃ¢niei.

Èi acest lucru nu face decÃ¢t sÄ avem o creÈtere a extremismului Èi o situaÈie de nemulÈumire care poate fi rezolvatÄ Ã®ntr-un singur fel: mÄsuri decisive, acum. Acum, cÃ¢t nu este prea tÃ¢rziu.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Gordan Bosanac (Verts/ALE). â Madam President, Commissioner, Madam Minister, I will not call it historic, but an unusually important European Council meeting is taking place tomorrow. For the first time ever, the Member States will discuss affordable and, I also hope, social housing. And this is good news. We have seen what the leaders will put focus on tomorrow, first discussing and then calling on the Commission to present the plan. Not that much for the first time, but okay.

I am using this stage now to call the leaders to go out of the comfort zone and be more ambitious tomorrow. I call on them to commit to take the mid-term review of the cohesion policy into account and increase investments in housing already this year. They can invite our cities and regions to have an actual seat at the table and draw up plans to invest enough money in housing and secure the money through cohesion. There is really no time to waste and we have to act, starting from tomorrow on.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Nicola Zingaretti (S&D). â Signora Presidente, signor Commissario, onorevoli colleghi, guardando al Medio Oriente, all'Ucraina, ai Balcani e ad altre tensioni una accanto all'altra, Ã¨ chiaro che nel mondo Ã¨ tornata ormai la logica del piÃ¹ forte.

Mentre le istituzioni internazionali garanti della pace vengono umiliate, i diritti vengono messi all'angolo e di fronte alle paure riemerge il nazionalismo, che divide e alla fine produce solo guerra.

Inoltre la rivoluzione digitale ci mostra un paradosso: colossi privati contano molto di piÃ¹ delle democrazie, influenzano le opinioni, la ricchezza ma difendono i loro interessi, non quelli dei cittadini e delle libertÃ . E potrei fare altre centinaia di esempi di cambiamenti, come l'energia.

Ma la conclusione in tutti gli scenari Ã¨ sempre la stessa: nessun Paese europeo da solo puÃ² affrontare queste sfide. Quindi, noi dobbiamo ricostruire speranza e per questo serve piÃ¹ Europa, unita, protagonista politica, democratica che investe sul suo futuro. E ai governi chiediamo piÃ¹ coraggio: siamo arrivati fin qui insieme ma l'Europa di oggi, cosÃ¬ com'Ã¨, non basta piÃ¹.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

Procedura "catch-the-eye"

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Nikolina Brnjac (PPE). â PoÅ¡tovana predsjedavajuÄa, povjereniÄe, drage kolegice i kolege, pred nadolazeÄi sastanak Europskog vijeÄa Å¾elim naglasiti tri kljuÄna prioriteta koji moraju oblikovati naÅ¡e zajedniÄke odluke: obranu, konkurentnost i priuÅ¡tivo stanovanje. Europa mora postati sigurnosno i industrijski samoodrÅ¾iva. Kao buduÄa izvjestiteljica EPP-a za Europski fond za konkurentnost, s posebnim programom za obrambena istraÅ¾ivanja i inovacije, vjerujem da upravo kroz ulaganja u inovacije, tehnologiju i europsku obrambenu industriju jaÄamo ne samo sigurnost, nego i konkurentnost naÅ¡eg gospodarstva.

No, sigurnost ne smijemo promatrati samo kroz obranu granica, nego i kroz socijalnu sigurnost graÄana. Kriza priuÅ¡tivog stanovanja prijeti naÅ¡oj druÅ¡tvenoj koheziji. Potrebna su usklaÄena ulaganja iz kohezijskih fondova Europske investicijske banke i nacionalnih sredstava kako bi se poveÄala ponuda stanova i smanjio jaz izmeÄu cijena i moguÄnosti graÄana. U konaÄnici, obrana, konkurentnost i stanovanje nisu odvojene politike, one su temelj europske otpornosti.

Â Â Reinier Van Lanschot (Verts/ALE). â Voorzitter, je hoeft maar naar buiten te lopen en met iemand in gesprek te gaan om te horen wat nu het grootste probleem is dat bij mensen speelt: ze kunnen geen huis vinden. Er is te weinig aanbod. Er zijn geen koopwoningen, geen huurwoningen, en wat er wÃ©l is te duur.

Nu gaat de Europese Raad voor het eerst vergaderen over het woningtekort. Voor het eerst! Wonen is een fundamenteel mensenrecht, en niemand kan een huis vinden. Hoe lang moet een probleem duren voordat het groot genoeg is en jullie het aanpakken?

Burgemeesters zijn nu gelukkig met een plan gekomen: er is 300Â miljard EUR aan directe investeringen nodig om het woningtekort in ieder geval voor een deel op te lossen. En dat zal helpen. Doe het net zoals bij Covid: begin met een noodpakket, en maak het daarna structureel onderdeel van de begroting.

Dames en heren, in "gelul" kun je niet wonen. We hebben actie nodig. Het heeft heel erg lang geduurd voordat de Europese Raad eindelijk ging vergaderen. Laten we zorgen dat het niet zo lang duurt voordat er eindelijk actie volgt. We hebben actie nodig. Mensen hebben huizen nodig. Dus: in "gelul" kun je niet wonen. Actie alsjeblieft!

Â 
Â Â

Comisia: doamna preÈedintÄ a venit Èi ne-a salutat Èi mereu ne salutÄ, âstimaÈi colegiâ, dar pleacÄ Èi nu ne mai ascultÄ. AtÃ¢t de mult ne stimeazÄ, Ã®ncÃ¢t nu ne mai ascultÄ.

Eu vreau sÄ Ètiu foarte clar: aÈi pus aceste subiecte pe ordinea de zi a Consiliului pentru a lua Èi decizii sau doar pentru a le discuta? CÃ¢te ore staÈi Ã®n Consiliu?

Nu mai putem sÄ mergem mai departe, se terminÄ Èi anul 2025, cu un proiect de buget nou, cadru financiar catastrofal. DiscutÄm Èi nu luÄm nicio decizie Èi Ã®ncepem anul viitor fÄrÄ a avea nimic sigur, nici pentru cetÄÈeni, nici pentru IMM-uri, nici pentru agriculturÄ, pentru nimic.

Â Â Ana Miranda Paz (Verts/ALE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, celebramos este debate sobre el Consejo Europeo de maÃ±ana y la seÃ±ora Von der Leyen ya se fue. Â¿DÃ³nde estuvo estos dos aÃ±os?

MaÃ±ana, el Consejo Europeo puede sancionar a Israel por el genocidio en Gaza y suspender el Acuerdo de AsociaciÃ³n UE-Israel. Si no lo hace, mantendremos la impunidad. Â¿Prefieren ustedes mantener la impunidad? El alto el fuego es bienvenido, pero no suficiente; de hecho, Israel siguiÃ³ asesinando a mÃ¡s de cien personas despuÃ©s de la tregua.

El plan de Trump mantiene la ocupaciÃ³n, no contempla el fin delÂ apartheid ni garantiza el respeto al pueblo palestino. No hay paz sin justicia ni paz sin dignidad. El plan de Trump viola el Derecho internacional y contradice la posiciÃ³n consultiva de la Corte Penal Internacional, que ordena a Israel poner fin a la ocupaciÃ³n en Cisjordania. Â Â JoÃ£o Oliveira (The Left). â Senhora Presidente, Senhora Ministra Bjerre, Senhor ComissÃ¡rio Å efÄoviÄ, como a reuniÃ£o do Conselho Europeu Ã© sÃ³ amanhÃ£, ainda vamos a tempo de deixar um desafio.

Â Â Petras GraÅ¾ulis (ESN). â Gerbiama pirmininke, gerbiami europarlamentarai, aÅ¡ norÄjau sureaguoti, galbÅ«t ir paremdamas savo kolegos Vytenio AndriukaiÄio kalbÄ. Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, liebe Menschen Europas! Wenn man Leuten wie dem Kollegen Haider von ganz rechts zuhÃ¶rt, dann hat man das GefÃ¼hl, im Rat sitzen nur ein paar graue BÃ¼rokraten, die gegen die Interessen der BevÃ¶lkerung Europas handeln. Oder wenn man Leuten wie dem Kollegen Oliveira von ganz links zuhÃ¶rt, dann hat man das GefÃ¼hl, dass die Leute, die dort sitzen, allenfalls so tun, als wÃ¼rden sie die Interessen der Menschen Europas vertreten.

Dabei werden doch eigentlich dort die groÃen Fragen unserer Zeit besprochen: die Ukraine, Verteidigung, WettbewerbsfÃ¤higkeit, Wohnen und die digitale SouverÃ¤nitÃ¤t. Wir mÃ¼ssen uns aber als europÃ¤ische Institution, als EuropÃ¤ische Union auch die Frage gefallen lassen, warum die Populisten und Extremisten dieser Zeit solche VorwÃ¼rfe Ã¼berhaupt erheben kÃ¶nnen. Das liegt ganz einfach an mangelnder Transparenz. Die Sitzungen des EuropÃ¤ischen Rates werden nicht wie diese Plenarsitzungen gelivestreamt, es gibt keine richtigen Transkripte, es gibt keine Protokolle, keine Ã¶ffentliche Einsicht. Und ja, manchmal braucht Diplomatie Diskretion, aber Intransparenz ist der Tod der Demokratie. Wenn Europa seine Debatten versteckt, dann gewinnen Populisten und Extremisten Zeit. Sie fÃ¼llen die Stille mit LÃ¼gen und mit Angst. Wer das europÃ¤ische Projekt verteidigen will, braucht mehr Transparenz.
