AdÃ³dik egy ennÃ©l is fontosabb kÃ©rdÃ©s: akarnak-e maguk egyÃ¡ltalÃ¡n bÃ©kÃ©t? Mert nem Ãºgy tÅ±nik. Ursula von der Leyen az itteni beszÃ©deit mindig a hÃ¡borÃºs politikÃ¡nak rendeli alÃ¡. Egy 800 milliÃ¡rd eurÃ³s csomagot akarnak elfogadtatni katonai kiadÃ¡sokra. Hogy erre legyen elÃ©g forrÃ¡s, leÃ©pÃ­tenÃ©k a kohÃ©ziÃ³s forrÃ¡sokat Ã©s az agrÃ¡rkasszÃ¡t. Ãvekkel ezelÅtt ebben a hÃ¡zban, amikor a lÃ¡togatÃ³knak elÅadÃ¡sokat tartottak, mindig elmondtÃ¡k, hogy az eurÃ³pai egyÃ¼ttmÅ±kÃ¶dÃ©s legfontosabb vÃ­vmÃ¡nya a bÃ©ke. Ez Ã­gy is van. De miÃ©rt felejtettÃ©k el?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Adam Bielan, on behalf of the ECR Group. â Mr President, today we must remind ourselves why Europe stood with Ukraine from the first day of Russia's brutal aggression. It was to defend our shared values: sovereignty, freedom and the international order that guarantees peace for all nations, not only the powerful ones.Â Today, the Kremlin hopes that fatigue division and the illusion of stability will paralyse us. We cannot allow that. A fragmented Europe would be the greatest gift to Putin.

Let us state it clearly: so-called peace dictated by Moscow would not be peace at all. It would reward an aggressor, legitimise occupation and betray the Ukrainian people who fight and die for their freedom. A just and durable peace must therefore be concluded on Ukraine's terms, respecting its sovereignty, territorial integrity and the right of its citizens to decide their future.

We will stand with Ukraine until victory. That means military support sufficient to repel aggression, financial support to sustain the state, and a shared plan for reconstruction based on accountability, including the use of Russian frozen assets.

Let us therefore send a clear message before any summit that might happen in the coming weeks. Europe will not surrender to blackmail, will not normalise aggression and will not abandon Ukraine. Only a peace grounded in justice can endure.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Nathalie Loiseau, au nom du groupe Renew. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Donald Trump mÃ©rite bien un prix Nobel, mais un seul, celui de l'inconstance. Nous ne pouvons pas compter sur lui. L'Ukraine ne peut pas compter sur lui. Un jour la Russie est un tigre de papier, un autre il a eu une trÃ¨s bonne conversation avec Vladimir Poutine et veut le rencontrer Ã  Budapest. Et puis il ne veut plus. Mais assez parlÃ© de Donald Trump. Ã attendre de lui qu'il prenne les bonnes dÃ©cisions, nous perdons notre temps. DÃ©cidons par nous-mÃªmes, agissons pour nous-mÃªmes et pour l'Ukraine.

L'Ukraine a besoin d'armes, pas d'embrassades entre Trump, Poutine et OrbÃ¡n. Utilisons les avoirs russes gelÃ©s pour que la Russie paye le prix de sa guerre. L'Ukraine a besoin que nous augmentions la pression sur Moscou et que nous cessions d'importer du gaz russe qui renfloue les caisses du Kremlin. Nous n'avons pas Ã  aller Ã  Budapest. Volodymyr Zelensky non plus, ni aujourd'hui, ni plus tard. Vladimir Poutine ne peut y Ãªtre accueilli car la Hongrie est encore tenue par ses obligations internationales. Et, rappelons-le, la place de Vladimir Poutine est Ã  La Haye, pas Ã  Budapest.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Villy SÃ¸vndal, for Verts/ALE-Gruppen. â Hr. Formand! Krigen, overfaldet pÃ¥ Ukraine, har snart vÃ¦ret i fire Ã¥r. Russerne fortsÃ¦tter deres angreb pÃ¥ civile. Trump nÃ¦vnte inden sit valg for snart et Ã¥r siden, at han kunne afslutte den her krig pÃ¥ 24 timer. Tiden er overskredet, eller som de siger i USA: "TACO: Trump Always Chickens Out". Men desvÃ¦rre betyder det forhold, at han gÃ¸r det â de der totalt omskiftelige meldinger â at han letter presset mod Putin som optakt til det mÃ¸de, der nu lige er blevet aflyst, og bliver ved med at trÃ¦kke tiden ud. For kort tid siden mente han, at Ukraine kunne vinde krigen. I den her uge meldte han sÃ¥ ud, at en vÃ¥benhvile skal finde sted ved kamplinjen. Den slags udtalelser, der hele tiden skifter, er en meget stor forÃ¦ring til Putin, fordi Trump ogsÃ¥ i den her sammenhÃ¦ng er utilregnelig. Krigen i Ukraine har varet alt for lÃ¦nge med utrolige tab af liv, Ã¸delÃ¦ggelser og smerte for den ukrainske befolkning. EU skal gÃ¸re, hvad vi kan for at fÃ¥ krigen afsluttet. Det gÃ¸r vi ved at lÃ¦gge maksimalt pres pÃ¥ Rusland, bÃ¥de politisk og Ã¸konomisk og militÃ¦rt, og aldrig skabe tvivl om vores ubÃ¸jelige stÃ¸tte til Ukraine, samt med et krav om, at de ansvarlige skal stilles til ansvar.

Â Â

Â Â Ãzlem Demirel, im Namen der Fraktion The Left. â Herr PrÃ¤sident! âIch dachte immer, jeder Mensch sei gegen den Krieg, bis ich herausfand, dass es welche gibt, die dafÃ¼r sind, besonders die, die nicht hingehen mÃ¼ssenâ. An dieses Zitat von Remarque musste ich in den letzten Tagen sehr oft denken. Liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, inzwischen sind es nicht mehr wie zu Beginn die USA, die einen Friedensschluss blockieren. Russland schreit: Wir haben den Krieg gewonnen, und die EU erwidert: Wir wollen zwar Frieden, aber erst mÃ¼ssen wir siegen. Was wir gerade erleben, ist schon ein hybrider Krieg, und jeden Tag mehr droht tatsÃ¤chlich ein groÃer heiÃer Krieg, ein groÃer heiÃer Krieg zwischen unterschiedlichen WeltmÃ¤chtenÂ â Ã¶konomischen und MilitÃ¤rmÃ¤chten.

Liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, ich frage mich jeden Tag mehr: Haben Sie die Geschichte dieses Kontinents mit zwei Weltkriegen vergessen? Ich weiÃ, dass Appelle an Ihre Vernunft nicht mehr wirken. Deshalb appelliere ich an die Menschen in Europa. Verweigert Euch dem Krieg, verweigert Euch in Russland, in der Ukraine, verweigert Euch aber auch den Kriegsvorbereitungen der EuropÃ¤ischen Union!

Â Â Pekka Toveri (PPE). â MrÂ President, dear colleagues, the most important lesson from history for us is that you don't stop dictators by appeasing them. Well, we all know that Putin has no interest in making peace in Ukraine, since nobody dares to tell him the truth in Russia, he still believes â wrongly â that he can win the war.

Therefore, he has playtime with President Trump. We saw it in Alaska. We see it clearly now in this very slow preparation for a possible Budapest conference. The panic in the Kremlin when Trump talked about giving Tomahawk cruise missiles to Ukraine shows that only the threat of Western military and economic force will put fear to Putin and force him into negotiations.

We can't trust in President Trump in this crucial issue; he is far too easily fooled by Russians. Therefore, we Europeans need to send a clear message to Putin: regardless of what the USA decides, we will increase sanctions on Russia and increase funding and military support to Ukraine, including capability to long-range strikes. And we need to actually do it, not just give declarations and talk about it. It is our responsibility, and it is in our interests. Europe is safe when Ukraine is safe.

Slava Ukraini!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marina Kaljurand (S&D). â MrÂ President, Commissioner, Council, colleagues, Ukraine is fighting against Russian aggression at the front, and with tens of thousands of civilian casualties, and two kids being killed in Kyiv last night. And Ukraine is under constant pressure to give up land and freedom under the soâcalled 'peace treaty' orchestrated in the Kremlin.

At the same time, the EU supports the Russian criminal regime by buying Russian energy. War criminal Putin fools around and continues killing. President Trump continues changing his positions. That is reality.

We are the last hope of Ukrainians. Together with the 'coalition of the willing' we have to get our act together. We must support Ukraine until full victory over aggressor in order to bring fair and durable peace to Ukraine and Europe. We must give Ukraine much needed military support, fully implement sanctions, stop the Russian shadow fleet, start using Russian frozen assets, stop financing the Russian war economy and bring the aggressor to justice. And we should do it in solidarity.

Thank you,Â Slava Ukraini.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Pierre-Romain Thionnet (PfE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, la rencontre entre Trump et Poutine Ã  Budapest n'aura probablement pas lieu. Ou pas maintenant.

La faute Ã  qui? Aux AmÃ©ricains? Non. Depuis des mois, ils s'Ã©vertuent Ã  Ã©tablir les conditions de nÃ©gociations difficiles entre Russes et Ukrainiens. La faute aux Ukrainiens? Non. Leur rÃ©sistance militaire est la condition mÃªme pour que des nÃ©gociations aient lieu, car leur capitulation acteraitÂ la fin de la guerre, certes, mais certainement pas l'avÃ¨nement de la paix en Europe. La faute aux EuropÃ©ens? Non plus. En dÃ©pit des rÃ©actions affligeantes d'officiels qui imaginent des nÃ©gociations sans partie adverse, on ne peut Ã  la fois dire que l'Europe est spectatrice mais qu'elle est responsable de la prolongation de la guerre.

Non, chers collÃ¨gues, les responsables de l'impasse ne sont ni Ã  Kiev, ni Ã  Bruxelles, ni Ã  Washington. Ils sont Ã  Moscou, au Kremlin, oÃ¹ l'on persiste Ã  poursuivre des objectifs maximalistes qui ont Ã©tÃ© ceux de Poutine depuis le dÃ©but, bien que les moyens d'y parvenir aient pu changer: la soumission totale de l'Ukraine pour anÃ©antir sa souverainetÃ© et son identitÃ©.

Â Â Marie-Agnes Strack-Zimmermann (Renew). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! âGebt nicht auf, Europa!â Das sagen die Ukrainer uns â seit dreieinhalb Jahren an der Front kÃ¤mpfend, ihre verschleppten Kinder suchend, vor den TrÃ¼mmern ihrer HÃ¤user stehend. Sie haben 24Â Stunden lang Angst, aber auch, dass wir unsere Hilfe einstellen und denen auf den Leim gehen, die Putins LÃ¼genlied singen â auch hier in diesem Haus â und aus dem Opfer die TÃ¤ter machen.

Es ist unertrÃ¤glich, dass der ukrainische PrÃ¤sident in der Welt um Hilfe betteln muss. Es mÃ¼sste schlicht selbstverstÃ¤ndlich sein, weil es auch um uns in Europa geht. Ungarn kann den roten Teppich wieder einrollen fÃ¼r den KriegsverbrecherÂ âder wird nÃ¤mlich nicht kommen. Der denkt gar nicht dran, weil er keinen Frieden will. Kaum hat Trump die MarschflugkÃ¶rper wieder eingepackt, ist die Diplomatie in der Tonne. Merkt eigentlich irgendjemand noch was davon? Deswegen: Wir werden so lange die Ukraine unterstÃ¼tzenÂ âÂ wirtschaftlich, humanitÃ¤r und auch militÃ¤rischÂ â, bis Putin endlich verstanden hat, dass seine Rechnung nicht aufgeht. Slawa Ukrajini!

Â Â Alice Kuhnke (Verts/ALE). â Herr talman! Putin har lindat den lika sjÃ¤lvupptagna som opÃ¥litliga Trump runt sitt lillfinger. Och med det hotas inte bara Ukrainas existens utan hela Europas sÃ¤kerhet.

Vi mÃ¥ste bestÃ¤mma oss. Ãr vi beredda att gÃ¶ra vad som krÃ¤vs fÃ¶r att fÃ¶rsvara Ukrainas frihet? Eller tÃ¤nker vi fortsÃ¤tta lÃ¥tsas som om vi gjort allt vi kan? Det Ã¤r nÃ¤mligen inte sant.

Trots att vi miljÃ¶partister redan frÃ¥n dag ett krÃ¤vde ett slut pÃ¥ att gÃ¶da Putins krigskassa genom vÃ¥r import av rysk olja och gas har det drÃ¶jt Ã¤nda tills nu â flera Ã¥r av ryska bomber Ã¶ver Ukraina â fÃ¶r att fÃ¥ med er andra pÃ¥ tÃ¥get.

Och trots att vi miljÃ¶partister krÃ¤ver att vi sÃ¤tter hÃ¥rt mot hÃ¥rt mot de politiska krafter som fÃ¶rrÃ¥der EU genom att flÃ¶rta med Putin och hans vÃ¤n OrbÃ¡n, fortsÃ¤tter hÃ¶gern att om och om igen samarbeta med just dessa partier.

Vi mÃ¥ste gÃ¶ra mer och vi mÃ¥ste gÃ¶ra det nu. Slava Ukraini!

Â Â Jonas SjÃ¶stedt (The Left). â Herr talman! Vi ska vara tacksamma fÃ¶r att det inte blev nÃ¥got toppmÃ¶te fÃ¶r Trump och Putin i Budapest, dÃ¤rfÃ¶r att ingen fÃ¶rhandling om Ukrainas framtid ska ske utan att Ukraina sjÃ¤lvt sitter vid fÃ¶rhandlingsbordet.

Putin har inte rÃ¤tt till nÃ¥gon del av Ukrainas territorium, och Trump har ingen som helst rÃ¤tt att sÃ¤lja ut Ukraina.

I Viktor OrbÃ¡n har Putin en beundrare som hade tagit emot Putin som en statsman och vÃ¤rldsledare. Det fÃ¶rtjÃ¤nar han inte. Putin behÃ¶ver inte besÃ¶ka Budapest. Han behÃ¶ver skickas till Haag som den krigsfÃ¶rbrytare han faktiskt Ã¤r.

Den bÃ¤sta hjÃ¤lp vi kan ge fÃ¶r att fÃ¥ en fred, fÃ¶r att fÃ¥ den vapenvila som Ryssland fÃ¶rvÃ¤grar Ukraina, det Ã¤r att fortsÃ¤tta stÃ¶det: politiskt, ekonomiskt och militÃ¤rt till det kÃ¤mpande Ukraina.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Grzegorz Braun (NI). â Mr President, colleagues, do you really want to go to war? I know you don't. You just like pushing others into a human meat grinder.

Ukraine had its chance in the year 2022, right after the escalation started. They were invited to the table and there was a peace agreement ready. But what happened? The Prime Minister of Great Britain, Mr Johnson, went immediately to Kyiv and the agreement was cancelled. This is the usual game that big guys, big imperialists, play at our Central European playground.

I don't want to be a chess pawn. I don't want my people to be killed and going bankrupt because you like wars going on. Stop subsidising Kyiv's Banderist, Nazi Ukrainian regime. Stop subscribing...

(The President cut off the speaker)

(The speaker declined to take two blue-card questions from Stine Bosse and Sebastian Tynkkynen)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Tobias Cremer (S&D). â MrÂ President, colleagues, this whole thing felt like Groundhog Day. Another phone call between Putin and Trump, another summit, and yet again, much ado about nothing. Just more strikes on Ukraine, more dead civilians, no Russian willingness for peace, no compromise.

Yet even this depressing cycle had a silver lining, and not just that this time our American friends recognised Putin's bad faith before rolling out the red carpet, but that twice now, the threat of tougher sanctions and weapons for Ukraine actually made Putin move. This shows how terrified the Kremlin is vis-Ã -vis a strong and united West. This shows that the 'peace through strength' strategy actually works.

But we can't just rely on an American knight in shining armour to do that work for us, colleagues. No, this is about European security. So, Europe must act by passing the 19th sanction package, by using Russia's frozen assets as a leverage for peace, and by putting out a plan, together with Ukraine, for a European just and lasting peace, because Ukraine cannot afford another Groundhog Day. And seriously, neither can we.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

PREDSEDÃ: MARTIN HOJSÃK
Podpredseda

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â GyÃ¶rgy HÃ¶lvÃ©nyi (PfE). â ElnÃ¶k Ãr! TÃ©nyleg azt kell mondjam, hogy ne szÃ³rakozzunk! HÃ¡t itt milliÃ³k haltak meg. MÃ¡sfÃ©l milliÃ³ halottrÃ³l beszÃ©lgetÃ¼nk itt, elÃ©g komfortos kÃ¶rnyezetben. CsalÃ¡dok ezrei hullottak szÃ©t, a vÃ¡rosok vÃ¡ltak teljes romhalmazzÃ¡. Â Â Stine Bosse (Renew), blue-card question. â Thank you for taking the blue card. Just two questions:

Who, please, started this war? And are you going to call Putin together with the far left? Have you got his phone number? And thirdly, who are you then going to call when you've got Russian soldiers and troops in your backyard?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â GyÃ¶rgy HÃ¶lvÃ©nyi (PfE), blue-card answer. â That seems to be a relevant question, but, you know, the problem is that you didn't listen to what I told you.

The only question is how can we save the lives of thousands and thousands of people? Of course, it's a negotiation. It's a balance. It's compromises. And, of course, I don't say to Mr Putin, 'I can't say "No"'.

But the problem is there: whether Europe is a player of the peace-making process or not.

(In response to an off-mic comment from another Member) OK, a blue card is a blue card, I have to give an answer. Thanks a lot.

The main issue in this question is whether we have somebody who is able to create the peace or not. This is the question. And we would like peace and not war.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Petras AuÅ¡treviÄius (Renew). â MrÂ President, Commissioner, Council representative, colleagues, the de facto leader of the terrorist Russian state, Vladimir Putin, has stated that wherever a Russian soldier sets foot, it becomes Russian territory. Indeed, Russian imperialism has become the enemy of civilised Europe.

Russia continues to demand Ukrainian capitulation as a condition for any negotiations. For years, we have witnessed the occupation of Ukrainian territories and war crimes, including referendums held in temporarily occupied territories, followed by their illegal incorporation into the Russian constitution. This is Russian â or more precisely Soviet â logic and plan for the subjugation of neighbouring nations.

We must do everything we can to ensure that President Zelenskyy's peace plan is not dismissed, and that the 'rule of the strong' is not imposed again. The outcome of this war determines Europe's future security architecture. Europe must play a full role in peace negotiations on Ukraine because it's our common future that is being decided right now.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Elisabetta Gualmini (S&D). â Mr President, we all know very well what is really at stake in the Russia-Ukraine war: the defence of democracy against tyranny; the defence of pluralism against dictatorship; the defence of freedom against despotic leaders who really do not care about international law. So Putin's real target is not just the Ukraine or its critical materials, but Europe and the West: our values, our freedom, our unity.

So we need the two very clear conditions for the European Council of tomorrow. First, military support by the EU and, at the same time, a solid peace proposal, including ceasefire, restitution of deported Ukrainian children to their families, and so on and so forth. The second condition is that Ukraine must be put in a position of strength in order to negotiate a peace. You cannot surrender to the strongest one. You cannot surrender to tackle Trump's crazy diktats. So, President Zelenskyy, your fight is our fight.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ãzlem Demirel (The Left), Frage nach dem Verfahren der âblauen Karteâ. â Ich danke Ihnen, dass Sie die Zwischenfrage erlauben. Ich hÃ¤tte zwei Fragen an Sie, weil Sie ja immer wieder diese Geschichte erzÃ¤hlen, dass Russland morgen bei uns in Europa einfallen wÃ¼rde. Wie erklÃ¤ren Sie sich die Tatsache, dass Russland seit drei Jahren mit der wesentlich schwÃ¤cheren Ukraine Krieg fÃ¼hrt und diesen Krieg nicht gewinnt? Sie sagen mir, die Ukraine kann diesen Krieg gewinnen. Gleichzeitig sagen Sie, Russland kann die EU angreifen, NATO-Staaten angreifen, die wesentlich stÃ¤rker ausgestattet sind, militÃ¤risch, vom Equipment, von den Ausgaben her. Wie passt Ihre ErzÃ¤hlung eigentlich zusammen? Oder versuchen Sie, uns alle eigentlich fÃ¼r dumm zu verkaufen?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Elisabetta Gualmini (S&D), blue-card answer. â No, no, we do not believe that you are crazy people, absolutely. But these are two questions. So first of all, it is absolutely true that Putin does not want peace, and we are in the middle of a hybrid war all over Europe. We can see it every day in Denmark, Sweden, Germany and so on and so forth. Second, of course Putin is not winning, because there is an incredible resistance by heroes, people fighting for their liberty and freedom thanks to the help of the European Union, thank God.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â AndrÃ¡s LÃ¡szlÃ³ (PfE). â MrÂ President,Â where are the European peacemakers? Where are the leaders with the vision, with the courage, with the strength to restore peace in Europe? Where are they? The leaders of great European powers are weaker than ever before. Leaders of France, Germany and the United Kingdom have never been so unpopular. Or does anybody really think that Ursula von der Leyen could negotiate a lasting peace in Europe? I don't think so.

It was more than three and a half years ago that Ursula von der Leyen and Joe Biden promised Ukraine victory, and they also promised that sanctions wouldn't impact our economies. How embarrassing. Or perhaps Kaja Kallas â does anyone think she would negotiate a lasting peace? We have recently learned she doesn't even know who won WorldÂ WarÂ II. Thank God, we don't really have a European foreign minister.

We have now adopted almost 20 sanctions packages, and the war is still raging. Since 2023, hundreds of thousands have died without a major military breakthrough. And yet, all we hear is 'more weapons', 'more money' and 'more sanctions'.

Elites in Brussels mock and sabotage peace efforts. However, our most important ally, the United States, is finally pursuing peace negotiations. So is Prime Minister OrbÃ¡n.

But the majority of the European Parliament refuses diplomacy. You have failed Europeans miserably, and eventually others will decide about the future of our continent. Europeans, Ukrainians and Russians are all losers of this war; the sooner we end it, the better.

We need to make Europe great again. Let's start by restoring peace in Europe.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lucia Yar (Renew). â MrÂ President, Vladimir Putin has no right to be in Ukraine, and he has certainly no business to be in Budapest. He is a war criminal. Period. He belongs before an international tribunal, colleagues.

Those that are already trembling with excitement roll out the red carpet for him, right? Many of you, MrÂ OrbÃ¡n, MrÂ Fico, they are not defending peace. Because â let me speak also for millions from central Europe â Fico's and OrbÃ¡n's courting to Putin do not represent us.

And to those that are calling us warmongers, are you serious? Putin refuses to stop bombing civilians â last night, a kindergarten. He demands that territory be handed over on his terms and then he puts words into the mouth of his apologists, daring to say that Zelenskyy, that EU, that us â that we are against the peace. Are you kidding me? Wake up. The only place Vladimir Putin belongs is The Hague.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Rasa JukneviÄienÄ (PPE). â MrÂ President, this war is not only about Ukraine, but also about us. It is an existential war for all of Europe. Russkiy Mir â Russian World â ideology is a form of fascism, the same evil this continent has seen before.

Wars are won first in people's minds, and we must win this war. We must stop trembling at every word coming from Washington, DC. One day Americans will realise that Putin is simply mocking their president.

Europe is on the right path, but we need speed. Putin fears that his friend OrbÃ¡n will lose, so he has turned to meet Trump in Budapest. This is an attempt to kill several birds with one stone: to humiliate Ukraine, to help OrbÃ¡n and to further undermine EU unity.

We must not give in. We must defend a free Europe.

Â

Â Â RaphaÃ«l Glucksmann (S&D). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, chers collÃ¨gues, ce que M.Â Trump, M.Â OrbÃ¡n et les relais de leurs gouvernements dans ces bancs appellent la paix, ce n'est que la capitulation. Quand vous cÃ©dez au tyran qui dÃ©clenche les guerres, vous n'apportez pas la paix, vous ne faites qu'attiser son appÃ©tit et vous ne faites que hÃ¢ter la prochaine agression. Mais il n'y a rien Ã  attendre de vous.

Par contre, il y a Ã  attendre beaucoup plus des dirigeants europÃ©ens. Parce que oui, ils ont raison de s'inquiÃ©ter de DonaldÂ Trump, de ViktorÂ OrbÃ¡n. Mais que font-ils, eux? Â Â OndÅej Knotek (PfE). â MrÂ President, many of you, colleagues, accent the need for united support of Ukraine, and I generally agree, but I believe it is much more important to provide smart support of Ukraine.

Last week, the ITRE Committee voted for the ban of Russian oil, oil products and natural gas from Russia, starting January 2026. But products derived from natural gas are allowed, for example, fertilisers. So buying Russian fertilisers is okay for the European Parliament. At the same time, the ban on Russian oil without exception for Slovakia and Hungary also means that Ukraine will not be able to sell own-harvested oil to refineries in the country, and now most in the Czech Republic. This example shows that maybe less activism and more realism would be much more beneficial, not only to the peace in Ukraine.

And allow me one sentence: some of you are so desperate about the fact that Trump has decided for Budapest, that it really shows that you do not have the situation under control.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Stine Bosse (Renew), blue-card question. â Thank you for taking the blue card. I just want to ask you a couple of questions.

One question, would you support Ukraine to become, after peace, part of the European Union?

And do you know, probably before you were born or close to when you were born, when Hungary joined the European Union? In my country, a lot of people were worried and thought that they were going to lose on agriculture and other areas, but we were a lot of people who wanted Hungary to be exactly part of Europe. Did you know that?

Â Â President. â Regarding another blue card, I will offer the blue card only when you raise the blue card during the initial speech of the Member. When you raise a blue card while the blue card is in process, I'm not going to be taking it.

Â Â President. â This was not a blue card; it was a complaint. You can file a complaint about the way I'm chairing.

I accepted now your blue card. Your previous blue card was registered by the system. So when you pressed the button while another blue card was already going on, you didn't ask for the blue card during the speech of the Member. Otherwise, I would take it.

So please, and as I explained before â you don't have to react this way â ask for the blue card during the speech.

And I'm sorry, Madam, this was not a blue card, this was a complaint on the way I'm chairing.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Riho Terras (PPE). â Mr President, dear colleagues, today we have no idea if, and when, the meeting between President Trump and the leader of the Russian regime will take place. But there is no doubt that neither Europe nor Ukraine needs such a meeting. The possibility to hold this meeting on the territory of a European Union Member State is yet another example of how Mr OrbÃ¡n continues to ignore the sanctions imposed on the aggressor.

Yesterday, we again discussed the frozen Russian assets. We need to stop discussing â it is vital to get the reparation loan mechanism up and running as soon as possible, to demonstrate Europe's continued support for Ukraine. We must make it clear that Russia has to pay the bill of the war it started.

Slava Ukraini!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Salvatore De Meo (PPE). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, ogni volta che sosteniamo Kiev riaffermiamo un principio fondamentale, ossia: nessuna potenza puÃ² riscrivere i confini con la forza o minare l'ordine internazionale fondato sul diritto.

L'attacco della Russia non Ã¨ una crisi regionale, ma un'aggressione all'intera architettura di sicurezza europea e globale. Per questo non possiamo piÃ¹ permetterci ambiguitÃ  e rinvii: sostenere l'Ucraina significa difendere l'Europa, la libertÃ  e la democrazia. La pace non puÃ² essere imposta dal piÃ¹ forte ma costruita attraverso un accordo equo e sostenibile che rispetti la sovranitÃ  dell'Ucraina e garantisca stabilitÃ  nell'intera regione.

In questo quadro, accogliamo con favore gli sforzi diplomatici del Presidente Trump per favorire una stabilitÃ  duratura in Medio Oriente, ma auspichiamo lo stesso impegno per una soluzione giusta e duratura in Ucraina con il coinvolgimento dell'Europa, perchÃ© difendere l'Ucraina significa difendere la nostra democrazia, la nostra sicurezza e il futuro stesso dell'Europa.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Mika Aaltola (PPE). â Arvoisa puhemies, hyvÃ¤t kollegat, VenÃ¤jÃ¤ toistaa vanhaa nÃ¤ytelmÃ¤Ã¤nsÃ¤, vaatimuksia juurisyistÃ¤, etupiiristÃ¤, yrityksiÃ¤ jakaa Ukraina, VenÃ¤jÃ¤n kuihtuva imperiumi, joka takertuu epÃ¤toivoisesti laajemmuushakuiseen menneisyyteensÃ¤. Putinin armeija vuotaa verta. Se uhraa kyynisesti vÃ¤hemmistÃ¶alueiden nuoria pelkkinÃ¤ kertakÃ¤yttÃ¶isinÃ¤ sotilaina, jotta yskivÃ¤ sotakone pysyisi pystyssÃ¤. Rintama ei liiku ja on syvÃ¤llÃ¤ VenÃ¤jÃ¤llÃ¤. SiellÃ¤ rÃ¤jÃ¤htelee. VenÃ¤jÃ¤n tÃ¤mÃ¤n pÃ¤ivÃ¤n ydinaseuhkailu on epÃ¤toivoisen vallan viimeisiÃ¤ temppuja. Todellinen rauha ei synny alistumisesta imperialismille, vaan lujasta tuesta Ukrainalle. VenÃ¤jÃ¤n ydin on jo murtumassa, kun Putin joutuu heiluttamaan ydinasekorttiaan. Valta on murenemassa Putinin jalkojen alta. Siksi meidÃ¤n on huolehdittava, ettÃ¤ sen epÃ¤toivoiset viimeiset teot eivÃ¤t roisku rajojemme ylitse.

Â Â Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). â MrÂ President, this conversation has been crazy. MrÂ Braun â or should we call you MrÂ Bean â you're kind of becoming like a joke in this House. Let me explain:

Te sanoitte, ettÃ¤ on Ukrainan vika, ettei vielÃ¤kÃ¤Ã¤n ole rauhaa. Te sanoitte VenÃ¤jÃ¤n hyÃ¶kÃ¤ttyÃ¤ Ukrainaan, kun sieltÃ¤ oli otettu alueita, ettÃ¤ olisi ollut valmis rauhansopimus pÃ¶ydÃ¤ssÃ¤, kunhan he olisivat vain allekirjoittaneet. HyvÃ¤ksyisittekÃ¶ te Mr Braun, ettÃ¤ jos Puolaan vieras valta tulisi ottamaan alueita haltuun, teille annettaisiin paperi kÃ¤teen, te vaan kirjoittaisitte sen? TÃ¤mÃ¤n kannan edustajille meillÃ¤ Suomessa on ihan oma nimi, kun oman maansa menee pettÃ¤mÃ¤Ã¤n. Ostimme juuri puolisoni kanssa oman talon, jossa on tontti, jossa on rajat. Jos naapurini tulee sisÃ¤lle ja sanoo, ettÃ¤ nyt vedetÃ¤Ã¤n uudet rajat, tÃ¤ssÃ¤ paperi allekirjoita, minÃ¤ soitan poliisit.

Â Â

Â Â Michael McNamara (Renew). â MrÂ President, Commissioner, Minister, on Friday, it will be three years and eight months since Putin's illegal and immoral invasion of Ukraine. It is an act that is to be entirely condemned without any reservation. But equally, it is now three years since I was told by an Irish Army officer in the Irish Parliament that the defeat of the conventional Russian forces was nigh. That hasn't happened. I wish it maybe did, but I have to live in the real world. It's not happening.

Yet, we are told that, contrary to what some believe in the House, we are not seizing Russian assets. Instead, we are making a loan of EURÂ 140 billion of European taxpayers' money, to be securitised with that money, which will be confiscated when Russia loses. But they're not losing â there is the problem.Â So it's time for plan B, it is time for negotiation. I am glad, Commissioner, that you have said that you support President Trump's efforts because it is not at all clear that your colleagues in the Commission do.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Od politikÅ¯, aÅ¥ jiÅ¾ nÃ¡rodnÃ­ch nebo evropskÃ½ch, bych vÅ¡ak Äekala, Å¾e budou stÃ¡t na stranÄ svÃ½ch obÄanÅ¯, neÅ¾ se snaÅ¾it maximalizovat zisky zbrojaÅÅ¯ i pÅes riziko eskalace konfliktu. (Koniec vystÃºpenÃ­ na zÃ¡klade prihlÃ¡senia sa o slovo zdvihnutÃ­m ruky)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jessika Roswall, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, thank you for the debate. It is important and it is encouraging to know that the majority of this House stands with Ukraine.

We also hear the request from you that the EU do more. Loud and clear. We all recognise that the EU and our Member States have done a lot, but I agree, there is still much more to be done. That is a clear task for the European Commission, and I can reassure you that we are working on new ways forward. We are doing this not only for Ukraine but also for our Union's very future.

Ukraine must succeed in its fight for sovereignty and territorial integrity. Ukraine's victory is the EU's best guarantee for our own security. We will continue to stand against Russia's aggression, including through the 19th sanctions package. We will continue to provide financial and military assistance.

A strong Ukraine at the negotiation table, capable of finally ending the war on its terms, remains our primary goal. And despite the difficulties caused by the ongoing war, we will keep up our support to Ukraine's efforts to come closer to the EU, where Ukraine rightly belongs.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, Commissioner, thank you for â I have to say â a quite interesting debate.

I took note of a wide range of opinions, from the extreme point of view that we, or Ukraine, should be responsible for the war or even continuing the war, or that our support could have been better used on our schools and hospitals â there would be no schools or hospitals to invest in if we are not capable of protecting our territory, our continent.

These points of view are absurd, and there are â thankfully â only very few of you. The vast majority of you know that Putin is the aggressor, that a peace agreement must be made with Ukraine, and that the only way to peace is a militarily strong Europe and a militarily strong Ukraine.

I want to sincerely thank the European Parliament for the very, very broad support and solidarity with the Ukrainian people. Together, we can show unity and commitment â this is important for Ukraine and it is important for Europe â and we will continue our effort together.

Ukraine needs our strongest support to reach a just and lasting peace, a peace based on Ukraine's terms.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â PredsedajÃºci . â Äakujem, pani ministerka. TÃ½mto sa rozprava k tomuto bodu skonÄila. NasledujÃº vyhlÃ¡senia Rady a Komisie PrvÃ© vÃ½roÄie povodnÃ­ DANA v Å panielsku: zlepÅ¡enie pripravenosti EurÃ³pskej Ãºnie. Na Ãºvod poprosÃ­m znova za Radu najprv pani ministerku Bjerre.

Â 

17. First anniversary of the DANA floods in Spain: improving EU preparedness (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â PredsedajÃºci . â Äakujem, pani ministerka. TÃ½mto sa rozprava k tomuto bodu skonÄila. NasledujÃº vyhlÃ¡senia Rady a Komisie PrvÃ© vÃ½roÄie povodnÃ­ DANA v Å panielsku: zlepÅ¡enie pripravenosti EurÃ³pskej Ãºnie. Na Ãºvod poprosÃ­m znova za Radu najprv pani ministerku Bjerre.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â Mr President, honourable Members, Commissioner, one year ago, devastating floods hit Andalusia, Valencia and other regions in Spain. Tragically, more than 230 lives were lost. Our thoughts are with the families of the victims and with the communities who suffered, and continue to suffer, from the effects. In addition to the loss of life, the floods did devastating damage to property and the environment. They also had a strong impact on agriculture and livestock sectors that are so important in the region, and they destroyed infrastructure, including transport and energy services.

The EU and its Member States showed solidarity and unity in the face of the disaster. They responded quickly with all the means at their disposal. As in previous natural disasters, the Union Civil Protection Mechanism, in particular, proved that it can deliver, and the EU's Copernicus Emergency Management Service was also activated to support relief efforts on the ground with satellite maps. The EU also gave strong financial support using the European Union Solidarity Fund as early as March this year. And I wish to stress that very soon, on 13 November, the Council will approve the mobilisation of an extra amount of EURÂ 945Â million from the fund to support the recovery of infrastructure and the repair of damages caused by the flooding.

Europe is the fastest heating continent and is more and more exposed to the consequences of climate change. Extreme weather now affects Europe more often. Last month, we had a debate on this topic in this Chamber. Back then, I said that the EU needs to be better prepared to handle these crises by moving from a reactive approach to a preparedness-by-design approach. I wish to repeat that today. We must continue to quickly handle climate change on two fronts: mitigation and adaptation. The EU has a strong track record in both areas, with ambitious policies to reduce its greenhouse emissions, prevent and reduce climate-related risk. We especially must ensure that Europe becomes more resilient against climate-related water extremes like floods.

On 21 October, the Council approved conclusions on water resilience. The Council stressed the need to strengthen water infrastructure, security and real-time early warning systems for floods. Also, the Council underlined the importance of cooperation and coordination between borders. We must also strengthen the EU's preparedness, resilience and capacity to respond to crises and disasters, to tackle the changing landscape of risks and threats, such as extreme weather. These are priorities for the Danish Presidency. This is why we aim to make progress with the legislative proposal to revise the Union Civil Protection Mechanism.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jessika Roswall, Member of the Commission. â Mr President, Minister, honourable Members, today marks one year since one of Europe's most tragic floods. We honour all the victims and their families. I want to express my deepest sympathy and solidarity with all those affected by this disaster.

At the same time, I would like to pay tribute to all those who stepped up to help during and after the floods.

In the early hours of 29 October 2024, a cold drop hit eastern Spain. Torrential rains poured down more than 700mm in just 12 hours. Rivers burst their banks across the Mediterranean coast and flash floods swept through Valencia. It was an unprecedented disaster, and all the data tells us events like this will happen more often.

Like many of you, we were deeply shocked by the images. People being rescued from rooftops, cars piled up, roads buried in mud. Whole communities devastated right here in Europe.

Honourable colleagues, from the very start, the European Union stood in full solidarity with Spain, its authorities and all those affected. We helped support relief efforts and speed up recovery in the affected areas. The Emergency Response Coordination Centre, 24/7 heart of the EU Civil Protection Mechanism, stayed in constant contact with Spanish civil protection teams ready to assist.

On 29 October 2024, Spain activated the Copernicus Rapid Mapping Service to assess the flood and the damages. A few days later, on 8 November, Spain requested help through the EU Civil Protection Mechanism. Several Member States offered assistance. Machinery and vehicles were quickly sent to the scene, and two EU liaison officers were deployed to help coordinate operations on the ground. This was European solidarity in action.

Just a few weeks ago on 6 October 2025, early warning systems again signalled heavy rain in Valencia. Spain, and the ERCC were again immediately in contact in full preparedness mode to assess the situation and discuss possible measures. That is what seamless coordination between our civil protection authorities looks like. It also shows why civil protection is a cornerstone of Europe's security and essential to protecting our people and our communities.

But with this sad anniversary is also a reminder, we must be better prepared. We must anticipate these events, prepare together and support each other when disaster strikes. And that is why the Commission has adopted the Preparedness Union strategy, and we are already putting the actions into practice. This is the EU's first comprehensive preparedness strategy across all policies, all levels of government and all parts of society. It is about changing the way we work and adopting a true mindset of preparedness.

The strategy takes an all hazard approach, covering every kind of natural or manmade risk, from floods to cascading effects. It brings together all relevant policies and actors under one common goal, a whole of government and a whole of society effort. Floods are a clear example. EU civil protection support is becomingÂ even more important.

But response alone is no longer enough. And that is why many of the key actions in this strategy focus directly on flood preparedness and on tackling environmental risks before they escalate.

Here are five key areas. First, preparedness by design. From now on, preparedness will be built in all EU laws, policies and programmes, not added as an afterthought. This must go hand in hand with flexible financial framework as proposed under the new Multiannual Financial Framework. For floods, this means investing in better preparedness through planning, environmental protection, smarter agriculture, cohesion, funding and public awareness. Only by connecting these areas can we build a truly integrated approach to flood preparedness.

Second, stronger foresight and preparedness. By combining solid national systems with closer European cooperation we can better protect our people and our ecosystem from ever more frequent and intense disasters.

Third, strengthening our early warning systems, we will strengthen the use of Copernicus to track natural hazards, including floods. And together with Member States, we will develop guidelines to help communicate with the public before and during grave crisis.

Fourth, raising public awareness. Too often people simply do not know how to prepare. That is why the strategy includes concrete measures to boost populations' preparedness, including guidelines for Member States to ensure citizens can be self-sufficient for at least 72 hours.

And fifth, acting earlier, faster and better together. The Commission will create a new EU crisis coordination hub, building on the expertise of our Emergency Response Coordination Centre to improve coordination when crises hit and manage their cross-sectoral impacts. We will also step up cooperation with science and the private sector to develop innovative solutions that help us to prevent, prepare for and respond to the growing risk of flooding floodings.

Honourable Members, our Water Resilience Strategy, adopted by the Commission in June 2025, is one of the key actions under the Preparedness Union Strategy. It calls for the building of resilience of floods, droughts and also other extreme weathers proactively and in solidarity across borders. The goal is simple to help Member States better identify, risk and take prevention actions by protecting and restoring ecosystems. It will use nature-based solutions to slow water flows and store it in our soil and our forests. This strategy also ensures proper funding for both green and grey infrastructure, for smarter water management and stronger flood protection.

The Commission is also supporting flood risk prevention and preparedness through the Cohesion Policy Fund. Already in the 2021 to 2027 period, EUR 7 billion of EU funds are available for Member States to invest in the prevention and management of floods. Moreover, cohesion policy funds can now quickly and flexibly be mobilised to address post-disaster recovery and enhance disaster resilience through the Restore initiative, which is already supporting the reconstruction after the DANA floods in Valencia.

Additionally, in the ongoing mid-term review of the cohesion policy, Member states are encouraged to increase the allocation in flood-risk management and climate adaptation. Redirecting resources to these actions provides greater flexibility and incentives to the authorities to accelerate implementation of prevention and preparedness projects on the ground, and we will reinforce the European solidarity to help Member States recover from water related disasters.

At the start of this month. On 3 October, the Commission proposed to mobilise EUR 946 million from the European Solidarity Fund to help Spain recover from the devastating DANA floods. This comes after the EUR 100 million already paid in advance to help kick start recovery efforts. This helps restoring infrastructure, providing temporary accommodation, funding rescue services and cleaning up and secure, preventing infrastructure and protecting cultural heritage. And yesterday, the commission mobilised EUR 166 million from the European Social Fund Plus to support self-employed workers who lost their businesses, provide training for unemployed and young people and strengthening counselling support for students in the affected areas.

Beyond these strategies, the Commission has also presented a proposal to upgrade the Union's civil protection mechanism. This mechanism, delivered during DANA just as it did during this summer, wildfires during COVID, and it continues to do so in supporting those affected by Russia's war against Ukraine.

The proposed revision builds on the lessons learned from DANA. It is ambitious but realistic, designed to strengthen Europe's response capacity, improve anticipation and promote a more integrated, cross-sectoral approach. It will help Member States to respond faster in crisis and involve all actors who can add value, including when needed, the military.

The rapporteurs were appointed this month, and I count on the European Parliament to move swiftly on this important file.

So, honourable Members, the losses caused by DANA remind us that we must go further and ensure that next time disaster strikes, Europe is better prepared. We owe it to the victims, to their families, and to all of those that risk their lives in every disaster to build a Europe that is ready, resilient and united.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Esteban GonzÃ¡lez Pons, en nombre del Grupo PPE. â SeÃ±or presidente, ayer, un aÃ±o despuÃ©s, todavÃ­a se rescatÃ³ del barro un posible cadÃ¡ver de una vÃ­ctima. La inundaciÃ³n que sufrimos fue una desgracia tan fieramente inhumana que no admite comparaciÃ³n histÃ³rica. Expreso mi profundo respeto y solidaridad a los familiares de vÃ­ctimas mortales.

Como ya dije en la ComisiÃ³n de Peticiones, la condiciÃ³n digna de las vÃ­ctimas sobrepasa cualquier consideraciÃ³n polÃ­tica y convierte en un imperativo moral compartir su exigencia de memoria y reparaciÃ³n.

El Estado espaÃ±ol, tomado en su acepciÃ³n extensa, fallÃ³ en la provincia de Valencia. No puede explicarse, de otra forma, que no se hubieran hecho las obras de encauzamiento del barranco, que no se acertase con la predicciÃ³n de la lluvia o que la comunicaciÃ³n entre instituciones fuese un telÃ©fono roto.

El lema Â«Solo el pueblo salva al puebloÂ», con el que miles de voluntarios acudieron en masa a atender a las personas atrapadas por la devastaciÃ³n, es suficientemente expresivo de este fracaso generalizado de las instituciones que, segÃºn el contrato social, existen para proteger a los ciudadanos.

Por eso, politizar el sufrimiento, querer sacar ventaja polÃ­tica de la desdicha o convertir los debates en un echarnos la culpa los unos a los otros no nos devuelve ningÃºn liderazgo ejemplar, sino al contrario: ahonda en el naufragio institucional y en la desafecciÃ³n ciudadana. Las culpabilidades, si las hubiera, se depurarÃ¡n en el procedimiento penal en curso y, ademÃ¡s, la Generalitat Valenciana ha presentado un plan de recuperaciÃ³n y su obligaciÃ³n es llevarlo a cabo.

Con humildad, aprendamos la lecciÃ³n y hagamos lo posible para que un desastre asÃ­ no vuelva a repetirse, porque si hoy lloviera otra vez como hace un aÃ±o nos encontrarÃ­amos en las mismas: ninguna infraestructura, ninguna alarma ni ningÃºn mecanismo de predicciÃ³n han sido mejorados desde aquella maldita noche.

Los paÃ­ses suelen salir mÃ¡s unidos de las catÃ¡strofes. No seamos los espaÃ±oles los Ãºnicos que, otra vez, las aprovechamos para separarnos mÃ¡s.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Leire PajÃ­n, en nombre del Grupo S&D. â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±ora comisaria, queremos que nuestras primeras palabras como Grupo sean para las vÃ­ctimas âdoscientas treinta y cinco personas, doscientas veintinueve en nuestra regiÃ³n, en Valenciaâ, para quienes hace un aÃ±o vieron morir a sus seres queridos y vieron arrasadas sus casas, sus vidas y sus recuerdos para siempre. Ellas estÃ¡n, siempre, en el centro, por encima de todo. Es ante ellas ante las que tenemos que comprometernos una vez mÃ¡s.

Comprometernos, seÃ±orÃ­as, a conocer con precisiÃ³n y con rigor lo que pasÃ³, quÃ© fallÃ³ y quiÃ©n fallÃ³ aquella tarde. A depurar responsabilidades y, sobre todo, a comprometernos a que esto no vuelva a pasar. Quienes no estuvieron donde debÃ­an deben pedir perdÃ³n y deben comprometerse a reparar ese dolor.

Porque la emergencia climÃ¡tica seguirÃ¡ provocando catÃ¡strofes cada vez mÃ¡s. Volveremos a tener danas e incendios virulentos y devastadores como los que hemos vivido en LeÃ³n, en Galicia y en tantos otros paÃ­ses de la UniÃ³n Europea. Volveremos a vivir pandemias y crisis cada vez mÃ¡s complejas. Por eso debemos trabajar sin descanso aquÃ­ para prepararnos mejor, para tener sistemas que permitan alertas claras y rÃ¡pidas, para garantizar que nuestra poblaciÃ³n sabe lo que debe hacer y cuÃ¡ndo debe hacerlo. Reconocer a nuestros profesionales, que estos dÃ­as vuelven a movilizarse, con los recursos suficientes y, sobre todo, no ceder ante quienes exigen el desmantelamiento de unidades de emergencia, como pasÃ³ en Valencia, y nos costÃ³ dolorosamente muy caro. La mejora del Mecanismo de ProtecciÃ³n Civil nos va a permitir en esta casa reforzar la solidaridad de la UniÃ³n Europea y es una buena oportunidad para mejorar todo esto.

AllÃ­ volveremos a escuchar a las vÃ­ctimas porque son a ellas a quienes debemos escuchar y no permitir que dirijan la polÃ­tica quienes niegan el cambio climÃ¡tico, quienes exigen el desmantelamiento de lo pÃºblico. Porque que no lo hagan serÃ¡ el mejor homenaje que hoy podamos hacerle a las vÃ­ctimas.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jorge BuxadÃ© Villalba, en nombre del Grupo PfE. â SeÃ±or presidente, LucÃ­a vive en Paiporta. LucÃ­a tiene un bebÃ© de dos meses. LucÃ­a vive en un cuarto piso sin ascensor. PasÃ³ su embarazo subiendo y bajando escaleras porque el fango destrozÃ³ el ascensor. Y un aÃ±o despuÃ©s sigue igual. Su marido ha vuelto a trabajar y la mayor parte del tiempo LucÃ­a lo tiene que pasar en casa porque bajar con el bebÃ© en brazos y el carrito resulta bastante complicado. EstÃ¡ atrapada en su propia casa porque SÃ¡nchez, ese sujeto sin escrÃºpulos, no ha desbloqueado las ayudas. Y LucÃ­a no es una excepciÃ³n. Hay miles de valencianos desamparados. Hoy mismo ha aparecido el cuerpo sin vida de Javier en el fango.

Un aÃ±o mÃ¡s tarde, no tenemos dudas. Las inundaciones del 29 de octubre en Valencia lo que demuestran es el perfil clÃ­nico de SÃ¡nchez, un sociÃ³pata armado y peligroso. Todos sabemos que la limpieza de cauces y la ejecuciÃ³n en tiempo de las obras en la rambla del Poyo y su conexiÃ³n con el nuevo cauce del Turia hubieran reducido las consecuencias devastadoras. Todos sabemos que el Gobierno debiÃ³ declarar la emergencia de interÃ©s nacional y no lo hizo pensando que con ello tumbaba un gobierno regional. No activÃ³ a tiempo al EjÃ©rcito ni la ayuda internacional y dejÃ³ que decenas de valencianos murieran en coches y pisos.

FallÃ³ el Estado, pero se alzÃ³ la naciÃ³n en un movimiento de solidaridad nacional inigualable. MÃ¡s de treinta y seis mil familias siguen esperando las ayudas del Gobierno de SÃ¡nchez. Valencia a duras penas ha vuelto a la normalidad. Centros de salud, colegios, depuradoras y colectores, carreteras provinciales, accesos a los polÃ­gonos industriales prÃ¡cticamente recuperados solo por el esfuerzo de los valencianos, porque se aprobÃ³ un presupuesto extraordinario para la recuperaciÃ³n gracias a nuestro partido.

A pesar de SÃ¡nchez, el pueblo salva al pueblo.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Diego Solier, en nombre del Grupo ECR. â SeÃ±or presidente, yo soy valenciano âestuve allÃ­ y estoy todas las semanas en mi tierraâ y lo que mÃ¡s nos duele no es solo lo que pasÃ³ el 29 de octubre, sino todo lo que no ha ocurrido desde entonces.

Estamos cansados; cansados de instituciones pÃºblicas incapaces de coordinarse, de promesas que no se cumplen y de ayudas que no llegan. Y, sobre todo, de una falta de transparencia absoluta.

Nadie ha explicado con claridad quÃ© pasÃ³ ese dÃ­a. Â¿Llegaron las alertas de los caudales? Â¿Por quÃ© no existe un listado pÃºblico de fallecidos? Â¿Por quÃ© la empresa Tragsa no dejÃ³ limpiar bien el alcantarillado a las empresas especializadas que estaban allÃ­? Â¿Por quÃ©, una vez mÃ¡s, nadie asume una responsabilidad?

Mientras tanto, las administraciones siguen cobrando impuestos a familias que ni siquiera han podido volver a sus hogares. Hay edificios sin ascensor, garajes y trasteros inservibles, negocios que no han vuelto a abrir, y cada vez que hay una alerta por lluvia la gente vuelve a revivir el miedo, la regiÃ³n entra en parÃ¡lisis y es un trauma real que sigue existiendo. Y no nos hablen de ayudas cuando ha sido la sociedad civil la que ha sostenido a los afectados.

Por eso, desde este Parlamento pedimos tres cosas: verdad, para saber quÃ© fallÃ³; justicia, para que las ayudas lleguen ya, y responsabilidad, para que esto no vuelva a ocurrir.

Hago un llamamiento a la sociedad: no normalicemos que una tragedia pase y nadie pague el precio polÃ­tico. Es hora de exigir una cultura institucional nueva, transparente, eficaz y humana. Porque cuando las instituciones fallan, la gente sufre las consecuencias.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â GrÃ©gory Allione, au nom du groupe Renew. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, mes chers collÃ¨gues, il y a quelques mois, nous parlions d'une Europe qui se dÃ©shydratait. DorÃ©navant, nous parlons d'une Europe qui, finalement, est sous les eaux. En ce moment mÃªme, en France, c'est une tempÃªte qui va frapper mon pays avec de nombreuses vigilances. C'est cela le dÃ©rÃ¨glement climatique. Une Europe qui brÃ»le et enfin une Europe qui se fait submerger quelques semaines aprÃ¨s. L'Espagne en a payÃ© le prix fort. Et oui, il faut penser aux victimes et Ã  toutes celles et ceux qui sont intervenus.

Au-delÃ  des polÃ©miques, un constat s'impose. Nous n'Ã©tions pas prÃªts et nous ne sommes pas prÃªts. Et en tant que sapeur-pompier, je ne peux m'y rÃ©soudre. L'absence de systÃ¨me d'alerte prÃ©coce, le manque de coordination ou encore les rÃ©flexes instinctifs de nos concitoyens, finalement, aggravent le bilan chaque jour. Aujourd'hui, il ne s'agit pas de chercher des coupables, mais d'assumer notre responsabilitÃ© commune, car c'est notre devoir de prÃ©parer notre population. C'est notre devoir d'instaurer une vÃ©ritable culture du risque. Il nous faut une union prÃ©parÃ©e, une union de la rÃ©silience partagÃ©e et durable. Faisons de la prÃ©paration un rÃ©flexe collectif et europÃ©en. Et surtout, n'opposons pas souverainetÃ© et solidaritÃ©. Les crises, elles, n'ont pas de frontiÃ¨res ni de partis politiques.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Vicent MarzÃ  IbÃ¡Ã±ez, en nombre del Grupo Verts/ALE. â SeÃ±or presidente, a diferencia del Gobierno valenciano, que sigue mÃ¡s centrado en huir de la justicia que en reparar el dolor de las vÃ­ctimas, o de Parlamentos como el Congreso del Estado espaÃ±ol, que estÃ¡n sumidos permanentemente en la bronca partidista, este Parlamento âtambiÃ©n la ComisiÃ³n Europeaâ ha estado relativamente a la altura. AquÃ­ ha sido el primer sitio donde las vÃ­ctimas han podido comparecer y ser escuchadas en sede parlamentaria. Y aquÃ­ nos hemos puesto de acuerdo entre representantes de fuerzas democrÃ¡ticas de diferentes partidos y diferentes nacionalidades para que los fondos lleguen a las zonas afectadas y para cambiar normativas que tenÃ­an que cambiarse de forma urgente. Por lo tanto, gracias en nombre de los valencianos y de las valencianas, aunque no sea suficiente.

Y no es suficiente porque tenemos que aprender, tenemos que aprender y mucho. Los fondos no pueden no ser condicionados. Miren, ahora mismo el Gobierno valenciano ha cambiado la ley para que se vuelva a poder construir en zonas inundables. Y se harÃ¡ âpuede serâ con dinero europeo. Esto es insostenible. No lo podemos permitir. Y segundo, los fondos deben ser suficientes. Miren, el Fondo de Solidaridad se ha mostrado totalmente insuficiente. Solo para las zonas afectadas de la Comunitat Valenciana, del PaÃ­s ValenciÃ , son el 81Â % de los fondos del Fondo de Solidaridad, y para el resto ya no quedan mÃ¡s fondos. TambiÃ©n tenemos que hablar de cÃ³mo se han desviado fondos estructurales para situaciones de emergencia. Lo hemos conseguido gracias al Restore y llegan fondos. Pero no puede ser que los fondos estructurales se destinen a emergencias y no a aquello para lo que fueron establecidos, a las cuestiones de infraestructuras esenciales.

AquÃ­ quiero llamar la atenciÃ³n a los grupos democrÃ¡ticos de derecha âsi es que todavÃ­a quedanâ. Y es que las consecuencias no solo son personales, que ya deberÃ­a ser suficiente argumento. No solo son ambientales, que ya deberÃ­a ser suficiente argumento. TambiÃ©n lo son econÃ³micas. Solo en el PaÃ­s ValenciÃ  las pÃ©rdidas son de dieciocho mil millones. Ustedes se rasgaban las vestiduras este verano con los grandes incendios. Ayer votaban en contra de proteger los bosques en una normativa que tenÃ­amos aquÃ­. Deben dejar de seguir la corriente de la ultraderecha de negar el cambio climÃ¡tico, porque la economÃ­a europea no se lo puede permitir. El cambio climÃ¡tico se combate o nos arrasa. Si no lo hacen, si no les parece suficiente hacerlo por las personas, si no les parece suficiente hacerlo por el planeta, hÃ¡ganloÂ al menos por la economÃ­a.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Irene Montero, en nombre del Grupo The Left. â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±orÃ­as, Â¿cÃ³mo pueden ser ustedes tan crueles de permitir que Carlos MazÃ³n acuda al funeral de Estado de las vÃ­ctimas de la dana? Â¿Se imaginan el dolor de esas familias que perdieron a un ser querido, que lo han perdido todo? Â¿Ni siquiera les van a dejar ustedes honrar la memoria de los suyos sin tener delante al responsable de esa gestiÃ³n homicida? Â¿No les han hecho ya sufrir lo suficiente?

Ustedes no estÃ¡n buscando unidad, seÃ±orÃ­as del PP, estÃ¡n buscando impunidad. Y todos los esfuerzos deberÃ­an dedicarse a reparar a las vÃ­ctimas y a acabar con las polÃ­ticas homicidas del Partido Popular para que nadie mÃ¡s tenga nunca que pasar esa angustia. A dÃ­a de hoy sigue habiendo indemnizaciones sin llegar, edificios con riesgo de derrumbe, niÃ±os y niÃ±as estudiando en barracones, pequeÃ±as empresas que no han podido reabrir, servicios de emergencia desmantelados. Siguen sin aprobarse medidas para evitar nuevas inundaciones y para poner freno a la especulaciÃ³n inmobiliaria en zonas inundables. NingÃºn empresario, como el presidente de Mercadona, ha pagado por haber mandado a sus trabajadores y trabajadoras a trabajar en plena emergencia.

No olvidamos, seÃ±orÃ­as, y no descansaremos hasta que se haga justicia.

Cuidemos los servicios pÃºblicos.

MazÃ³n, dimisiÃ³n. Y, sobre todo, MazÃ³n a prisiÃ³n.

Â Â

Â Â Anja Arndt, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. â Herr PrÃ¤sident! Vor einem Jahr ereignete sich ein unfassbarer Starkregen in Valencia in Spanien. In 24Â Stunden fielen unglaubliche 300Â Liter pro Quadratmeter. 230 Menschen starben. Dieser Starkregen wird als WetterphÃ¤nomen abgetan, und dem Klimawandel durch CO2âEmissionen wird die Schuld gegeben.

Das ist nicht nur falsch, sondern es blendet auch wichtige Fakten aus: Seit 1980 erzeugt der Nachbarstaat Marokko kÃ¼nstlich Regen mit Silberjodid, das von Flugzeugen versprÃ¼ht wird. Genau zwei Monate vor diesem Starkregen in Valencia wurde dieses Programm in Marokko massiv ausgeweitet. Spanische Meteorologen, Politiker und UmweltschÃ¼tzer warnten im gleichen Monat vor Starkregen und Ãberschwemmungen auf spanischem Gebiet.

Auch in China, Indien, Saudi-Arabien, Europa, den USA und Afrika: Ãberall gibt es GeoengineeringâProjekte. Das wird komplett ausgeblendet, um das MÃ¤rchen vom Klimawandel durch CO2âEmissionen aufrechtzuerhalten. Damit lassen sich ja hinter den Kulissen gigantische GeldgeschÃ¤fte machen. Ãber den Einfluss von Geoengineering muss endlich ehrlich diskutiert werden, um Menschenleben in Zukunft zu retten. Deutschland braucht die AfD, und Europa braucht die ESN.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ana Miguel Pedro (PPE). â Senhor Presidente, Senhores Deputados, poderia iniciar esta intervenÃ§Ã£o de muitas formas, mas escolho fazÃªâlo da Ãºnica maneira digna deste Parlamento: com respeito profundo pelas vÃ­timas. Morreram 230 pessoas num paÃ­s europeu, moderno, com tecnologia, com recursos, com promessas de seguranÃ§a e discursos de progresso. Morreram em garagens, morreram em carros arrastados pela corrente, morreram em circunstÃ¢ncias que nenhum aviso deveria permitir, mas que todos os sinais anunciavam.

A DANA nÃ£o foi apenas uma tempestade meteorolÃ³gica: foi um colapso humano. Foi um desastre que expÃ´s fragilidades de sistemas, atrasos em alertas, falhas de coordenaÃ§Ã£o e uma vulnerabilidade que ultrapassa fronteiras e governos.

Hoje, Ã© tempo de solidariedade, com as famÃ­lias que choram, com as comunidades que perderam tudo, com os bombeiros, os tÃ©cnicos e os voluntÃ¡rios que resistiram atÃ© ao limite. Mas Ã© tambÃ©m tempo de aprender. NÃ£o temos, ainda, uma verdadeira cultura de prontidÃ£o, nÃ£o possuÃ­mos uma estrutura sÃ³lida e integrada para responder a crises desta dimensÃ£o e, quando a sorte substitui a preparaÃ§Ã£o, o risco deixa de ser acaso e tornaâse destino.

E Ã© por isso, senhores deputados, que esta tragÃ©dia nÃ£o deve ser lembrada apenas com lÃ¡grimas, mas com vergonha: vergonha cÃ­vica, vergonha moral, vergonha de termos criado um tempo em que as catÃ¡strofes se repetem e as responsabilidades se diluem.

Talvez este seja o verdadeiro teste do nosso tempo: se conseguimos transformar a dor em dever, a memÃ³ria em aÃ§Ã£o e o poder, finalmente, em serviÃ§o.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Thomas Bajada (S&D). â MrÂ President, the deadly floods that we had in Spain a year ago reminded us how fragile life can be.

Let's be honest, dear colleagues, Europe could be drowning and burning at the same time. Europe is not prepared. Europe is not adapting to the changing climate. As the rapporteur on European water resilience, I speak on behalf of all Europeans, especially those most vulnerable, those who adapt every single day to survive and to live with dignity at the frontline of climate disaster.

We must be prepared when the next disaster strikes, and that means investing in water systems that store, reuse and protect. That means making health preparedness a cornerstone of our climate response. That means mapping risks across borders and funding prevention.

Climate resilience must now become Europe's new growth model, anchored in prevention, powered by innovation and guided by solidarity. Because climate resilience is not about resisting climate and climate change, it is about shaping it together before it reshapes how we are today.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Julien Sanchez (PfE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, nous sommes ici pour Ã©voquer les suites des inondations terribles intervenues dans la rÃ©gion de Valencia, en Espagne, il y a un an. L'aide europÃ©enne au titre du Fonds de solidaritÃ© de l'Union ne reprÃ©sente que 5,23Â % des dommages directs. On nous parle de solidaritÃ© europÃ©enne sans arrÃªt, mais je voudrais dire ici qu'Ã  mon sens, cette aide europÃ©enne n'est pas du tout Ã  la hauteur des enjeux de cette catastrophe majeure. Elle est mÃªme dÃ©risoire au regard des besoins rÃ©els. Nous y sommes hÃ©las habituÃ©s, puisque les catastrophes de l'Ã®le de la RÃ©union et de Mayotte n'ont reÃ§u aussi que 2,5Â % des dommages directs. Cette disproportion est inacceptable, d'autant que l'Union trouve sans difficultÃ© des milliards d'euros pour financer des programmes spÃ©ciaux taillÃ©s sur mesure pour des pays non membres de l'Union europÃ©enne. Il ne faudrait pas que nos citoyens aient le sentiment ubuesque que l'Europe prÃ©fÃ¨re aider les autres avant les nÃ´tres. Alors retrouvons un peu de dÃ©cence et de bon sens. Â Â Stine Bosse (Renew), blue-card question. â Thank you very much for taking the blue card. I have a couple of questions.Â I spent 30Â years in the insurance industry, and I saw these events piling up.

I acknowledge the fact that you want to help, but your help will not bring lives back. Your help will not bring houses back for the people who are homeless. So what are you actually going to do about the fact that the climate is changing?

And do you acknowledge that the change is made by our CO2 emissions and that we can actually do something about it? Thousands of actuaries within the insurance industry and also meteorologists acknowledge this completely and they show us the way forward.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Julien Sanchez (PfE), rÃ©ponse carton bleu. â Je crois, Madame, chÃ¨re collÃ¨gue â j'ai Ã©tÃ© maire d'une commune en France pendant dix ans qui Ã©tait le long du RhÃ´ne, un fleuve europÃ©en â et je crois surtout que, ce qui ne ramÃ¨nera pas les morts, comme vous le dites, ce sont les dÃ©faillances des gouvernants et des dirigeants qui ont prÃ©sidÃ© aux destinÃ©es de l'Espagne Ã  ce moment-lÃ . Et je crois que, de ce point de vue-lÃ , il faudrait rÃ©ellement que les gouvernants agissent et n'attendent pas les catastrophes pour rÃ©agir. Ce que nous avons malheureusement trop vu en Espagne.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â GrÃ©gory Allione (Renew), question Â«carton bleuÂ». â Vous remettez la faute chaque fois sur les gouvernants. Nous sommes ici Ã  prendre des dispositions et, chaque fois, vous refusez finalement toutes les dispositions que l'on prend ici mÃªme. Â Â Julien Sanchez (PfE), rÃ©ponse carton bleu. â Non, je crois, Monsieur le dÃ©putÃ©, cher collÃ¨gue, que vous vous Ãªtes mal renseignÃ©. Nous ne demandons pas le Frexit, nous demandons Ã  changer l'Union europÃ©enne de l'intÃ©rieur et nous dÃ©plorons notamment que celui que vous soutenez, c'est-Ã -dire EmmanuelÂ Macron, ne dÃ©fende pas ici les intÃ©rÃªts de la France et que tous les postes soient trustÃ©s gÃ©nÃ©ralement par des Allemands, parce que la France ne sait pas dÃ©fendre ses intÃ©rÃªts. Je le regrette, je le dÃ©plore. Et donc moi, j'essaie de dÃ©fendre les intÃ©rÃªts franÃ§ais et je dis qu'effectivement Ã§a me paraÃ®t peu d'aider l'Espagne Ã  5Â % et d'aider la France aussi Ã  2,5Â % quand il y a des catastrophes de ce style-lÃ . Â Â Nora Junco GarcÃ­a (ECR). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±orÃ­as, las gotas frÃ­as y las riadas en la costa mediterrÃ¡nea son parte de la naturaleza, no de un relato climÃ¡tico inventado para justificar la falta de gestiÃ³n.

Esta tragedia se veÃ­a venir: desde el 94, los expertos ya venÃ­an advirtiendo de que en el barranco del Poyo se necesitaba un encauzamiento y un drenaje. Nueve proyectos se redactaron, nueve. Â¿Y quÃ© hemos visto? DÃ©cadas de Gobiernos, de todos los colores, dejando esas obras en el cajÃ³n; obras que debÃ­an haberse hecho en los 90, en los aÃ±os 2000, y que hoy siguen pendientes.

En quince aÃ±os no se ha ejecutado ni una sola obra hidrÃ¡ulica de prevenciÃ³n relevante en la Comunidad Valenciana. Por eso, hoy despuÃ©s de un aÃ±o, me pregunto: Â¿Por quÃ© los planes de infraestructura preventiva llevan dÃ©cadas abandonados? Â¿CuÃ¡ndo se van a ejecutar las obras que podÃ­an haber evitado esta tragedia? Â¿QuÃ© mecanismos concretos ha puesto en marcha el Ministerio para la TransiciÃ³n EcolÃ³gica para evitar que esto vuelva a pasar, mÃ¡s allÃ¡ de los discursos?

SeÃ±orÃ­as, Bruselas ha cumplido: mÃ¡s de 1Â 600 millones de euros en fondos de solidaridad y cohesiÃ³n, pero Â¿cuÃ¡ntas ayudas se han activado desde el Gobierno de EspaÃ±a? Muchas siguen bloqueadas. Lo que ha fallado es la responsabilidad polÃ­tica en EspaÃ±a, la inacciÃ³n del Ministerio para la TransiciÃ³n EcolÃ³gica y las promesas incumplidas...

(el presidente retira la palabra a la oradora)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ciaran Mullooly (Renew). â Mr President, there can be no doubt about the scale of the tragedy that hit the people of Valencia this time last year. Our thoughts are with those who lost loved ones in the floods. But one has to ask critical questions about the response of the Spanish authorities and, indeed, the reaction of the so-called 'European crisis machine'. Where was it? The failure of the early warning system is responsible for the damage done in severe weather events like this. Prevention measures are essential.

I raised this directly with Commissioner Lahbib when she came before the Parliament and Marco Panigalli, the head of the Commission's Civil Protection Emergency Response Capabilities unit, admitted it. He said clearly, when they see from the weather instruments there might be a catastrophe looming, the mechanism can be pre-activated, meaning the emergency teams from throughout Europe can go directly to an area under threat before the storm, before the rain, and go on standby, ready to be deployed. Whether it's Spain, or along the Shannon Callows in County Offaly, or indeed any potential blackspot for flooding.

So we must wake up to use these supports from Europe. Member States must trigger the alarm earlier and take more precautions or more will die and more land will be damaged.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Petras GraÅ¾ulis (ESN), pakÄlus mÄlynÄjÄ kortelÄ pateiktas klausimas. â Daugiau kaip prieÅ¡ 80 metÅ³ dalis mokslininkÅ³ skelbÄ, kad Å½emÄ Å¡Äla. JungtinÄse Tautose prieÅ¡ 50 metÅ³ buvo paskelbta, kad Å½emÄ kaista â klimato atÅ¡ilimas. Ir kad 2025 metais Europa dÄl to, kad tirpsta ledynai, bus po vandeniu. Bet prieÅ¡ 10 metÅ³ pamatÄ, kad nepaskendo Europa, tai paskelbÄ, kad vyksta klimato kaita. AÅ¡ noriu paklausti, ar jÅ«s prieÅ¡ tai, kad nebÅ«tÅ³ vasaros ir Å¾iemos, nakties ir dienos? UÅ¾ kÄ jÅ«s? Kad bÅ«tÅ³ visada vasara ar Å¾iema?

Â

Â Â Ciaran Mullooly (Renew), blue-card answer. â I am in favour of the meteorological services, the professional services who tell us today: 'There will be rain â and severe rain â in your Member State on Friday'. I am critical of people who do not respond to this. I do not care whether or not it is a modern activity, whether it is a once-off flood or a major event. The critical thing is lives are being lost.

We saw it in my own constituency and â Commissioner, I read this point to you as well about the role of the habitats act â sometimes we do not deal with the flooding because it is protected. The land is protected.

People must come first. I agree with my colleagues from Spain from today: people must come first.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Estrella GalÃ¡n (The Left). â SeÃ±or presidente, han pasado doce meses desde la dana que se llevÃ³ por delante a mÃ¡s de doscientas treinta y cinco personas. Doce meses en los que hemos visto en las calles la dignidad del pueblo valenciano frente a la indignidad de un Gobierno valenciano que sigue mintiendo sin asumir responsabilidades.

Esta tragedia demostrÃ³ que negar la emergencia climÃ¡tica mata. Lo que pasÃ³ en el PaÃ­s Valenciano no fue mala suerte ni un error de coordinaciÃ³n, fue una indecente dejaciÃ³n de funciones y fue consecuencia de recortar en servicios pÃºblicos, en prevenciÃ³n y tambiÃ©n, o por tanto, en vidas humanas.

Y entre tanto, el seÃ±or MazÃ³n sigue apoyÃ¡ndose en los negacionistas que eliminaron la unidad de emergencia para seguir gobernando allÃ­. Y Â¿por quÃ©? Â¿Por quÃ© lo hace? Porque MazÃ³n elige el poder ante la verdad. Y el Partido Popular lo consiente. Exigimos responsabilidad polÃ­tica, verdad y reparaciÃ³n para que esta tragedia no se repita y que el agua no se lleve lo que la polÃ­tica no supo proteger.

Las vÃ­ctimas aÃºn esperan respuestas y merecen justicia.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Alvise PÃ©rez (NI). â SeÃ±or presidente, quÃ© bonitos los discursos de mis compaÃ±eros sobre la dana, los discursos que emocionaron a Spielberg, atacÃ¡ndose unos a otros, que si el cambio climÃ¡tico, etcÃ©tera.

Nadie ha caÃ­do en que, por ejemplo, la ComisiÃ³n ha hablado aquÃ­ de doscientos treinta muertos. Los datos oficiales, entre comillas, del Gobierno son doscientos diecisÃ©is. Anoche se encontrÃ³ otro cadÃ¡ver. En fin. Ni siquiera tenemos una lista oficial de muertos de la dana. Pero Â¿quÃ© importa? Total, los muertos no votan. Â¿No?

Y luego la ComisiÃ³n Europea aquÃ­ felicitÃ¡ndose de que han tardado solo cinco meses en traer ayuda a las vÃ­ctimas. Â¿Cinco meses, saben ustedes lo que son para todas las vÃ­ctimas de nuestros compaÃ±eros valencianos? Â¿La gente es consciente de lo que son cinco meses? Yo creo que no, en absoluto. Pero Â¿quÃ© mÃ¡s da cuando tenemos a un Carlos MazÃ³n que, efectivamente âfÃ­jense, en algo estoy totalmente de acuerdo con Irene Monteroâ, deberÃ­a estar en prisiÃ³n y tenemos a un Partido Popular Ãºnicamente centrado en la parte electoralista del tÃ©rmino?

Yo de verdad que solo siento pena y lÃ¡stima de que lo Ãºnico que podamos sacar de este Parlamento sean discursos hechos con ChatGPT sobre mÃ¡s de doscientos muertos, porque hasta una mÃ¡quina necesitan para fingir emociones. Los muertos les dan igual. Pero, en fin, Â¿quÃ© importa?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Maravillas AbadÃ­a Jover (PPE). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±ora comisaria, hace un aÃ±o que las lluvias torrenciales acabaron con la vida de mÃ¡s de doscientas personas en Valencia y dejaron cientos de familias destrozadas.

AÃºn resuena en las cabezas una pregunta clave: Â¿cÃ³mo es posible que no contÃ¡ramos con las infraestructuras necesarias para evitar âo al menos mitigarâ esta catÃ¡strofe? No faltaba el conocimiento, las inversiones estaban identificadas, pero faltaron los recursos, y esto no puede volver a ocurrir.

Este Parlamento actuÃ³ con responsabilidad, modificando urgentemente la polÃ­tica de cohesiÃ³n para apoyar la reconstrucciÃ³n, y con la revisiÃ³n intermedia hemos reafirmado nuestro compromiso: dar mÃ¡s flexibilidad y capacidad de respuesta a las regiones.

Sin embargo, mientras el Gobierno nacional ha tardado casi un aÃ±o en movilizar los recursos puestos a su disposiciÃ³n, las regiones actÃºan de inmediato. Es el caso de una regiÃ³n como la mÃ­a, Murcia âtambiÃ©n afectada por las danasâ, que ha ampliado la inversiÃ³n en infraestructuras hÃ­dricas en hasta 30 millones de euros.

Invertir con sentido y propÃ³sito salva vidas. Por eso la polÃ­tica de cohesiÃ³n debe seguir estando en manos de las regiones, porque son quienes mejor conocen los retos y las amenazas a las que se enfrentan.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sandra GÃ³mez LÃ³pez (S&D). â SeÃ±or presidente, aprender de la dana es no solo invertir mÃ¡s en prevenciÃ³n, emergencias o resiliencia. Es tambiÃ©n hacerlo en humanidad y en empatÃ­a. Porque cuando las instituciones maltratan o ignoran a las vÃ­ctimas, como lo ha hecho la Generalitat Valenciana, eso tambiÃ©n es daÃ±o. El vicepresidente llegÃ³ a culpabilizarlas de no haberse sabido autoproteger y, por lo tanto, de sus muertes. MazÃ³n ni se ha reunido con ellas, las ha ignorado, las ha difamado y ademÃ¡s las ha acusado de estar politizadas, como si una madre pudiera fingir o exagerar el dolor de haber perdido a su hija. Insinuar eso es maltrato y abandono institucional. Y forma parte tambiÃ©n del desastre.

Y tenemos que aprender del fracaso polÃ­tico del Partido Popular en mi tierra. Europa no solo necesita mÃ¡s y mejores infraestructuras, necesita instituciones humanas que escuchen, acompaÃ±en y reparen. Por eso deberÃ­amos crear el estatuto europeo de las vÃ­ctimas de catÃ¡strofes naturales, para que la empatÃ­a no dependa de quiÃ©n gobierne, sino que sea un derecho garantizado para todos. Y deberÃ­amos hablar tambiÃ©n de cÃ³mo tratamos a quienes sufren estas catÃ¡strofes.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

VORSITZ: KATARINA BARLEY
VizeprÃ¤sidentin

Â Â

Â Â Hermann Tertsch (PfE). â SeÃ±ora presidente, se cumple un aÃ±o de la tragedia, de la riada, de aquellos 230 âo los muertos que fueranâ. Ni el Gobierno nacional del PSOE ni el regional del Partido Popular avisaron antes de la catÃ¡strofe, y SÃ¡nchez no permitiÃ³, durante varios dÃ­as, que llegara ayuda a pueblos destruidos.

Hoy se sabe que los muertos se habrÃ­an evitado de haberse acometido unas simples obras en una serie de barrancos, pero habÃ­an sido paralizadas por el Ministerio de una seÃ±ora conocida aquÃ­, Teresa Ribera, esa socialista de efectos letales por su fanatismo ideolÃ³gico. Â¿Y el retraso en la ayuda? SÃ­, seÃ±orÃ­as, SÃ¡nchez dejÃ³ morir a espaÃ±oles para desgastar al adversario polÃ­tico que gobierna esa regiÃ³n.

Hoy los damnificados viven aÃºn entre escombros, basuras y ratas, y los niÃ±os estudian en barracones. El fanatismo climÃ¡tico, el fracasado sistema autonÃ³mico y la falta de escrÃºpulos de un SÃ¡nchez a quien nada importa mÃ¡s que su poder, hicieron posible esta tragedia que, como tanto crimen cometido por el Gobierno espaÃ±ol en este lustro, sigue aÃºn impune. Eso es lo que hay que corregir.

Â Â Carmen Crespo DÃ­az (PPE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±ora comisaria, quiero transmitir la consideraciÃ³n de mi Grupo a las vÃ­ctimas y a sus familias y nuestro acompaÃ±amiento caluroso en su aniversario sin excluir a nadie, porque en AndalucÃ­a tambiÃ©n hubo inundaciones. Por tanto, un no rotundo a la utilizaciÃ³n de las vÃ­ctimas y un sÃ­ a la unidad y a la recuperaciÃ³n. Porque el Gobierno autonÃ³mico y la sociedad valenciana estÃ¡n en marcha, en marcha con un plan de recuperaciÃ³n. Y tambiÃ©n de Europa, con 1Â 600Â millones de euros. El sectarismo contra los embalses, la regulaciÃ³n o la restauraciÃ³n de los cauces, esto cuesta vidas. Hay mucho que hacer por las vÃ­ctimas y, desde luego, dignidad.

Planes de recuperaciÃ³n. Trabajar rÃ¡pido por la seguridad hÃ­drica, porque ya hemos cometido demasiados errores. Y humanidad. Â Â Thomas Pellerin-Carlin (S&D). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, je me tiens aujourd'hui devant vous le cÅur serrÃ©. Ãviter l'ingÃ©rable, car oui, le pire peut encore Ãªtre Ã©vitÃ© grÃ¢ce Ã  notre action ici dans ce Parlement. Â Â Hildegard Bentele (PPE). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, Frau Kommissarin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Aber gerade fÃ¼r StÃ¤dte ist auch die Umsetzung prÃ¤ventiver MaÃnahmen wichtig, wie der Umbau zur Schwammstadt, den wir in meiner Heimatstadt Berlin 2016 mit EUâGeldern begonnen haben. Dieser Umbau kostet viel Geld und noch mehr Zeit, und er setzt eine gute Einbindung der BevÃ¶lkerung voraus. EuropÃ¤ische StÃ¤dte kÃ¶nnen auf diesem Weg sicherlich voneinander lernen. Das haben meine GesprÃ¤che insbesondere mit den Experten und Vertretern des Ausschusses der Regionen und Kommunen gezeigt. Â Â Cristina Maestre (S&D). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, un aÃ±o despuÃ©s de la dana, algunos siguen negando lo evidente: que el cambio climÃ¡tico no es una amenaza. Yo creo que sÃ­, que es una realidad: municipios que se lleva por delante el agua, vidas arrebatadas. Y si nos unimos al fanatismo y al negacionismo âya lo hemos visto y escuchado aquÃ­ en este Pleno inclusoâ. Y si lo unimos con la incompetencia y la desvergÃ¼enza, tenemos como resultado un gobierno fantasma que deambula por el castillo con su sÃ¡bana y su cadena. Y lo mÃ¡s importante, que los ciudadanos fallecidos y sus familias no se merecen lo que estÃ¡ ocurriendo en Valencia.

Â¿QuÃ© lecciones debemos sacar respecto a los efectos del cambio climÃ¡tico? Uno, que la prevenciÃ³n no puede ser solo una opciÃ³n, sino el primer pilar de la acciÃ³n climÃ¡tica y territorial. Dos, que la prevenciÃ³n y la respuesta suponen voluntad y dinero: voluntad polÃ­tica para cambiar la tendencia cambiando el modelo y eso supone dinero para hacerlo posible. Y tres, que a veces las catÃ¡strofes no se pueden evitar, pero sÃ­ se puede evitar la magnitud de su impacto y tambiÃ©n la forma de abordar la respuesta.

En EspaÃ±a, el dÃ­aÂ 29 de octubre deÂ 2024 hubo una catÃ¡strofe en dos puntos de EspaÃ±a, pero la respuesta durante y despuÃ©s fueron totalmente distintas. Los ciudadanos tendrÃ¡n que juzgar.
