Â Â Pascal Arimont (PPE). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, sehr geehrte Frau Kommissarin! Insgesamt 500Â Tote haben wir in den letzten fÃ¼nf Jahren durch Hochwasser in Europa zu beklagenÂ â unter anderem und nicht zuletzt in Valencia. Die Natur schlÃ¤gt unerbittlich zu. Sie reiÃt Menschen in den Tod und verwÃ¼stet ganze Landstriche. Die Flut vernichtet unzÃ¤hlige LebensrÃ¤ume und damit zahllose LebenstrÃ¤ume. Alle Experten sind sich einig: Der Anstieg dieser desastrÃ¶sen Naturkatastrophen ist in Zukunft nicht linear, sondern exponentiell.

Katastrophen sind daher nicht nur eine nationale oder regionale Angelegenheit, sondern mittlerweile und aufgrund der Zahlen immer Ã¶fter eine europÃ¤ische Angelegenheit. Das gilt fÃ¼r die Information, dass etwas geschehen kann, das gilt fÃ¼r die Koordination, wenn etwas geschieht, und das gilt bei der Hilfe fÃ¼r den resilienten Wiederaufbau. Europa muss angesichts dieser Katastrophen und der steigenden Anzahl Katastrophen mehr sein als ein Binnenmarkt fÃ¼r Waren. Europa muss ein Binnenmarkt fÃ¼r gelebte SolidaritÃ¤t sein. Die Opfer in Valencia verdienen das.

Â Â Claire Fita (S&D). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, chers collÃ¨gues, inondations Ã  Valence, incendies dans l'Aude, le dÃ©rÃ¨glement climatique frappe chaque annÃ©e plus fort, plus longtemps, plus prÃ¨s.

L'Union europÃ©enne prÃ©voit de tripler les moyens de la protection civile, et c'est indispensable, mais ce n'est absolument pas suffisant. Parce que, dans le mÃªme temps, la Commission propose de rÃ©duire les moyens de la PAC, et c'est inacceptable.

On ne peut pas prÃ©tendre protÃ©ger les citoyens tout en coupant les financements Ã  ceux qui, sur le terrain, prÃ©viennent les catastrophes. Parce que c'est grÃ¢ce notamment Ã  la PAC que les Ã©lus des collectivitÃ©s forestiÃ¨res d'Occitanie se battent chaque jour; entretenir les forÃªts, tracer les pistes et donc limiter les risques.

Alors oui, l'Europe doit choisir: la prÃ©vention ou l'abandon. Moi, je refuse l'abandon, l'abandon qu'imposent la droite et l'extrÃªme droite, toujours promptes Ã  couper dans les solidaritÃ©s. PrÃ©venir, c'est protÃ©ger. Renoncer, c'est trahir. Ã nous de choisir.

Spontane Wortmeldungen

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marcos Ros Sempere (S&D). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, una riada arrasa en segundos lo que ha costado toda una vida construir, y, por eso, las instituciones tenemos que predecir, prevenir y, cuando el desastre es inevitable, reaccionar con la mÃ¡xima celeridad y eficacia.

Muchas de las 229 muertes pudieron haberse evitado; un aÃ±o despuÃ©s, debemos honrarlas, estar al lado de quienes sufrieron y trabajar para que nunca vuelva a repetirse.

Hace unos meses este Parlamento aprobÃ³ Restore para responder mejor a los desastres provocados por el cambio climÃ¡tico. Pero hay algo que ninguna norma, por muy exhaustiva que sea, puede garantizar: la ejemplaridad.

La ejemplaridad de alcaldes como Mario PÃ©rez Cervera âen Los AlcÃ¡zares, en la RegiÃ³n de Murciaâ que, una dana tras otra, estÃ¡ al pie del caÃ±Ã³n junto a sus ciudadanos âla Ãºltima hace diez dÃ­as, con el delegado del Gobierno, gestionando la emergencia, acompaÃ±ando y dando la caraâ; esa ejemplaridad que no tuvieron hace un aÃ±o los valencianos con su president. Quien no estuvo a la altura debe marcharse, pedir perdÃ³n y rendir cuentas ante los ciudadanos y la justicia.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). â Arvoisa puhemies, Espanjan tuhoisat tulvat, vuosi tulee tÃ¤yteen. Monen espanjalaisperheen elÃ¤mÃ¤ jÃ¤rkkyi silloin pysyvÃ¤sti. Se oli traaginen hetki monille, mutta se oli myÃ¶s kansallinen tragedia. Minun on valitettavasti sanottava Ã¤Ã¤neen nÃ¤mÃ¤ vaikeat sanat: liittovaltiokehitystÃ¤ ei pidÃ¤ edistÃ¤Ã¤ millÃ¤Ã¤n syyllÃ¤, edes hyvÃ¤llÃ¤ syyllÃ¤. EU ei voi, eikÃ¤ siitÃ¤ pidÃ¤ tulla luonnonilmiÃ¶iden ylikansallista vakuutusyhtiÃ¶tÃ¤. Realismia on, ettÃ¤ kyseisiin ilmiÃ¶ihin varaudutaan ja reagoidaan tehokkaimmin kansallisella tasolla. Jokaisella maalla on omat viranomaisensa sekÃ¤ kansalliset budjettinsa, joiden pitÃ¤Ã¤ nÃ¤issÃ¤ tilanteissa astua peliin. Tulee taivaalta sitten vettÃ¤ tai kuten meidÃ¤n tapauksessamme Suomessa lunta, joka pistÃ¤Ã¤ kaupunkeja jumiin, jÃ¤senvaltion on vastattava. Surraan uhreja, mutta Ã¤lkÃ¤Ã¤mme kÃ¤yttÃ¤kÃ¶ heidÃ¤n tragediaansa liittovaltion rakennusaineena.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Diana Riba i Giner (Verts/ALE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, hoy recordamos a las vÃ­ctimas de la devastadora dana que afectÃ³ al PaÃ­s ValenciÃ  el 29Â de octubre deÂ 2024, a sus familias y a todos los que siguen sin recibir la reparaciÃ³n que les corresponde. La tragedia no merece la demora ni la indolencia institucional y, por supuesto, no merece que su mÃ¡ximo responsable siga sin rendir cuentas. Porque no fallaron los meteorÃ³logos. FallÃ³ la polÃ­tica. Y su rostro tiene nombre y partido: Carlos MazÃ³n, Partido Popular.

Pero esta conmemoraciÃ³n debe servir para algo mÃ¡s. Los episodios extremos ya no son anomalÃ­as. Apenas hace unos dÃ­as, en mi paÃ­s, en Catalunya, en les Terres de lâEbre, vivimos de nuevo lluvias torrenciales que nos recuerdan que el cambio climÃ¡tico no espera. Tenemos una obligaciÃ³n: reaccionar no solo cuando el caos ha estallado, sino anticipÃ¡ndonos, invertir en resiliencia, alertas tempranas e infraestructuras que soporten esta nueva normalidad.

El maÃ±ana exige que estemos preparados hoy.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Grzegorz Braun (NI). â Madam President, colleagues, stop using human tragedy to justify your climatic nonsense, to justify your political pursuit of central governing that you want to impose. This is all nonsense. Human anthropogenic global warming â give me a break.

As we speak, changes â real changes â are approaching. We are approaching the new world because, thanks to maybe a bit of warming, ships from the Far East are coming to Europe through the Northeast Passage. And if so, this will mean changing the polarity of the world trade map. So give me some more of your global warming, because this is something that we can profit off, and stop imposing your doctrines, stop justifying your really wrong policies.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, queridos ciudadanos de Europa, seÃ±ora Junco GarcÃ­a, usted dijo: Â«No fue el cambio climÃ¡tico, fue un fallo administrativoÂ». Con respeto, eso me sorprende mucho. Primero, sÃ­ fue el cambio climÃ¡tico, porque el cambio climÃ¡tico provoca cada vez mÃ¡s desastres naturales, como las lluvias de la dana; y segundo, el problema real en la AdministraciÃ³n es la falta de comprensiÃ³n o, a veces, la negaciÃ³n del cambio climÃ¡tico, tambiÃ©n aquÃ­, en este Parlamento.

Escuchemos a la ciencia, a los expertos reales, no a los charlatanes que dicen mentiras. Solo juntos podremos cuidar nuestro planeta y salvar a la humanidad.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(Ende der spontanen Wortmeldungen)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jessika Roswall, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, Minister, honourable Members, I was long in my first remarks, so I will be brief. Thank you for this debate. The Commission will keep working with the European Parliament, with Member States and all partners across every sector, to strengthen Europe's preparedness for all types of crisis and disasters, including floods.

Our approach is simple all assets, all sectors, all hands on deck.

Let me end with one key message. Preparedness does not come for free. Failing to prepare is far more costly. Every euro we invest in preparedness saves lives and saves money in the long run. It builds resilience, keeps our economy stable, and helps Europe bounce back faster after crisis.

Everyone has a role to play because protecting people is at the heart of our Union. It's who we are. It's what we believe in and it is what solidarity means in practice.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â MadamÂ President, honourable Members, Commissioner, I will also be brief in my concluding remarks.

The tragic floods in Spain one year ago ruined many lives and left an impact that is still being felt. I offer, once again, my heartfelt condolences to all those affected. The Council remains committed to strengthening the support for recovery efforts and improving EU climate actions.

We will also work hard to strengthen the EU's preparedness to disasters, including floods. That includes the future Union Civil Protection Mechanism, which we look forward to negotiating with the Parliament soon.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Die PrÃ¤sidentin. â Die Aussprache ist geschlossen.

Â Â

Â 

18. Allegations of espionage by the Hungarian government within the EU institutions (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â Die PrÃ¤sidentin. â Als nÃ¤chster Punkt der Tagesordnung folgt die Aussprache Ã¼ber die ErklÃ¤rungen des Rates und der Kommission zu dem Thema âMutmaÃliche SpionagetÃ¤tigkeiten der ungarischen Regierung in den EU-Institutionenâ (2025/2941(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Piotr Serafin, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, honourable Members, let me start by saying that we are aware that the Commission and the EU institutions are a prominent target for information gathering entities, and that the number of security threats is only growing.

Let me assure you that the Commission is strongly committed to protecting information, staff and networks from illicit Intelligence gathering actions, the Commission has in place a robust security framework with rules and processes aimed at identifying and mitigating risks, as well as strengthening the security resilience of our staff.

A key role in this respect plays a specialised team responsible for human and digital counterintelligence analysis. On intelligence gathering, we rely on a significant level of cooperation with the services of the Member States and other EU institutions.

To strengthen the resilience of our staff regarding human and digital espionage, we carry out awareness raising campaigns. Through these, staff members are reminded regularly how to recognise and deal with intelligence gathering attempts. But we also follow closely the media reports. Allegations of espionage by the Hungarian authorities against staff of the EU institutions are taken by the Commission services very seriously.

Regarding the recent allegations, I can confirm that there is an internal investigation ongoing for operational reasons and to ensure the integrity of the investigation, we must ensure the confidentiality of the process and hence I cannot communicate on the details. But I want to make it clear that the Commission services are closely examining the case.

We also recognise the strong and legitimate interest of the Parliament and EU citizens in this matter. As I emphasised in another case related to Olaf officials operating in Hungary, ensuring the right conditions for the staff to carry out their mission and looking after their safety is and will remain our important task, also, for me personally.

I want to assure you that the President of the Commission also takes this matter very seriously. These alleged incidents undermine the principle of loyal cooperation between Member States and EU institutions. Even though the investigations have not yet been fully completed, I can assure you that the Commission will raise its concerns with the Hungarian Government at all levels. We are fully committed to devote continued efforts to protect the security of the Commission.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â

Â Â Birgit Sippel, im Namen der S&D-Fraktion. â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin! Zwei Menschen treffen sich, flÃ¼stern, tauschen Dokumente aus. Was wie eine Szene aus einem Spionagefilm klingt, war laut Recherchen von Investigativjournalisten bittere RealitÃ¤t. Von 2013 bis 2018 sollen ungarische Geheimdienste ein Spionagenetzwerk in EU-Institutionen aufgebaut und unter diplomatischer Tarnung versucht haben, EU-Mitarbeiterinnen und âMitarbeiter fÃ¼r die Weitergabe vertraulicher Informationen zu gewinnen. Laut Berichten geschah dies mit Wissen des damaligen Botschafters OlivÃ©r VÃ¡rhelyi, heute EU-Kommissar.

Bereits frÃ¼here Recherchen zeigen, dass der ungarische Geheimdienst OLAF-Ermittler Ã¼berwachte, die den Missbrauch von EU-Geldern unter anderem im Umfeld von OrbÃ¡ns Familie untersuchten. Weitere Berichte belegen den Einsatz von SpÃ¤hsoftware gegen Journalisten, AnwÃ¤lte, Politiker. Der PEGA-Ausschuss des Parlaments und der EuropÃ¤ische Gerichtshof fÃ¼r Menschenrechte kritisierten diese Praktiken scharf.

GrundsÃ¤tzlich: Wo immer Regierungen zivile, kritische Akteure Ã¼berwachen, wird Demokratie angegriffen. Und wer seine Partner ausspioniert, verletzt eine wesentliche Grundlage vertrauensvoller Zusammenarbeit. Wir brauchen jetzt Transparenz und Konsequenz. Ich begrÃ¼Ãe die AnkÃ¼ndigung der Kommission fÃ¼r eine interne Untersuchung. Aber das darf nicht im Verborgenen bleiben. Dieses Parlament und die Ãffentlichkeit haben ein Recht zu erfahren, was geschehen ist und wie derartige AktivitÃ¤ten Ã¼ber Jahre unbemerkt bleiben konnten. Sollte sich auch nur ein Teil der VorwÃ¼rfe bestÃ¤tigen, darf es nicht bei mahnenden Worten bleiben. Dann braucht es klare Konsequenzen, auch an der Spitze der Kommission. Wir mÃ¼ssen sicherstellen, dass Europa ein Ort bleibt, an dem wir alle als BÃ¼rger und Institutionen frei, sicher und ohne Angst vor Ãberwachung leben und arbeiten kÃ¶nnen in einem freien und demokratischen Europa.

Â Â Csaba DÃ¶mÃ¶tÃ¶r, a PfE kÃ©pviselÅcsoport nevÃ©ben. â ElnÃ¶k Asszony! Ãgy lÃ¡tom, hogy megint kÃ©pesek voltak egyetlen ÃºjsÃ¡gcikk Ã¡llÃ­tÃ¡sai alapjÃ¡n vitÃ¡t rendezni MagyarorszÃ¡grÃ³l. Na miÃ©rt is van az, hogy ezekrÅl sosincs vita itt, a Parlamentben, MagyarorszÃ¡grÃ³l mindig. Mindenki tudja, hogy valÃ³jÃ¡ban mirÅl van szÃ³. Az a bajuk, hogy MagyarorszÃ¡g Ã¶nÃ¡llÃ³, a baloldali, nÃ©ppÃ¡rti fÅsodortÃ³l eltÃ©rÅ politikÃ¡t folytat. Maguk migrÃ¡ciÃ³s paktumot akarnak, MagyarorszÃ¡g zÃ©rÃ³ bevÃ¡ndorlÃ¡st. Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sophie WilmÃ¨s, au nom du groupe Renew. â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, en dÃ©but d'annÃ©e, nous exprimions dÃ©jÃ  nos inquiÃ©tudes aprÃ¨s des rÃ©vÃ©lations sidÃ©rantes. On parlait dÃ©jÃ  de la Hongrie, Ãtat membre Ã  part entiÃ¨re de l'Union europÃ©enne, je le rappelle, qui a pourtant espionnÃ©, suivi et mis sur Ã©coute des enquÃªteurs de l'Office europÃ©en de lutte antifraude. Ã l'Ã©poque, vous nous aviez rassurÃ©s, vous nous aviez dit que les institutions Ã©taient Ã  l'abri de ce type d'ingÃ©rence. Dix mois plus tard, aujourd'hui, grÃ¢ce aux enquÃªtes de la presse, notamment Direkt36 et DeÂ Tijd, nous dÃ©couvrons que c'est tout un rÃ©seau d'espionnage qui aurait Ã©tÃ© mis en place.

Soyons clairs, aujourd'hui, le pouvoir hongrois dÃ©fie tous les principes de loyautÃ© et de solidaritÃ© entre les Ãtats membres, agit contre leurs intÃ©rÃªts et ceux de l'Europe et, vu la nature des faits, met bien Ã©videmment en danger la sÃ©curitÃ© des EuropÃ©ens.

Monsieur le Commissaire, aujourd'hui, on ne sait en fait rien de l'enquÃªte interne Ã  la Commission, rien des prÃ©cautions et des dispositifs qui ont Ã©tÃ© mis en place depuis, s'ils ont Ã©tÃ© mis en place, pour Ã©viter les opÃ©rations d'espionnage. En plus, lorsque ces opÃ©rations ont Ã©tÃ© lancÃ©es depuis la reprÃ©sentation permanente hongroise, il faut le rappeler, c'est votre collÃ¨gue, M.Â VÃ¡rhelyi, qui en Ã©tait le patron. La Commission auraitÂ dÃ» suspendre le commissaireÂ VÃ¡rhelyi, au moins le temps de faire toute la lumiÃ¨re sur cette affaire.

Alors, si la Commission ne prend pas pleinement ses responsabilitÃ©s, c'est Ã  nous, je pense, le Parlement, de le faire, et c'est pourquoi nous soutenons la crÃ©ation immÃ©diate d'une commission d'enquÃªte sur les opÃ©rations d'espionnage de la Hongrie.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Terry Reintke, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. â Madam President, dear Commissioner, dear colleagues, almost 70 years ago, tens of thousands of courageous Hungarians rose up for freedom and democracy. They wanted to break free from the bloody Soviet regime in Moscow. That uprising âwith actually many hundreds killed â reminds us of how precious freedom is and how important it is for us to defend it.

But autocrat Viktor OrbÃ¡n has other plans. He's dragging Hungary back into dark times. He's building a hybrid regime of electoral autocracy. OrbÃ¡n is, in fact, choking democracy in Hungary. And now this: multiple European media outlets report an alleged spying network within Hungary's EU representation in Brussels. The aim: to get sensitive information. It sounds indeed like a Cold War novel.

We urgently need to clarify whether these reports are true. Were Parliament and the Council also targeted? Was data maybe even passed on, for example, to other autocrats, like those in Moscow or Beijing? And what role did Commissioner VÃ¡rhelyi play? Is it possible that this close ally of OrbÃ¡n did not know about all this in his own unit, under his own watch?

And how did the Commission services react? Did they realise the seriousness of the situation and protect their officials, or did they choose to avoid a conflict with a Member State?

Colleagues, we need clarity on this. We need to have a full inquiry committee also here in this House, and we need it now. Because if spying did take place and sensitive data was leaked, this poses a severe security risk for us in the European Union. Then the Commission and Member States must decisively act not only within the Commission, but also in the Council, and immediately suspend Hungary's voting rights.

Enough is enough, colleagues. It is time for Europe to stand up to this autocratic bully.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Tomasz Froelich, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin! Immer wieder geht es gegen Ungarn. Warum? Weil OrbÃ¡n das verteidigt, was Sie bekÃ¤mpfen: christliche Werte, Familie, nationale SouverÃ¤nitÃ¤t und Frieden. Seine FriedensbemÃ¼hungen haben Sie torpediert, weil Sie lieber Krieg wollen. Sie schlieÃen ungarische Studenten vom Erasmus-Programm aus, Ã¶ffnen dieses aber fÃ¼r Studenten aus Tunesien, Ãgypten und Marokko. Sie decken Ilaria Salis und die Linksterroristen der âHammerbandeâ, nur, um OrbÃ¡n eins auszuwischen. Und Sie wollen uns etwas Ã¼ber Rechtsstaatlichkeit erklÃ¤ren? Ãber Moral? Das ist doch lÃ¤cherlichÂ â genauso lÃ¤cherlich wie diese Debatte hier heute.

Ungarn hatte bis 2017 ein Spionagenetz in BrÃ¼ssel. Das ist StandardÂ â haben viele Staaten. Vertreter der EU-Spionageabwehr sagten: Wir betrachten den Fall nicht als ernstÂ â keine Sicherheitsbedrohung. Vor acht Jahren ist dieses Spionagenetz aufgeflogen, und wir reden heute darÃ¼berÂ â nach acht Jahren? Ja, aus einem Grund: weil in sechs Monaten in Ungarn Wahlen sind. Wahlen, in die Sie sich mal wieder einmischen, nur um OrbÃ¡n zu verhindern. Aber ich garantiere, das wird Ihnen nicht gelingen. Der aktuelle Regierungschef Ungarns heiÃt Viktor OrbÃ¡n und der nÃ¤chste auch.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Juan Fernando LÃ³pez Aguilar (S&D). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, en mi calidad de presidente de la ComisiÃ³n de Libertades Civiles, Justicia y Asuntos de Interior, presidÃ­ varias misiones de informaciÃ³nÂ del Parlamento Europeo en HungrÃ­a y experimentÃ© personalmente la hostilidad hacia una instituciÃ³n europea, obligÃ¡ndome a recordar que el Parlamento Europeo no es ninguna nave exterior, ninguna nave espacial que sobrevuele HungrÃ­a, sino que representa de manera directamente democrÃ¡tica a diez millones de hÃºngaros. Y HungrÃ­a ha estado bajo el foco de este Parlamento Europeo, se ha activado el artÃ­culoÂ 7Â por violaciÃ³n de los valores consagrados en el artÃ­culoÂ 2, por ser reluctante al cumplimiento del Derecho europeo y rebelde frente a las sentencias del Tribunal de Justicia Europeo.

Y ahora sabemos que se ha dedicado a espiar desde la REPER, nada menos hÃºngara, en Bruselas, a las instituciones europeas, como han revelado las fuentes periodÃ­sticas dignas de todo crÃ©dito que han suscitado este punto en el debate. Las opciones estÃ¡n claras. Para empezar, la presidenta Von der Leyen tiene una herramienta âel artÃ­culoÂ 16 del Tratado de la UniÃ³n Europeaâ para forzar la dimisiÃ³n de un comisario que no se muestre a la altura. Porque no se examina solamente la competencia para una cartera sino la probidad y la integridad moral para ejercerla y responder ante este Parlamento. En su caso podrÃ­a imponÃ©rsele incluso el Tribunal de Justicia.

Pero realmente la cuestiÃ³n, la clave es: Â¿puede la UniÃ³n Europea permitirse por mÃ¡s tiempo un Gobierno en un Estado miembro que piensa que la UniÃ³n Europea es un territorio hostil al que deba infiltrarse practicando el espionaje? Y la respuesta tiene que ser rotundamente no. Por tanto, este es un caso mÃ¡s en el que se pone de manifiesto hasta quÃ© punto HungrÃ­a, invitando a Putin, rebelde ante el Derecho europeo y ante las sentencias del Tribunal de Justicia, se ha constituido en un problema sistÃ©mico para la UniÃ³n Europea.

Â

Â Â Jean-Paul Garraud (PfE). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, MmeÂ vonÂ derÂ Leyen est-elle obsÃ©dÃ©e par la Hongrie? L'acharnement politique de Bruxelles contre Budapest n'est plus une dÃ©rive, c'est une stratÃ©gie assumÃ©e. ViktorÂ OrbÃ¡n dirige un gouvernement lÃ©gitimement Ã©lu. C'est le peuple hongrois qui a tranchÃ©, que cela vous plaise ou non, et ce peuple est fier de son histoire, de son identitÃ©, de ses traditions, de sa souverainetÃ©, bref, tout ce que Bruxelles combat.

Hier, on accusait la Hongrie de bafouer votre conception fumeuse de l'Ã©tat de droit. Aujourd'hui, on parle d'espionnage. Et demain, jusqu'oÃ¹ ira ce dÃ©lire de stigmatisation?

Pendant que les Ã©tudiants hongrois sont injustement privÃ©s du programme Erasmus, la Commission europÃ©enne souhaite dÃ©sormais intÃ©grer Ã  ce programme des pays du Moyen-Orient et d'Afrique du Nord, comme l'AlgÃ©rie, oÃ¹ la situation des droits humains est loin d'Ãªtre exemplaire. L'Union europÃ©enne punit un Ãtat membre, mais rÃ©compense des rÃ©gimes autoritaires Ã©trangers. Quel scandale et quelle hypocrisie!

En octobreÂ 2025, la Hongrie subit toujours une astreinte hallucinante de 1Â million d'euros par jour, imposÃ©e par la Cour de justice de l'Union europÃ©enne pour avoir refusÃ© l'accueil obligatoire de migrants. Tout cela s'ajoutant Ã  une amende complÃ©mentaire de 200Â millions d'euros. VoilÃ  le prix de la souverainetÃ©. Et ce n'est pas tout.

Au nom du mÃ©canisme de conditionnalitÃ©, la Commission retient prÃ¨s de 19Â milliards d'euros de fonds europÃ©ens, pourtant dus Ã  la Hongrie, privant dÃ©libÃ©rÃ©ment un Ãtat membre et son peuple de leurs droits. Un vÃ©ritable chantage! Un scandale politique! La Hongrie, qui rÃ©siste, est le bouc Ã©missaire de la Commission. La Hongrie ne pliera pas. Tous les Patriotes europÃ©ens non plus.

Â Â Moritz KÃ¶rner (Renew). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Wo ist eigentlich die RatsprÃ¤sidentschaft in dieser Debatte? Da gibt es VorwÃ¼rfe, dass ein Mitgliedstaat die EUâInstitutionen ausspioniert. Aber das ist den anderen Mitgliedstaaten egal? Das ist business as usual, da geht man mittlerweile schon zu Ã¼ber, das einfach so zu akzeptieren, als ob das vÃ¶llig normal wÃ¤re?

Und auch in der Kommission, muss ich ganz ehrlich sagen: Der Kommissar hat uns eben dargestellt, dass es offenbar eine Untersuchung, eine Ermittlung gibt. Man konnte ja von einem Sprecher hÃ¶ren, Frau von der Leyen hÃ¤tte sozusagen Herrn VÃ¡rhelyi zu sich kommen lassen und hat gesagt: âHast du spioniert?â Und dann hat er gesagt: âNein, ich habe nicht spioniert.â Und dann ist das gut. Es muss doch mindestens eine Suspendierung geben, bis die Untersuchungen abgeschlossen sind.

Wir gehen damit so um, als wÃ¤re das normal, und das ist das Schlimme, dass wir mittlerweile zulassen, dass ein Mitgliedsstaat offen gegen die EU-Institutionen vorgeht, dass wir uns an Spionage und all diese Dinge gewÃ¶hnen mÃ¼ssen und dass es eben nicht das Konzept der Kooperation zwischen den Mitgliedstaaten gibt, dass die Rechtsstaatlichkeit in der EuropÃ¤ischen Union StÃ¼ck fÃ¼r StÃ¼ck kaputt gemacht wird. Und das dÃ¼rfen wir nicht zulassen, meine Damen und Herren.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Tineke Strik (Verts/ALE). â Madam President, from Russia with love in our own Union. Who could have imagined that? For Hungary, the EU is the big enemy ten years ago, but it still is.

Think, for example, about how OrbÃ¡n designates EU funding as foreign interference in his draft anti-NGO law, or his friendship and collaboration with war criminals like Putin and Netanyahu, whom he happily invites into our Union's territory.

And to make things worse. Hungary's espionage seems to have happened under the leadership of Commissioner VÃ¡rhelyi, of now Commissioner VÃ¡rhelyi.

Our Parliament already called for investigations into Commissioner VÃ¡rhelyi's conduct as Commissioner several times, where he prioritised OrbÃ¡n's interests above those of the Union. This scandal is the last straw. With the enemy within, the EU's functionality and credibility is under serious threat.

So Commission, Parliament and Council conduct a thorough investigation and take the only right action if these revelations appear true.

Â Â Alexander Jungbluth (ESN). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin! Â Â AdriÃ¡n VÃ¡zquez LÃ¡zara (PPE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, durante los Ãºltimos aÃ±os hemos debatido mucho en este Parlamento sobre el espionaje como una forma de injerencia extranjera que amenaza el futuro de nuestra UniÃ³n. Sin embargo, resulta increÃ­ble que hoy tengamos que estar aquÃ­ debatiendo un presunto caso de espionaje cometido no por una potencia extranjera, sino por un Estado miembro de la UniÃ³n contra la propia UniÃ³n.

Mientras en esta CÃ¡mara y en las capitales europeas âpor lo menos en la mayorÃ­aâ trabajamos para intentar mejorar la vida de nuestros ciudadanos, en Budapest hay un Gobierno que se dedica a debilitar nuestras instituciones y a sembrar desconfianza dentro de nuestras fronteras.

Viktor OrbÃ¡n ha decidido jugar a intentar ser el caballo de Troya de MoscÃº dentro de la UniÃ³n Europea. Y ahora parece, tambiÃ©n, que sus tÃ¡cticas para socavar las instituciones europeas son importadas, presuntamente, del Kremlin.

SeÃ±or comisario, mÃ¡s allÃ¡ de las acciones que estÃ¡n tomando âlas legales, las de investigaciÃ³n en curso que hay que llevar a cabo sobre las actividades de la RepresentaciÃ³n Permanenteâ a mÃ­ lo que me sorprende es que no hayan hecho lo mÃ­nimo, que es pedir la suspensiÃ³n del seÃ±or VÃ¡rhelyi de toda actividad hasta que no sepamos hasta quÃ© punto estaba involucrado en esto. Eso es lo mÃ­nimo que tiene que hacer la ComisiÃ³n en los prÃ³ximos dÃ­as.

Para terminar, solo querÃ­a intentar sacar una parte positiva de todo esto y es que esperemos que este sea el Ãºltimo escÃ¡ndalo de un Gobierno que pronto serÃ¡ echado en las urnas en las prÃ³ximas elecciones nacionales de HungrÃ­a.

Â Â Elio Di Rupo (S&D). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, mÃªme avec mes adversaires, mÃªme avec mes ennemis, il y a un principe dÃ©mocratique que j'ai toujours voulu mettre au fronton de ce que je fais, c'est la prÃ©somption d'innocence. Et donc je ne vais pas changer.

Seulement, ce qui est dit et Ã©crit dans la presse mÃ©rite un examen approfondi, et je me rÃ©jouis de voir MmeÂ Loiseau qui se prÃ©pare. Elle prÃ©side justement la commission spÃ©ciale sur le bouclier dÃ©mocratique europÃ©en, et je pense que Ã§a pourrait Ãªtre cette commission qui essaie de comprendre et de voir si, oui ou non, nous avons des Ã©lÃ©ments tangibles, des tÃ©moignages qui permettraient d'aller plus loin.

Si Ã§a permet d'aller plus loin, dans ces conditions-lÃ , et la Commission, d'une part, et peut-Ãªtre le Parlement avec une commission d'enquÃªte, d'autre part, et/oÃ¹ la justice doivent absolument s'en prÃ©occuper. Si ce n'est pas vrai, ce n'est pas vrai; si c'est vrai, c'est d'une gravitÃ© extrÃªme, et alors il faut aller jusqu'Ã  la suspension du pays. Mais je pense qu'il y a une mÃ©thodologie, il y a un respect des procÃ©dures que nous devons absolument mettre en Åuvre.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Paolo Borchia (PfE). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, non Ã¨ sfiducia nei vostri confronti â ci mancherebbe â perÃ² mi sia permesso di dubitare lievemente della buona fede di questo dibattito, perchÃ© dei presunti fatti accaduti otto anni fa, stranamente, casualmente, emergono a pochi mesi dalle elezioni in Ungheria.

Ma, colleghi, se noi pensiamo di impegnare quest'Aula ogni volta che emergono delle indiscrezioni giornalistiche â guardate â abbiamo finito di lavorare! Siamo seri, perchÃ© di solito funziona che, prima si prova un'accusa, si ricostruisce come sono andate le cose, si traggono le conclusioni e poi, eventualmente, c'Ã¨ un dibattito in Aula.

Invece, dobbiamo renderci conto che un governo europeo stravotato da parte di un popolo, quello ungherese, Ã¨ sotto attacco, costantemente, da parte di questo Parlamento. Dobbiamo dirci la veritÃ ? Prendete gli ordini del giorno delle ultime plenarie e vediamo quante volte e per cosa si Ã¨ parlato di Ungheria.

Adesso, Ã¨ giusto che ci siano le inchieste, Ã¨ giusto che ci siano gli approfondimenti del caso. Vediamo se, scavando, uscirÃ  qualche "James Bond" o qualche "Mata Hari" in salsa ungherese. Ma finchÃ© si scava, cerchiamo di impegnare quest'Aula con argomenti seri e che esistono e non con supposizioni.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Nathalie Loiseau (Renew). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, je plains le peuple hongrois. Je le plains parce que c'est un grand peuple et que ViktorÂ OrbÃ¡n le reprÃ©sente bien mal. Je le plains parce qu'il a appris, comme nous, que ViktorÂ OrbÃ¡n aurait fait espionner les institutions europÃ©ennes, comme si la Hongrie Ã©tait l'ennemi de notre Union. Je le plains parce qu'il a appris, comme nous, que des drones partis de Hongrie avaient survolÃ© l'Ukraine, comme si Budapest Ã©tait l'ennemi de Kiev. Le peuple hongrois veut tout savoir des pratiques de ViktorÂ OrbÃ¡n et d'OlivÃ©rÂ VÃ¡rhelyi. Je veux l'y aider.

Sur le scandale de l'espionnage de la Commission europÃ©enne, il faut une commission d'enquÃªte. D'ici-lÃ , la commission spÃ©ciale que je prÃ©side recevra, leÂ 5Â novembre, les journalistes qui ont enquÃªtÃ© sur cette affaire. Retenez cette date.

Il est une autre date que je n'oublie pas. LeÂ 23Â octobre, demain, la Hongrie cÃ©lÃ©brera le dÃ©but du soulÃ¨vement de 1956. N'oublions jamais que le grand peuple hongrois s'est levÃ© contre l'oppression russe et qu'il a su combattre pour sa libertÃ©.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Daniel Freund (Verts/ALE). â Madam President, dear colleagues, Viktor OrbÃ¡n is afraid. He is afraid of anyone that criticises him, anyone that exposes the rampant corruption of his friends and family, the billions being stolen.

So, he uses all the tools that he has to fight everyone that could criticise, that could investigate his corruption. He goes after journalists, after civil society, he goes after academics, and it seems he is also afraid of the EU institutions.

He is spying, apparently, on Members of the European Parliament. I myself was attacked with spyware. It seems that he has been running a spy network out of the EU embassy of Hungary for years, trying to spy on other Member States, on the EU institutions.

This is not something you do among allies, among friends, among members of the European Union. When we found out that the Americans were spying on us, this Parliament launched an investigation. And we need to launch an investigation on this as well, because it's an EU Member State spying on us.

Commissioner, you need to look into this. I think just saying 'I had no idea what was happening in my embassy that I was running' is not a good excuse. Either he's lying or he's incompetent. We need to find out.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â DanuÅ¡e NerudovÃ¡ (PPE). â Madam President, colleagues, we already know about OrbÃ¡n's proximity to a bloody Russian president, but we didn't expect him to adopt the methods of Russian KGB. Sorry. Forgive me. FSB, as they call themselves nowadays.

Mr OrbÃ¡n allegedly offered Hungarian officials in the EU institutions to change, to become informants for his secret services.

Dearest colleagues, that is not a Netflix plot. It is how the Hungarian regime now operates. If you refuse to sign an informant contract and spy on behalf of the Hungarian Government, you can be branded as a traitor of your own country. After letting Russian citizens unchecked enter the Schengen Area last year, after using Pegasus spyware against the opposition, Mr OrbÃ¡n now seeks to spy on the institutions of which he is a part of.

What will be the next? Falling people from the windows in Brussels? We must stop him and thank you for all your efforts, Mr Commissioner.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Sandro Ruotolo (S&D). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, ricapitolando un attimo la vicenda â se ho capito bene â questo Ã¨ il dibattito sull'Europa delle spie, che non Ã¨ il titolo di un film di azione ma riguarda questa Commissione da vicino, da molto vicino.

Agenti dei servizi segreti ungheresi si sarebbero spacciati per funzionari della rappresentanza permanente, infiltrati nelle istituzioni tra il 2015 e il 2017, proprio quando OlivÃ©r VÃ¡rhelyi, oggi commissario europeo, ne era a capo. Se fosse tutto vero, ci chiediamo: ma VÃ¡rhelyi sapeva ed era complice o non sapeva?

Colleghe e colleghi, questa Ã¨ una vicenda che riguarda uno Stato membro dell'Unione europea e della NATO e che chiama in causa la responsabilitÃ  politica di un commissario attualmente in carica.

Alla luce di questi fatti, Ã¨ necessario che la Commissione risponda a due domande fondamentali: quale ruolo avrebbe avuto il commissario VÃ¡rhelyi in queste vicende? PerchÃ© la Presidente von der Leyen, in attesa della conclusione delle indagini, non sospende il commissario VÃ¡rhelyi?

Se la sicurezza delle istituzioni europee viene violata dall'interno, questa Ã¨ una minaccia diretta alla democrazia europea. E, per concludere, aspettando le "Mata Hari", io voglio esprimere la mia solidarietÃ  a Daniel Freund, spiato illegalmente dal regime di OrbÃ¡n.

Â Â Harald Vilimsky (PfE). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, meine sehr geehrten Damen und Herren! Wann immer hier von den Linken eine Debatte Ã¼ber Ungarn und Viktor OrbÃ¡n vom Zaun gebrochen wird und man sich in den VorwÃ¼rfen schon ins Absurde Ã¼berschlÃ¤gt, stelle ich mir die Frage, ob ich nicht die medizinischen Dienste hier im Haus kontaktieren sollte. Ich habe das GefÃ¼hl, wir haben bei manchen so etwas wie ein politisches Tourettesyndrom, wo man vÃ¶llig ohne Kontrolle hier gegen Ungarn und gegen Viktor OrbÃ¡n eine Politik treibt, die bar jeglicher RealitÃ¤t ist. Sie wissen, dass diese Spionagegeschichte, die so einen Bart hat, ein Unfug ist, ich weiÃ es, wir alle wissen, dass es ein Unfug ist.

Sie inszenieren das wenige Monate vor der Wahl, um Viktor OrbÃ¡n und der Fidesz zu schaden. Das wird Ihnen aber nicht gelingen. Aber kommen Sie bitte nicht mit einer Geschichte, die so einen Bart hat und nur so was von offensichtlich dazu dient, Ihrem Kunstkandidaten PÃ©ter Magyar, diesem Wunschkandidaten der BrÃ¼sseler Eliten, zu helfen, anstatt hier tatsÃ¤chlich eine saubere, anstÃ¤ndige und ehrliche Politik zu betreiben.

Â Â Helmut BrandstÃ¤tter (Renew). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Es gibt Mutige und weniger MutigeÂ â das ist halt einmal so. Der Herr OrbÃ¡nÂ â um bei OrbÃ¡n zu seinÂ â, der ist natÃ¼rlich einer der weniger Mutigen. Und er ist einer, der weder Europa mag noch mag er eine Gemeinschaft. Und deswegen ist es ganz logisch, dass er natÃ¼rlich gegen unsere EuropÃ¤ische Union auch spioniert. Und logisch ist auch, dass er Feindbilder sucht. Das ist Ã¼brigens die nÃ¤chste Idee: Was bekommt denn China dafÃ¼r? Was Russland dafÃ¼r bekommt, dass er hier den russischen Agenten spielt, das wissen wir. Wir wissen, dass er auch versucht hat, gegen die Ukraine zu agitieren. Und wir kennen ja auch GroÃungarn, er will natÃ¼rlich auch ein StÃ¼ck der Ukraine haben. Der Mann ist gefÃ¤hrlich, und das mÃ¼ssen wir endlich verstehen. Und ich verstehe natÃ¼rlich auch nicht, dass die anderen LÃ¤nder das nicht endlich aufgreifen.

Â Â Reinhold Lopatka (PPE). â MadamÂ President, dear colleagues, accounts of foreign espionage are unfortunately nothing new, but espionage contacted by a Member State of the European Union, targeting its own institutions to which it belongs â this is alarming. It strikes at the foundation of trust and cooperation that must underpin relations among Member States and within our institutions. These revelations come at a time when EU-Hungary relations are already fragile â we know it, strained by concerns about backlashes and backsliding of the rule of law and the Hungarian leadership proximity to Russia.

If the allegations are verified, this would represent yet another significant breach of trust by a Member State towards the European Union, and the European project rests entirely on trust and shared democratic values. No government within our Union can be permitted to turn European institutions into instruments of domestic or foreign agendas, which is obviously done by Hungary.

We must, therefore, act firmly to safeguard our democracy, defending it not only from external threats but also from those that arise within the European Union. And this questionÂ is a question: what was the role of Commissioner VÃ¡rhelyi when he served here as ambassador of Hungary? This is an open question.

Â Â Jorge BuxadÃ© Villalba (PfE). â SeÃ±ora presidente, coincido con mi colega Harald Vilimsky en que hay que reforzar el servicio mÃ©dico y psicolÃ³gico de la CÃ¡mara. Â Â Dirk Gotink (PPE). â Voorzitter, als Hongarije vandaag zou willen toetreden tot de Europese Unie, dan zou dat niet kunnen. Terwijl Orban paleizen bouwt, worden de ziekenhuizen, de scholen en de publieke voorzieningen voor gewone Hongaren met de dag slechter. Dat is de twijfelachtige eer van de door en door corrupte regeringen van Viktor OrbÃ¡n. Net toen we dachten dat het niet erger kon, blijkt dat de Hongaarse regering een netwerk van spionnen heeft ingezet tegen ons, tegen de partners die hun welvaart en vrijheid willen delen met alle Hongaren.

Laten we daarom geen halve maatregelen nemen: ten eerste moet het Parlement een onderzoek instellen. Ik weet niet of we kunnen aansluiten bij het onderzoek van de van de Europese Commissie? Van een eigen onderzoekscommissie verwacht ik eerlijk gezegd niet veel. Ten tweede moeten we de toegang van de vertegenwoordigers van de ambassade tot dit gebouw weigeren, in ieder geval totdat we duidelijk hebben en weten wie er nog betrokken is. Hetzelfde geldt voor de Commissaris, die nog steeds toegang heeft tot de belangrijkste informatie in de Europese Commissie enÂ het College. Iedereen doet mee. Ik vind dat dit moet stoppen, in ieder geval tot het einde van het onderzoek van de Europese Commissie.

Â Â

Â Â Hannes Heide (S&D). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, Herr Kommissar! Ausspionieren, Ãberwachen, AbhÃ¶ren, Hacken: Das System OrbÃ¡n kommt nicht aus den Schlagzeilen heraus. Illegale Ãberwachung mit Spionagesoftware, Laptops hacken, Telefone abhÃ¶ren, Ermittler des EuropÃ¤ischen Amtes fÃ¼r BetrugsbekÃ¤mpfung im VisierÂ â immer dreister, immer ungenierter, so scheint es.

Ungarische Geheimdienste sollen versucht haben, EU-Bedienstete in BrÃ¼ssel als Informanten anzuwerben. Verantwortliche streiten das gar nicht ab. Unfassbar! Wer nationale Geheimdienste auf eigene europÃ¤ische Institutionen ansetzt, systematisch Recht beugt, stellt sich gegen die EuropÃ¤ische Gemeinschaft und verstÃ¶Ãt gegen das im EU-Vertrag verankerte LoyalitÃ¤tsprinzipÂ â somit ein Musterbeispiel fÃ¼r eine Vertragsverletzung. Spontane Wortmeldungen

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Grzegorz Braun (NI). â MadamÂ President, so this is just another hate hour â all the bad guys in Europe concentrated in Hungary in this season. This is ridiculous. Obviously, if there was some kind of illegal intelligence activity, bring your cases to courts in France or in Belgium. That is your right.

But if there was no espionage, there should be, ladies and gentlemen, because you are the enemy. You are the enemy. You are a hateful, terrorising, warmongering organisation, and if that was up to me, in my country, I would order Polish services to infiltrate and, let's say, neutralise your activities as the chief enemy of free nations of all the countries in Europe. Down withÂ eurokoÅchoz.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI). â Madam President, it's a point of order regarding Rule 10(3) of the Rules of Procedure of the European Parliament, which says that we as Members, we shall not impose inappropriate behaviour.

In my personal opinion, threatening EU officials to neutralise them with the security services of a Member State is inappropriate behaviour, and I would ask the Bureau to look into this.

So much for my point of order. Thank you for that.

So, dear people of Europe, I think it doesn't matter so much what I say, but because whenever I speak just after Mr Braun, it's something better. One of our colleagues told us all to go to the medical service, and I think that's a very great idea because you can go to therapy there. I do this myself because of the stress. And you can also do this when you are under the impression that everyone is planning together, that they have something against you.

And no surprise for the AfD, like Mr Jungbluth, the people behind all this are people of Jewish descent once again. What we are seeing right now is nothing more than treachery. Treachery against Europe. Treachery against the European Union. Treachery against the people of Hungary. Because the people of Hungary democratically decided to be part of the European Union and to build together with us the European idea.

I think the Commission knows what to do. You already have a bunch of proceedings against OrbÃ¡n, and I just hope that you can conclude them all before he will be kicked out of office next year.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(Ende der spontanen Wortmeldungen)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Piotr Serafin, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, as always, I am honoured and privileged to be in this House and to take part in debates with you.

But frankly speaking, in normal circumstances, this debate should have never taken place. The mere fact that we are now discussing allegations that one of our Member States decided to spy on the institutions of our common Union is simply unthinkable. But we are here, and we are discussing it. This shows the gravity of the situation.

As I said at the beginning, the internal investigation on all allegations related to this case is ongoing. Today, it is my responsibility to ensure the confidentiality of this process. Today, operational considerations should prevail.

But rest assured, the Commission stands ready to inform you about the outcome of this investigation once it is completed.

And already today, I would like to thank you for your support in safeguarding staff members from espionage in all its forms, and I feel encouraged by your strong call to uphold the principle of loyal cooperation between Member States and the EU institutions. You cannot reconcile spying with this principle.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Die PrÃ¤sidentin. â Die Aussprache ist geschlossen.

Â 

19. UN Climate Change Conference 2025 in BelÃ©m, Brazil (COP30) (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â Die PrÃ¤sidentin. â Als nÃ¤chster Punkt der Tagesordnung folgt die Aussprache Ã¼ber.

â die Anfrage zur mÃ¼ndlichen Beantwortung an den Rat Ã¼ber die Klimakonferenz 2025 der Vereinten Nationen (COPÂ 30) in BelÃ©m (Brasilien) von Antonio Decaro, LÃ­dia Pereira, Annalisa Corrado, Emma Wiesner, PÃ¤r Holmgren, Catarina Martins im Namen des Ausschusses fÃ¼r Umwelt, Klima und Lebensmittelsicherheit (O-000028/2025 â B10-0011/2025) (2025/2666(RSP)) und.

â die Anfrage zur mÃ¼ndlichen Beantwortung an die Kommission Ã¼ber die Klimakonferenz 2025 der Vereinten Nationen (COPÂ 30) in BelÃ©m (Brasilien) von Antonio Decaro, LÃ­dia Pereira, Annalisa Corrado, Emma Wiesner, Catarina Martins, PÃ¤r Holmgren im Namen des Ausschusses fÃ¼r Umwelt, Klima und Lebensmittelsicherheit (O-000029/2025 â B10-0012/2025) (2025/2666(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Mohammed Chahim, author. â MadamÂ President, allow me to start to introduce the oral question on behalf of the ENVI Committee:

'Minister Bjerre, Commissioner Hoekstra, dear colleagues, to achieve an outcome from the UN Climate Change Conference (COP30) that meaningfully advances the agenda on mitigation, adaptation, finance and loss and damage, the EU needs to strengthen its climate diplomacy. What actions are the Commission and the Council taking individually to ensure that the COP30 in BelÃ©m concludes with significant progress, particularly in relation to the following three points:

1. raising the collective ambition in nationally determined contributions, in line with the goals of the Paris Agreement;

2. mobilising climate finance by the public and private sectors towards the goals agreed at COP29, including through innovative financing schemes and equitable burden-sharing mechanisms;

3. implementing and further strengthening the commitments and actions agreed in the first global stocktake, which forms part of the UAE Consensus?'

Commissioner, Council, colleagues, the climate conference lays ahead. In less than a month from now, we hope to reach a conclusion that will deliver on climate neutrality in 2050, on climate finance, on ending fossil fuel subsidies, on climate adaptation, indigenous rights, improving biodiversity and reducing greenhouse gases massively.

Parliament's draft motion for a resolution calls for the Union to lead by example and underlines the need for a science-based EU climate target for 2030. It highlights the triple planetary crisis of climate, biodiversity and pollution because these are interlinked and we need an integrated response. Importantly, it recognises that climate change is a threat multiplier. Climate diplomacy is building stability and building peace. I hope that when the resolution is adopted, the Commissioner and the Council feel that the Parliament stands ready to report a great outcome at the climate conference.

As global warming accelerates, we have no time to hesitate because we are reaching a scale of climate consequences which almost seems unimaginable. The Commissioner himself recently warned us of the collapse of the Gulf Stream. He rightfully called it a wake-up call that would start an era where we cannot prepare for what will come. With that news came just recently a scientific discovery on the coral reefs. They are close to almost irreversibly dying off, while more than a billion people depend on healthy corals. Just imagine these people seeking refuge elsewhere, as they rightfully would.

Our farmers already face increasing droughts, which causes more and more crop losses, not only here, but across the world. Harvests of coffee and cacao, but also close to home, grapes are noticeably delivering less and less. People feel this directly. Prices are rising.

There are no quick fixes. There is no clever trick or path to become climate-neutral. It sure will be hard. It tests our society's capacity to innovate, to adapt, to stay at course. Because each compromise on climate, each delay on environment will hit us like a boomerang. Stalling our efforts now would only mean accelerating the climate crisis. There is no scenario where the cost of actions outweighs those of inaction, and action delivers not only for climate but also for our economy. Decarbonising and competitiveness go hand in hand. It offers sustainable growth in every sense of the word.

We have to address the elephant in the room. We must avoid that Europe shows up empty-handed, and I have to say, I have a lot of trust in this Council, the Presidency of Denmark, and this Commissioner. Because what will we tell those island states that are shrinking year by year beneath rising seas, to countries facing droughts that destroy livelihoods, to the regions overwhelmed by floods, without the means to prevent such catastrophes? I'm afraid that we will disappoint them while we know all too well what lies ahead because their present is our future, and our future is not that far away. Today we remember the DANA floods in Spain from last year. This summer, wildfires raged through Portugal, while in July, heatwaves claimed hundreds of lives in Romania, Bulgaria, Greece and Cyprus alone. There would not be a more cynical time for Europe to arrive at the climate conference empty-handed.

But again, with this Commissioner, I have confidence that it will not happen â because not so long ago, we led, we guided, we inspired and we united, we built alliances with countries and shared our ambition. We show that climate action works by bringing down emissions in record time. This is not the time to stall our efforts.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â Madam President, honourable Members, Commissioner, this year's climate conference, COP30, will offer an opportunity to mark the 10th anniversary of the Paris Agreement. It will also be an opportunity to underscore the global community's commitment to the ambition of the agreement. Finally, COP30 must address the collective level of ambition and implementation to keep 1.5 degrees within reach.

The Paris Agreement has made a difference. The agreement has pushed global climate action by calling on parties to set their climate targets, advance in adaption plans and mobilise climate finance. The projections for global temperature rise are now lower than before the Paris Agreement, but reaching the 1.4-degree goal of the agreement will be difficult. COP30 should be a key milestone in our efforts to that end.

The Council outlined its overall position for COP30 in Council conclusions, which were approved yesterday. Allow me to underline our main priorities. The EU believes that the COP should address the global level of climate ambition. According to the Paris Agreement, parties are expected to submit their new climate commitments ahead of COP30, the so-called nationally determined contributions (NDCs).

It is likely that there will be a gap in the ambitions to reach the 1.5-degree goal. It is important that COP30 provides a clear response to the NDCs, addressing the level of ambition and implementation.

The EU's position is that this would require a dedicated political space, a negotiated outcome that addresses the collective level of ambition and implementation. This is our clear call to the Brazilian COP Presidency, which is currently considering how to address the topic.

As the Presidency, we are also firmly committed to working towards a submission of the next EU NDCs ahead of COP30.

Linked to the priority of climate ambition is the implementation of the commitments agreed within the first global stocktake at COP28. We agreed to transition away from fossil fuels, to treble renewable energy capability and to double the annual rate of improvements in energy efficiency.

In its outreach ahead of the COP, the EU has stressed the need to collectively implement all aspects of the first global stocktake.

Climate finance is another aspect raised in the oral question. More climate finance is needed to respond to the increasing impacts of climate change. After the agreement on the new climate finance goal of COP29, we expect the discussions this year to focus on a call for scaling the broader climate finance to USDÂ 1.3Â trillion annually by 2035.

The EU will approach these discussions in line with its position as a leading contributor of climate finance.Â In 2023, the EU provided EURÂ 28.6Â billion in public climate finance. We also mobilised EURÂ 7.2Â billion from the private sector.

The EU is committed to remaining a trusted partner in support of the countries most vulnerable to climate change. But the public finance from developed countries cannot and should not fill the gap alone. We need a broader range of contributors, more private finance and fewer barriers to financial flows.

A key deliverable for the Brazilian Presidency is progress on the global goal on adaption that was introduced by the Paris Agreement. COP30 is expected to adopt indicators to guide adoption action, identify challenges and capture successes at global and national level.

In line with these priorities, the Presidency, Member States and the Commission are working hard to shape the result of the COP30, and the European Parliament is an active participant in framing the EU priorities for the COP.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

PRZEWODNICTWO: EWA KOPACZ
WiceprzewodniczÄca

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Wopke Hoekstra, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, honourable Members and dear Minister, dear Marie, thank you very much for having this debate and also for the statements made by Mr Chahim and by you, dear Minister. And I think it was it was spot on that Mr Chahim started with remembering the horrible events of exactly one year ago, which was another, â and I'm going to stress that â another, sobering reminder of what is going on globally.

Unfortunately, Europe is very much at the receiving end of these horrible events. But with whomever you talk from wherever across the globe, these type of events are taking place everywhere and their frequency is going up â I was slightly disturbed by a sign that went up over there -, it is going up year by year, unfortunately.

The first the first thing Mr Chahim asked, and I think there was there is there is also a very important point, is how do we navigate in this hugely complex geopolitical world and how do we continue to engage with others? And it is true, every single COP I have been involved with, we have stressed how dramatically difficult the geopolitics of the time were. And still, if you think back about last year or the year before, it is fair to say that things didn't get any easier. An interlocutor I talked to recently said to me when I asked, well, how are things? He basically said, well, worse than last year, but better than next year. And that is pretty much the geopolitical situation we're in.

But there is no alternative than to continue to engage. And we are doing it, of course, with those very close to us like the British, the Australians, the Canadians, the South Koreans and many others who are very much in the same position as we are. We've also made huge efforts again and for all the right reasons, with our friends from the cities, most recently in New York with our friends and partners from Africa. Just recently, when I met many of them in New York, last week in Brazil, but also in August in Spain, when we had a very good conference with the Minister of India, who I had the pleasure to meet last week in Brazil, but also earlier this year in New Delhi itself. And of course, with China, where we were with a number of Commissioners earlier this year, and who we also had over in Brussels, and with whom we're going to have the MOCHA conference in just a couple of weeks.

So a lot of diplomacy that is going on because there is a lot at stake. I wanted to thank Parliament for the interaction we've already had, and I also wanted to thank you for the COP 30 resolution and for your call to continue to lead in international climate negotiations.

And leadership is very much about what you do and what others can expect of you. But let me stress once again that leadership is also very much about what you articulate, about what you expect from others.Â And it is important that we also bring the message forward that with the type of world we're living in, solidarity and reciprocity do need to go hand in hand.

We're ten years on from the Paris Agreement. On the one hand, we're very much delivering, and at the same time we are not delivering enough. Yes, we are on track to 2.1 degrees and we must collectively accelerate action to keep the global temperature increase below 1.5. And that is why we need to deliver new, ambitious, economy-wide national determined contributions ahead of COP 30.

Now, as the minister just said, the Energy Council yesterday adopted a set of conclusions that â just to be upfront about it â I very much like and subscribe to.

First, an outcome that acknowledges the findings of the UNFCCC and the IPCC Synthesis Report. And being clear about the ambition and the need to address the gap.

Secondly, on agreeing on the modalities of the UE dialogue, to follow up on the implementation of the first global stocktake.

Third, the fact that we are deciding on a way forward of the Sharm el-Sheikh dialogue on Article 2.1(c) of the Paris Agreement.

Fourth, the capturing in the Baku to BelÃ©m Roadmap of the 1.3 trillion. Hugely important, particularly in making sure that we bring others along.

Fifth, an agreement on the UAEâBelÃ©m Work Programme on adaptation indicators, because the problem has mushroomed into something that is so large that mitigation alone, unfortunately no longer will do the trick.

And sixth and finally, enhancing capacity to implement a just transition domestically.

That is a lot. And I'm not going to hide the fact that this is going to be complicated once again. Around 100 countries and then talking about NDC accounting for nearly 80Â % of global emissions, have submitted or announced their post-2030 climate goals. But despite that, we expect a level of collective ambition to be insufficient, insufficient to keep 1.5 degrees within reach. And that has very, very significant ramifications for everyone across the globe.

So we need a decision. And that is one of the main parameters we have to face in BelÃ©m, we need a decision on how to address ambition, implementation and implementation gaps, barriers and also opportunities. And we do need collective agreement and collective commitment to step up efforts in the near term.

Secondly, on the GST, forgive me for all the horrible abbreviations, but it is meaningful stuff that lies beneath it. We do like and we do ask for all aspects of the GST to be implemented in the relevant UNFCCC work streams. This is not a supermarket. You cannot have some of the elements we agreed on without the others. It is the full to do list for everyone involved. It's that simple.

Third, the NC QG, which basically means the money. Here, it is hugely important that the difficult agreement we managed to reach last year, we put it into practice and countries like the European Union basically live up to their commitments. They make it concrete, they make it actionable. They make it plannable, particularly for those in the Global South who desperately wait for the money to arrive and to be able to do the things they need to do with it.

Just to give you the numbers again, because it shows how the European Union is leading this effort. In 2023, we delivered roughly 30 billion in public climate funds, on top of the 7.2 billion mobilised from the private sector. And that makes us by far, by far the continent that takes the most responsibility. And I agree we should. But it is also clear that we do need others to join the party and to help out, because the European Union alone cannot solve this problem.

And then, I've mentioned that earlier today, to the esteemed Members of this Parliament as well, there is a huge opportunity hidden in the domain of carbon pricing that might not be part of the major negotiations, but it is a topic for which the time has come, and many of our friends globally have embarked on looking into it or already agreeing on it. And sometimes it's very similar to our ETS system, something that is a bit farther out. But pricing carbon is probably one of the most effective things that humanity can do to come up with a solution. And interestingly enough, interestingly enough, I think that is even true here to some degree, across the political spectrum, in many geographies, there is appetite for such a type of tool, and that is simply very good news.

So forgive me for being a bit long Chair, but this is going to be important. We have a lot of work to do ahead of the COP at the COP, and certainly after the COP, because then again, implementation really needs to take place. But we will give it our best. And we do invite nations from all over the globe, whether they're developed or developing, whether they have done a lot or not that much to join in and make sure we do tackle one of the largest problems humanity is currently facing.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â LÃ­dia Pereira, em nome do Grupo PPE. â Senhora Presidente, sobre o ambiente, Roger Scruton dizia que o mundo natural agora depende dos nossos esforÃ§os para o conservar. Quando procuramos consolaÃ§Ã£o na natureza, estamos a olhar para um espelho que criÃ¡mos para esse fim.

A COP30 nÃ£o Ã© apenas mais uma conferÃªncia, Ã© um momento decisivo, um daqueles raros momentos em que a humanidade se olha ao espelho e decide quem quer ser. Ã um encontro de vontades que se erguem perante o ceticismo, o imobilismo, e que acreditam â como dizia um poeta portuguÃªs, Fernando Pessoa â, que Â«Deus quer, o homem sonha, a obra nasceÂ».

A resoluÃ§Ã£o que hoje apresentamos Ã© a prova viva de que a Europa continua a liderar. Lidera com ambiÃ§Ã£o, com responsabilidade e com a coragem de quem sabe que o futuro nÃ£o se espera, constrÃ³i-se.

Cuidar do ambiente nÃ£o Ã© apenas salvar o planeta, Ã© redefinir o nosso propÃ³sito coletivo. Ã provar que podemos crescer e descarbonizar ao mesmo tempo, que podemos inovar, colocando o conhecimento ao serviÃ§o das pessoas, que podemos viver em comunhÃ£o com a natureza, respeitando os ecossistemas, protegendo as comunidades, com as pessoas no centro das polÃ­ticas.

Por isso, a resoluÃ§Ã£o que levamos Ã  COP30 propÃµe medidas muito concretas. Queremos continuar a reforÃ§ar a lideranÃ§a da UniÃ£o Europeia e garantir um financiamento climÃ¡tico justo, e adotar jÃ¡, sem demora, o compromisso climÃ¡tico para 2035. Queremos continuar a acelerar o fim dos combustÃ­veis fÃ³sseis e eliminar os subsÃ­dios perversos, promovendo mercados limpos e competitivos. Queremos mostrar que Ã©, de facto, possÃ­vel descarbonizar e crescer.Â Porque isto jÃ¡ estÃ¡ a acontecer. O meu paÃ­s, Portugal, Ã© a prova viva de que, com recordes de energia renovÃ¡vel e o fim do carvÃ£o, a ambiÃ§Ã£o e a aÃ§Ã£o podem andar de mÃ£os dadas.

A COP30 Ã© um teste Ã  nossa determinaÃ§Ã£o, Ã© um teste Ã  nossa capacidade de conjugar a ciÃªncia e a polÃ­tica, a ambiÃ§Ã£o, a coragem e o pragmatismo. Ã esta a nossa missÃ£o, Ã© este o nosso momento, e todos juntos podemos fazer nascer a obra.

Â Â HelÃ©ne Fritzon, fÃ¶r S&D gruppen. â Fru talman! Kommissionen. RÃ¥det. Den grÃ¶na omstÃ¤llningen Ã¤r under attack. USA har lÃ¤mnat Parisavtalet, och lÃ¤nder som tidigare varit ledande â som mitt land, Sverige â har nu en hÃ¶gerkonservativ regering som Ã¶vergett klimatuppdraget. Och det Ã¤r i en tid nÃ¤r vi nu mer Ã¤n nÃ¥gonsin behÃ¶ver ledarskap och politiskt mod, bÃ¥de lokalt, nationellt och globalt.

TyvÃ¤rr gÃ¶r EU i mÃ¥nga stycken precis tvÃ¤rtom. Vi ser hanteringen av EU:s klimatmÃ¥l till 2040. Europas hÃ¶gerregeringar bromsar. Beslutet har skjutits fram. Samma sak ser vi i parlamentet dÃ¤r fÃ¶rhandlingarna nu stÃ¥r still.

Â Â

Â Â OndÅej Knotek, on behalf of the PfE Group. â MadamÂ President, honourable colleagues, the COP is an excellent opportunity to share good and bad practices. And as EU is responsible for hardly 7Â % of global emissions, the Patriots for Europe agree that we shall help the largest emitters of greenhouse gases to reduce their emissions. I was thinking that the best way, maybe, is perhaps to advise them what they should do if they wanted to fail in the climate transition.

I started to write the following list: make your Green Deal a religion, not a flexible economy tool; do not respect technology neutrality because market-based solutions are for kids anyway; make your carbon price most expensive worldwide; if possible, close as soon as possible all your stable fossil power plants and, of course, even if you have no adequate replacement available; don't allow alternative scientific opinion and politicians opposing your religion labelled as extremists; don't be at all stressed if your strategical industry leaves your country or continent, and the last and most important, use people money for PR campaigns saying that all of this is great.

And at this stage of writing, I realised how happy I am that this is definitely not the way that we do in Europe. Enjoy the COP, ladies and gentlemen.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Emma Wiesner, on behalf of the Renew Group. â Madam President, where is the climate leadership? I am genuinely worried that the Paris Agreement would not be possible today â the climate leadership has disappeared.

Our next climate conference is approaching, but the EU is still empty handed. Where is our climate target? Where is our contribution to the global COP agenda?

And at the same time, country after country failed to reach their climate goals, and they're all pretending that it's not a big deal. But the targets of Sweden, of Denmark, of France, Romania, Bulgaria and Germany, they sum up to the EU target and the EU target is aligned according to the Paris Agreement. So when your country fails on your national targets, you are failing on the EU target and you are betraying the Paris Agreement.

I am going to Brazil with a clear message to Ulf Kristersson and the other heads of state and government who avoid responsibility: it's time to wake up. It is time to show leadership and show that you are supporting the Paris Agreement.

And to you, Hoekstra, good luck in BelÃ©m. And it does not make sense that the global stocktake only takes place every fifth year. This House demands more frequent follow-up. Good luck with that.

Â Â Catarina Martins, em nome do Grupo The Left. â Senhora Presidente, Senhor ComissÃ¡rio, em agosto de 2024, o mundo atingiu pela primeira vez mais dois graus centÃ­grados. A Europa Ã© o continente que aquece mais depressa. No meu paÃ­s, em Portugal, os fogos no verÃ£o nunca nos deixam esquecer que as alteraÃ§Ãµes climÃ¡ticas sÃ£o o nosso presente.

HÃ¡ dez anos, o Acordo de Paris selou a promessa global de todos os esforÃ§os serem feitos para nÃ£o ultrapassarmos um grau e meio. NÃ£o aconteceu. O petrÃ³leo falou sempre mais alto.

Vamos para a trigÃ©sima COP, e haverÃ¡ muita gente que se pergunta se ainda podemos esperar alguma coisa. Perante o negacionismo de Trump, a inaÃ§Ã£o da ComissÃ£o Europeia e dos governos, a resoluÃ§Ã£o do Parlamento nÃ£o desiste, e bem, de mudanÃ§as efetivas de uma estratÃ©gia para o fim do fÃ³ssil. Mas hÃ¡ um recuo trÃ¡gico: a militarizaÃ§Ã£o como exceÃ§Ã£o e que pÃµe em causa todos os objetivos.

Os sinais de esperanÃ§a global chegamânos hoje dos movimentos ambientais. Na COP30, no Brasil, os povos indÃ­genas fazemâse ouvir e, nos compromissos parlamentares feitos, hÃ¡ uma secÃ§Ã£o dedicada que reconhece os seus direitos, o seu papel imprescindÃ­vel.

Convido os deputados e deputadas a votarem este compromisso que nos chega da CÃºpula dos Povos, e pergunto ao senhor comissÃ¡rio se terÃ¡ tempo para reunir com a CÃºpula dos Povos e conhecer as suas exigÃªncias, ou se todo o seu tempo serÃ¡ para o lÃ³bi do petrÃ³leo.

Â Â Peter Liese (PPE). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Klimaschutz bleibt eine PrioritÃ¤t. Trotz aller Krisen sind wir es unseren Kindern und Enkelkindern schuldig, dass wir uns um das Thema kÃ¼mmern. Aber wir mÃ¼ssen stÃ¤rker darauf achten, dass wir wirklich Vorbild sind fÃ¼r andere in der Welt. Denn nur weltweit kann erfolgreicher Klimaschutz gewinnen.

Wir sollten an den Zielen festhalten, die wir bereits beschlossen haben. Aber wir mÃ¼ssen unsere Politik Ã¤ndern: Wir mÃ¼ssen technologieneutral werden. Wir mÃ¼ssen flexibler werden, pragmatischer, Industrie und Menschen besser mitnehmen, als das bisher gelungen ist. Und dann geht eben nicht alles auf einmal. Da mÃ¼ssen manche Dinge, die nichts mit Klimaschutz zu tun haben, vielleicht auch mal zurÃ¼ckstehen in der Umweltpolitik.

Der Text ist gut. Alexandr Vondra, vielleicht wÃ¤re der Text noch besser, wenn deine Fraktion da mal dabei geblieben wÃ¤re und mitverhandelt hÃ¤tte. Hinterher meckern ist nicht konstruktive Politik. Also bitte an den Tisch, dann wird er auch noch besser.

Liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, das Pariser Klimaabkommen lebt. Nur die USA sind ausgeschieden. Auch Argentinien ist weiter drin, auch weil die KommissionÂ â Ursula von der Leyen, Wopke und andereÂ â darauf bestanden haben: Mercosur gibt es nur, wenn das Pariser Klimaschutzabkommen eingehalten wird. Das Problem, das wir haben, ist mit den zukÃ¼nftigen Zielen fÃ¼r 2035 und 2040. Viele bei uns in der FraktionÂ â ich auch â tun sich schwer mit den 90Â Prozent.

Aber wir brauchen jetzt schnell einen NDC. Und in der EntschlieÃung steht, dass das am oberen Ende der Range sein muss. Und die EVP wird das unterstÃ¼tzen. Wir brauchen einen ambitionierten NDC und wir brauchen ihn ganz schnell. Sonst brauchen wir gar nicht erst nach BelÃ©m zu fahren. Also lasst uns morgen dafÃ¼r abstimmen.

Â Â Javi LÃ³pez (S&D). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±or comisario, el mundo tiene una cita de nuevo con su futuro, esta vez en BelÃ©n, en la CP30. En un multilateralismo hoy dislocado va a tener que afrontar la acciÃ³n climÃ¡tica global bajo el liderazgo de Brasil.

Nuestro primer objetivo es evaluar los compromisos actualizados y saber si son suficientes para cumplir con el objetivo de grado y medio de aumento de temperatura en la Tierra. Y sabemos que lo que se ha hecho hasta ahora ha sido poco y tarde. Y tambiÃ©n sabemos lo que tenemos que hacer: descarbonizar, reducir drÃ¡sticamente las emisiones, especialmente el CO2 y el metano, apostar por las energÃ­as renovables, duplicar nuestra eficiencia energÃ©tica y desconectarnos de los combustibles fÃ³siles.

TambiÃ©n servirÃ¡ para hablar de adaptaciÃ³n y financiaciÃ³n, dos clÃ¡sicos de las conversaciones climÃ¡ticas. De adaptaciÃ³n porque es especialmente importante para Europa, sabiendo que vamos a ser el segundo bloque mÃ¡s afectado despuÃ©s del Ãrtico en aumento de la temperatura en la Tierra. Y de financiaciÃ³n porque tenemos que hacer realidad la hoja de ruta de BakÃº a BelÃ©n movilizando 1,3 billones de dÃ³lares anuales, que ese fue el compromiso.

Europa tiene que llegar allÃ­ con los deberes hechos para tener credibilidad internacional, es decir, tener CDN para 2035 ambiciosas y, sobre todo, acordar una ley del clima para 2040 con un target que sea el 90Â % de reducciÃ³n de las emisiones domÃ©sticas, como nos dice la ciencia.

No solo la comunidad internacional tomarÃ¡ nota, tambiÃ©n las empresas. Tenemos que enviar un mensaje potente de nuestra ambiciÃ³n climÃ¡tica para poder canalizar inversiones los prÃ³ximos veinte aÃ±os en la Agenda verde.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Silvia Sardone (PfE). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, le politiche dell'Unione europea hanno aiutato la Cina a emergere nel settore delle tecnologie verdi.

Chi l'ha detto, Commissario? L'ho detto io? No. L'ha detto qualcuno pericoloso dei Patrioti? No. Qualche pericoloso sovranista? Nemmeno. L'ha detto Ursula von der Leyen: dopo sei anni di sua guida disastrosa, ha fatto questa rivelazione in una lettera in cui parla di strategia per la competitivitÃ  dell'Europa.

In sostanza, chi ha consegnato alla Cina il dominio sulle energie verdi pretende ancora di spiegarci come rilanciare l'industria europea. Eppure Ã¨ stata proprio lei, spinta dai fanaticiÂ green, a trascinare il settore dell'auto verso il baratro.

Ora, alla vigilia della COP 30, ci prepariamo a imporre nuovi obiettivi irrealistici, mentre Paesi come la Cina e gli Stati Uniti seguono tutt'altra strada. Lei, prima Commissario, parlava di reciprocitÃ . Ecco: non esattamente quello che sta accadendo.

Dopo queste ammissioni, von der Leyen dovrebbe solo dimettersi e non continuare a guidare l'Europa verso il fallimento produttivo.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Beatrice Timgren (ECR). â Fru talman! COP30 hÃ¥lls i BelÃ©m, i utkanten av Brasiliens regnskog. Ãnnu ett klimattoppmÃ¶te dit femtio tusen deltagare flygs ner frÃ¥n hela vÃ¤rlden och bor pÃ¥ hotell fÃ¶r 20Â 000 kronor per natt.

COP30 handlar allt mindre om klimatet och mer om vem det Ã¤r som ska betala. FN talar om ett finansieringsgap pÃ¥ 11Â biljoner kronor per Ã¥r som ska gÃ¥ till utvecklingslÃ¤nder â en summa stÃ¶rre Ã¤n EU:s hela Ã¥rliga budget. USA lÃ¤mnar. Kina, vÃ¤rldens stÃ¶rsta utslÃ¤ppare, de vÃ¤grar betala. De rika oljelÃ¤nderna i Gulfen, de betalar inte heller. Och dÃ¥ Ã¤r det vi i Sverige och Europa som ska stÃ¥ fÃ¶r notan.

Men Sverige Ã¤r inte lÃ¤ngre ett land som betalar alla andras rÃ¤kningar. VÃ¥ra pengar, de behÃ¶vs hÃ¤r hemma; till pensionÃ¤rer, till sjukvÃ¥rd, till vÃ¥r sÃ¤kerhet.

Vill man rÃ¤dda klimatet dÃ¥ satsar man pÃ¥ innovation, inte korruption.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Brigitte van den Berg (Renew). â Voorzitter, 400Â miljard EUR.Â Zoveel betaalt Europa jaarlijks voor fossiele energie uit landen buiten ons continent. 400Â miljard EUR die wegvliegt naar oliesjeiks en vaak ook naar ondemocratische leiders. En wie draait daarvoor op? De gewone Europeaan, die een torenhoge energierekening betaalt, en onze industrie, die gebukt gaat onder die gigantische energieprijzen.

Dit terwijl we allang weten dat groene energie de goedkoopste energie is. Daarom is het nu tijd om te kappen met kolen en te kiezen voor een toekomst zonder fossiele brandstoffen. Er is geen weg terug. Er is alleen de weg vooruit. En die weg vooruit leidt naar schone, Europese energie.

Ten eerste zijn we daarmee goedkoper af, ten tweede minder afhankelijk en ten derde bouwen we zo onze cleantech-industrie op waarin Europa een leidende rol kan spelen in de wereld.

En nu is het tijd voor de klimaattop en is het tijd dat we de rest van de wereld ook weer meekrijgen in een stap vooruit.

Vier jaar geleden hadden we het tijdens de klimaattop al over het uitfaseren van fossiele energie en het uitfaseren van kolen. Maar ondertussen blijkt dat China en heel veel andere landen niet alleen investeren in cleantech, maar ook nog altijd âvrolijkâ kolencentrales bouwen.

Daarom is mijn vraag aan de Commissie en aan de heer Hoekstra: wat is uw strijdplan om te kappen met kolen?

(De Voorzitter ontneemt de spreker het woord)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lena Schilling (Verts/ALE). â Madam President, dear colleagues, once upon a time, we had climate ambition. But it's not 'once upon a time': ten years ago, the leaders promised with the Paris Agreement to care about the future of the next generations, the future of the children of Europe.

And today, we are having this debate. We know the forests are on fire, harvests are failing, and people are dying of heat in our cities. And we in this House, what are we doing? Are we showing this leadership that we like to talk about? I would say no.

We are standing there with empty hands, with nothing more than a letter of intent for our own climate targets. We don't have a 2040 target in place, and it would be so clear. It would be so clear â the science is clear, what we need to do.

There is a letter signed by 2Â 000 scientists saying that we need 90Â % decarbonisation in 2040. But it's also clear what we need to do for our climate targets.

And I'm standing here as a person who marched with thousands â thousands and even millions around the world â of young people demanding climate ambition. My generation doesn't need any excuses. We need courage. We need truth. We need us to fucking act.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Radan Kanev (PPE). â MadamÂ President,Â dear Minister, dear Commissioner, dear colleagues, I'm afraid there is a challenge we are overlooking here in in this debate: the widespread feeling of many of our citizens â expressed, by the way, in a rather radical way, by our far-right colleagues â that the EU is making big sacrifices to no avail, that we are leading the way of climate ambition while our global competitors are following only to stab us in the back. There is a certain truth in this, we must admit.

But most importantly, we must address this public concern because it is serious. We might not agree with it, but we certainly need to communicate with the people who express it. And this, in my opinion, should happen exactly at the highest possible diplomatic level, which is the Climate Change Conference in Brazil coming in just a few days. This should happen in quite an open and sincere way, in my point of view, by saying that our climate ambition is strong â it is sincere, but it is hardly unconditional. Any new targets that we impose on our economy, society and industry should depend on China's, India's and Brazil's reduction. And I mean effective reduction of emissions, not only reduction emission pledges.

Â 
Â Â 
Â
