Â Â Sergio Berlato (ECR). â Signora Presidente, signor Commissario, onorevoli colleghi, per molti anni i profeti di sventura ci hanno messi in guardia dai pericoli del riscaldamento globale, causato â a loro dire â dalle attivitÃ  umane.

Adesso ci raccontano che sono le attivitÃ  umane a causare i cambiamenti climatici, ignorando che i cambiamenti climatici sono sempre avvenuti ed avverranno sempre, non certo a causa delle attivitÃ  umane ma a causa della diversa intensitÃ  dell'attivitÃ  solare.

La veritÃ  Ã¨ che la CO2 Ã¨ un elemento naturale, indispensabile per la vita sulla Terra e non una sostanza inquinante. La veritÃ  Ã¨ che le attivitÃ  umane producono appena il 4% di tutta la CO2 presente in atmosfera, mentre il rimanente 96% viene prodotto dai vulcani, dagli oceani, dal respiro di tutti gli esseri viventi.

Per quanto tempo siamo ancora disposti a farci ingannare da coloro che, per raggiungere i loro obiettivi, usano false certezze per terrorizzare le persone e le istituzioni?

(L'oratore accetta di rispondere a una domanda "cartellino blu")

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Brigitte van den Berg (Renew), âblauwe kaartâ-vraag. â De beweringen die u doet, worden door de wetenschap keer op keer weerlegd. Bovendien kostten overstromingen, hittegolven, droogtes en bosbranden afgelopen zomer alleen al 43Â miljard EUR aan economische schade.

Wat zegt u tegen alle mensen die hun woning verloren hebben, die zagen hoe hun leefomgeving werd verwoest?

Wat zegt u tegen die mensen wanneer u ontkent dat klimaatverandering een probleem is dat wij samen moeten oplossen?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sergio Berlato (ECR), risposta a una domanda "cartellino blu". â A tutte queste persone â e non solo a loro â dico: attenti a non farvi spaventare inutilmente da coloro che hanno interesse a terrorizzarvi per raggiungere i loro scopi.

I cambiamenti climatici sono sempre esistiti ed esisteranno sempre e non certo causati dalle attivitÃ  umane ma dalla diversa intensitÃ  dell'attivitÃ  solare.

Questo ci dicono i piÃ¹ eminenti scienziati a livello mondiale e non saremo certo noi a rincorrere i profeti di sventura che â ripeto â hanno interesse a terrorizzarci per raggiungere i loro scopi.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sigrid Friis (Renew). â MadamÂ President, Climate policy is so last year. At least, that's how it sounds when you listen to some priorities in this House.

But when did fighting for our planet's survival become something to hide away? When did climate action turn into something that could be torn apart in the name of simplification or deregulation? On our quest towards competitiveness, we cannot ignore the simple truth that economic strength and climate stability are two sides of the same coin.

Living up to the Paris Agreement can save us EURÂ 1Â trillion euro by 2030 in avoided damages. It cannot be clearer that Europe needs ambitious climate policy to stay competitive. Lower energy prices mean a safer Europe and mean a greener Union. This is one and the same fight.

Yet, the EU goes to COP without a legally binding 2040 target. That is embarrassing. We cannot leave our planet's future to OrbÃ¡n or Trump because, at this rate, the planet will be deregulated into oblivion.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Toussaint (Verts/ALE). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, chers collÃ¨gues, Monsieur le Commissaire. Je l'utiliserai pour dire que l'urgence n'est plus un concept abstrait, mais aujourd'hui une rÃ©alitÃ© brutale.

Des territoiresÂ ravagÃ©s, des foyers qui disparaissent et des vies Ã  jamais dÃ©truites. La science est claire. 2Â 000Â scientifiques viennent de nous le rappeler: rÃ©duire d'au moins 90Â % nos Ã©missions de gaz Ã  effet de serre d'ici 2040 est un impÃ©ratif. Mais l'Europe est en retard. Elle n'a toujours pas fixÃ© ses objectifs, ni pour 2040, ni pour 2035. Et ce retard est criminel.

Ne tuez pas l'accord de Paris, dÃ©bloquez l'Union et laissez-nous sauver le climat.

ÎÎ½ÏÎ¯ Î½Î± ÎºÏÎ½Î·Î³Î¬Î¼Îµ ÏÎ¿ ÏÎ¬Î½ÏÎ±ÏÎ¼Î± ÏÏÎ½ Î¼Î·Î´ÎµÎ½Î¹ÎºÏÎ½ ÎµÎºÏÎ¿Î¼ÏÏÎ½, Î±Ï Î±ÎºÎ¿Î»Î¿ÏÎ¸Î®ÏÎ¿ÏÎ¼Îµ Î¼Î¹Î± Î´Î¹Î±ÏÎ¿ÏÎµÏÎ¹ÎºÎ® Î´Î¹Î±Î´ÏÎ¿Î¼Î® ÏÏÎ¿Ï ÏÎ·Î½ Î¿Î¹ÎºÎ¿Î»Î¿Î³Î¹ÎºÎ® Î¹ÏÎ¿ÏÏÎ¿ÏÎ¯Î±, Î¼Îµ ÎµÏÎ¹ÏÏÏÎ¿ÏÎ® ÏÏÎ¹Ï ÏÎ±ÏÎ±Î´Î¿ÏÎ¹Î±ÎºÎ­Ï Î±Î¾Î¯ÎµÏ, Î¼Îµ ÎµÏÎ¹ÏÏÎ·Î¼Î¿Î½Î¹ÎºÎ® ÏÎµÎºÎ¼Î·ÏÎ¯ÏÏÎ· ÎºÎ±Î¹ ÏÏÎ¹ Î¼Îµ ÎµÏÎ¹Î²Î¿Î»Î® ÏÎ·Ï ÏÎ¿Î»Î¹ÏÎ¹ÎºÎ®Ï Î¿ÏÎ¸ÏÏÎ·ÏÎ±Ï ÏÎ¿Ï ÎµÎ¾ÏÏÎ·ÏÎµÏÎµÎ¯ ÏÏÎ¼ÏÎ­ÏÎ¿Î½ÏÎ± ÎºÎ¬ÏÎ¿Î¹ÏÎ½ ÎµÎ»Î¯Ï.

Â Â Letizia Moratti (PPE). â Signora Presidente, signor Commissario, onorevoli colleghi, oggi dobbiamo guardare in faccia a una realtÃ  difficile: per ogni tonnellata di emissioni tagliata nell'Unione europea nel 2024, il mondo ne aggiunge dieci.

Questo ci dice una cosa chiara: l'Europa deve essere la guida ma non puÃ² essere l'unico motore della transizione. Vogliamo un impegno collettivo, solidarietÃ , reciprocitÃ , come ha sottolineato il Commissario.

La COP 30 deve diventare una vera leva di coordinamento globale, perchÃ© la sfida climatica Ã¨ comune ma il peso oggi non lo Ã¨. Le conseguenze economiche sono giÃ  sotto i nostri occhi: 600.000 posti di lavoro persi â numeri che raccontano non solo di fabbriche che chiudono, ma di famiglie che faticano, di territori che si spengono.

Noi vogliamo un'Europa ambiziosa, sÃ¬, ma non isolata, un'Europa che sappia coniugare ambiente, economia e giustizia sociale, che usi la diplomazia e l'innovazione per coinvolgere il mondo intero, invece di portare da sola il peso della transizione.

L'Europa puÃ² e deve guidare il cambiamento con realismo, con equilibrio, nell'interesse delle proprie persone, delle proprie fabbriche, dei propri agricoltori, delle proprie comunitÃ .

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Niels Fuglsang (S&D). â MadamÂ President, dear colleagues, dear Commissioner, I want to congratulate you on the proposal on reducing greenhouse gases by 90Â % in 2040. I think it's the right proposal. I think it's a sound proposal.

And I think it is important, when we go to the COP meeting, that the European Union delivers ambition also to move other countries. We are struggling in the European Parliament to get your proposal through. I am leading the negotiations in the ITRE Committee, and as you can hear, Commissioner, there are lots of groups in this Parliament that either don't believe in climate change or don't think it's important that the Europeans should act.

So we need to work together with those that can â the S&D, the Greens, Renew, and hopefully the EPP. Actually, we're struggling with the EPP right now, so I hope that, EPP â your group â will come together and support your proposal.

We want to work and we need it. The European Union needs it. The climate needs it, and we need it for the COP meeting. So good luck to all of us.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â TomÃ¡Å¡ ZdechovskÃ½ (PPE), blue-card question. â I have just a question about why you are speaking about the EPP today. We saw your reliability â you promised us to vote on one issue with us and half of your group resign for this opinion.

Also, why are you saying that the European People's Party is not against climate change? We are a predictable partner. The problem is the Socialists. If you are not in, we will vote it with someone who has, with us, the majority.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Niels Fuglsang (S&D), blue-card answer. â I think when it comes to the vote today, it was mainly the extremes on the right and the left that voted the proposal down.

Unfortunately, it was a secret ballot so we could not see who voted. We didn't ask for this secret ballot. When it comes to the climate change proposal, there are lots of good people in the EPP. I work with them. I think you can be a reliable partner and ask you to be one.

We support the 90Â % in the S&D Group, and I hope you will work with us to do the same thing, because we would sure like to work with you.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Mathilde AndrouÃ«t (PfE). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, la COP 30 qui se tiendra Ã  BelÃ©m, au BrÃ©sil, en plein cÅur de l'Amazonie s'annonce dÃ©jÃ  comme une nouvelle grand-messe de l'hypocrisie climatique. Des milliers de responsables politiques, d'ONG et de lobbyistes s'y rendront en jet privÃ© pour expliquer aux peuples qu'ils doivent changer leurs comportements. Pendant ce temps, les grandes puissances continueront Ã  exploiter massivement leurs ressources fossiles. La Chine construira des centrales Ã  charbon et l'Union europÃ©enne Ã©touffera ses propres industries avec des taxes et des normes impossibles Ã  respecter.

Depuis des annÃ©es, ces COP s'enchaÃ®nent et les mÃªmes discours creux se rÃ©pÃ¨tent. Culpabiliser les EuropÃ©ens, multiplier les promesses intenables, financer des bureaucraties vertes, mais ne jamais traiter les vraies causes du dÃ©rÃ¨glement environnemental. DerriÃ¨re les grands mots, c'est toujours la mÃªme logique: dÃ©localiser notre production, importer plus, dÃ©pendre davantage. L'Ã©cologie, la vraie, ne se dÃ©cide pas dans des hÃ´tels climatisÃ©s de BelÃ©m. Elle se construit dans nos territoires, avec nos agriculteurs et nos ingÃ©nieurs, nos entreprises. Elle doit rimer avec souverainetÃ©, rÃ©alisme et bon sens, pas avec culpabilitÃ©, mondialisme et ruine Ã©conomique.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Laurence Trochu (ECR). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, sur une question aussi sÃ©rieuse que l'environnement et l'Ã©conomie de nos territoires, je dÃ©plore le fait que l'idÃ©ologie s'en mÃªle.

La rÃ©solution dÃ©posÃ©e sur la future COP30 ne fait qu'effleurer le nuclÃ©aire sans le prÃ©senter pour ce qu'il est, la seule solution pour une Ã©nergie peu coÃ»teuse, stable et dÃ©carbonÃ©e. Dans le mÃªme temps, l'Ã©galitÃ© des genres occupe une place de choix, dÃ©montrant ainsi l'intention idÃ©ologique d'un texte sans crÃ©dibilitÃ©.

Le funeste pacte vert, qui a brisÃ© la compÃ©titivitÃ© de l'Europe et lui a fait prendre un retard considÃ©rable sur la Chine et les Ãtats-Unis, imprÃ¨gne les intentions de cette rÃ©solution. Non, le changement climatique ne peut servir de prÃ©texte Ã  la sortie de l'histoire! Encourager l'innovation plutÃ´t que l'interdiction, l'Ã©cologie intelligente plutÃ´t que l'Ã©cologie punitive, le progrÃ¨s technique plutÃ´t que la dÃ©croissance, voici les principes qui devraient nous inspirer.

Les aveuglements desservent la cause de l'environnement, qui mÃ©rite d'Ãªtre pris davantage au sÃ©rieux. L'Ã©cologie de gauche abÃ®me, l'Ã©cologie de droite conserve.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Anna StÃ¼rgkh (Renew). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin! An die Rechten in diesem Haus: Sie sagen immer, Sie seien fÃ¼r die Menschen, aber jede Wortmeldung, die Sie hier abgeben, zeigt genau das GegenteilÂ â dass Ihnen offensichtlich die Menschen egal sind. Offensichtlich ist es Ihnen egal, wenn die Menschen kein Trinkwasser mehr haben, weil unsere Wasserreserven austrocknen. Offensichtlich ist es Ihnen egal, wenn tausende Menschen ihr Zuhause verlieren wegen der immer hungrigeren Flammen und unbÃ¤ndigen Fluten. Offensichtlich ist es Ihnen egal, wenn Ã¼ber 60Â 000Â Menschen wegen der Hitze sterben â Ã¤ltere, verletzliche Menschen, von denen Sie immer behaupten, sie schÃ¼tzen zu wollen.

Ihnen mag das egal sein, aber mir ist es nicht egal, ob meine Tochter noch eine Zukunft vorfindet, in der sie auch leben kann. Es ist zu spÃ¤t, dass wir nur noch das Minimum machen. Es ist Zeit, dass wir mutig, entschlossen und ehrgeizig sind. Wir haben unsere Zukunft immer noch selbst in der Hand, aber wir mÃ¼ssen auch dementsprechend handeln.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sara Matthieu (Verts/ALE). â Voorzitter, wie van jullie heeft een oliesjeik in de familie? Niemand. Maar wie heeft iemand in de familie die zonnepanelen op het dak heeft liggen? Bijna iedereen. Gewone mensen kiezen voor eigen groene energie, waarmee ze hun energierekening jaarlijks met 450Â EUR verlagen en onafhankelijk worden van olie en van gas.

Tientallen miljoenen Europeanen doen dit al, niet omdat politici het voorschrijven, maar omdat ze het zelf willen. Het succes van de klimaattop begint niet in vergaderzalen, maar gewoon op straat, waar mensen kiezen voor groene energie.

Rechtse politici houden jullie echter tegen: zij blijven subsidies pompen in fossiele brandstoffen en luisteren slaafs naar Trump, die ons afhankelijk wil houden van zijn gas.

Straks staan wereldleiders in BraziliÃ« voor een keuze: luisteren zij ofwel naar de fossiele lobby, ofwel naar die tientallen miljoenen mensen die vandaag al hun best doen voor het klimaat?

Laat Europa vooroplopen met ambitieuze klimaatdoelstellingen en een verdriedubbeling van de hernieuwbare energie. Niet voor de winsten van oliesjeiks, maar wel voor jou.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Martin GÃ¼nther (The Left). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, werte Damen und Herren! Wir werden die Klimakrise nicht lÃ¶sen, ohne auch die Ungleichheit zu bekÃ¤mpfen. Die reichsten ein Prozent verursachen mehr Emissionen als die Ã¤rmsten 66Â Prozent. Mehr Kapitalismus kann ein Problem nicht lÃ¶sen, das durch Kapitalismus forciert wurde. Die Klimakrise ist auch Folge kolonialer Ungerechtigkeit. Der Globale SÃ¼den leidet am stÃ¤rksten, obwohl er am wenigsten zur Erderhitzung beigetragen hat.

Die EU muss ihrer Verantwortung gerecht werden, wie es auch das Pariser Abkommen festlegt. Doch Deutschland, grÃ¶Ãter Emittent der EU, geht den falschen Weg: neue Gaskraftwerke, mehr GasfÃ¶rderung, weniger Klimaschutzgesetze und Tricksereien mit CO2âZertifikaten statt echter MaÃnahmen â das ist ein moralisches Versagen und ein klarer VerstoÃ gegen ArtikelÂ 2 des Pariser Abkommens. Und vor allem: Menschen mit geringem und mittlerem Einkommen werden am meisten unter den Folgen dieser verfehlten Politik leiden.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ingeborg Ter Laak (PPE). â Voorzitter, de boodschap van klimaatwetenschappers is helder. Klimaatverandering gaat ons de komende decennia hard raken. Dat vraagt ons om eerlijk te zijn over wat moet, maar ook hoopvol over wat kan. De uitdaging van de klimaatverandering zit hem niet enkel in geld of bereidheid, maar ook in de uitvoering.

De transitie naar een duurzame wereld vraagt om duidelijke politieke keuzes. Dat betekent ambitie en een ambitieus Europees klimaatbeleid, want het klimaat stopt niet bij de grens. Het betekent een keuze voor een stevige, tijdige en onvoorwaardelijke aanpak van de uitstoot van CO2. Een keuze voor de transitie naar schone energie op een manier dat iedereen mee kan doen. En het vraagt om een langetermijnvisie met voorspelbaar beleid, behapbare kosten voor inwoners en een gelijk speelveld voor ondernemers die verduurzamen.

Klimaatverandering is een opdracht voor ons allemaal: de samenleving, de overheid, de industrie. De overheid zal richting moeten geven, zodat bedrijven een duidelijke stip op de horizon hebben en kunnen en durven investeren. Mensen moeten het vertrouwen hebben dat hun inzet ook echt het verschil gaat maken. Alleen zo bouwen we samen aan draagvlak en versnellen we samen deze transitie. De klimaattop in BelÃ©m is hÃ©t moment om te laten zien dat Europa leiderschap koppelt aan realisme en uitvoerbaarheid, dat economische ontwikkeling en duurzaamheid elkaar versterken en dat we niet alleen goed zijn in het stellen van nieuwe doelen, maar vooral dat we de uitvoering laten werken.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â CÃ©sar Luena (S&D). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, en la intimidad de estos debates vespertinos, seÃ±or comisario, le voy a decir un refrÃ¡n espaÃ±ol: Â«A Dios rogando y con el mazo dandoÂ». Y es que estamos perdiendo credibilidad y liderazgo a chorros.

En el programa de trabajo de ayer he leÃ­do que quieren reabrir la prohibiciÃ³n de los motores de combustiÃ³n para 2035. Esto no es compatible con tener una imagen fuerte en la CP30, sobre todo por esa neutralidad tecnolÃ³gica que defienden. Van a abrir la puerta de nuevo a los combustibles fÃ³siles: error, error, error.

Ayer votamos la ley de vigilancia de los bosques y su Grupo se fue con la extrema derecha y la tumbÃ³: Â¡una propuesta suya, de la ComisiÃ³n! De nuevo, perdemos credibilidad.

En la ley de deforestaciÃ³n âen cuyas discusiones internas ha estado usted, en el Colegio de Comisariosâ el balÃ³n ha rozado el larguero. Vamos a ver cÃ³mo se portan aquÃ­ y en el Consejo.

Por tanto, el Pacto Verde hay que defenderlo aquÃ­. Y si no, no seremos creÃ­bles en las cumbres internacionales.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Gilles Pennelle (PfE). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, l'Europe a Ã©tÃ© le berceau de la dÃ©mocratie. D'ArchimÃ¨de Ã  Einstein, elle a inventÃ© les grandes dÃ©couvertes scientifiques. D'Aristote Ã  Voltaire, elle a irriguÃ© le monde de sa philosophie. Elle fut le berceau de la rÃ©volution industrielle. Elle a toujours Ã©tÃ© en pointe dans l'esprit pionnier.

Aujourd'hui, l'Europe de Mme von der Leyen n'est plus qu'une machine technocratique qui produit des normes climatiques, des normes punitives. C'est la seule chose que cette Europe, cette Union europÃ©enne, produit aujourd'hui. Cette Union europÃ©enne se soumet au diktat des confÃ©rences climatiques qui fonctionnent comme une scÃ¨ne morale oÃ¹ les pays les plus vertueux, c'est Ã  dire les pays europÃ©ens, sont sommÃ©s d'en faire toujours plus.

Vous avez renoncÃ© Ã  la puissance. Vous faites sortir, pour vous donner bonne conscience, l'Europe de l'histoire. Vous fabriquez chaque jour les instruments de notre effacement. Il est temps de tourner votre page.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Kristoffer Storm (ECR). â Hr. formand! Endnu et klimatopmÃ¸de. Endnu en runde tomme lÃ¸fter, store ord og formentlig ingen resultater. Det er, hvad vi kan forvente af klimamÃ¸det i Brasilien.

Jeg husker tydeligt, da den danske udenrigsminister, Lars LÃ¸kke, for seksten Ã¥r siden sad og halvsov under klimatopmÃ¸det i KÃ¸benhavn. Og hvad er der sket siden?

Vi har kastet milliarder og atter milliarder af Euro efter vÃ¦rdilÃ¸s klimaaktivisme. Og vi borgere har intet fÃ¥et ud af det. Til gengÃ¦ld smuldrer vores konkurrenceevne, migranter har oversvÃ¸mmet vores kontinent, Rusland har besat store dele af Ukraine, fordi vi ikke har afsat penge nok til forsvar, og almindelige europÃ¦iske familier har ikke lÃ¦ngere rÃ¥d til helt almindelig hverdagsmad pga. stigende priser.

Det er resultatet af at bruge alle vores penge pÃ¥ klimahysteri. Jeg har personligt fÃ¥et nok af at se vores penge blive smidt efter kaffemÃ¸der og "feelgood aftaler". Vi er nÃ¸dt til at vÃ¥gne op fra den grÃ¸nne feberdrÃ¸m, hvis vi skal passe pÃ¥ Europa og give vores bÃ¸rn en god fremtid, og det mener jeg bÃ¸r vÃ¦re det budskab, vi sender EU af sted med til klimatopmÃ¸det i Brasilien.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Oliver Schenk (PPE). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, Herr Kommissar, meine Damen und Herren! In wenigen Wochen beginnt die Klimakonferenz COP 30 in BelÃ©m. Dort wird die Weltgemeinschaft Ã¼ber ehrgeizige Ziele sprechen, und das ist gut. Aber entscheidend ist, ob wir sie auch erreichen. Europa ist stark im Setzen von Zielen, aber wir mÃ¼ssen auch stark in der Umsetzung werden. Ehrgeiz allein reicht nicht. Wir brauchen Mut zur ErmÃ¶glichung, weniger BÃ¼rokratie, mehr Anreize, mehr Vertrauen in Innovation und Technologie. Klimaschutz entsteht nicht durch Verbote, sondern durch Ideen, Wettbewerb und kluge Rahmenbedingungen. Eine Ã¼bergriffige Politik, die alles bis ins Detail reguliert, nimmt den Menschen den Gestaltungswillen. Wir brauchen Vertrauen in die Schaffenskraft der klugen KÃ¶pfe in Europa. Dann werden auch die Innovationen entstehen, die uns voranbringen. Dann werden wir ein Wachstum haben, das uns die Mittel erwirtschaftet, um die notwendigen Entwicklungen zu finanzieren und gleichzeitig das Pariser Klimaabkommen einzuhalten.

Wir gewinnen nichts, wenn wir uns ambitionierte Ziele setzen, aber auf dem Weg dahin ArbeitsplÃ¤tze, Industrie und Menschen verlieren, weil unsere Wirtschaft lahmt. Und wir mÃ¼ssen aufpassen, dass Europa nicht isoliert wird. Wir hÃ¤tten uns von China denselben Ehrgeiz gewÃ¼nscht und von den Vereinigten Staaten ein aktives Bekenntnis zum Klimaschutz statt Destruktion. Gerade wir in Ostdeutschland wissen: Nach 1990 war es die Marktwirtschaft, die den Umweltschutz vorangebracht hat. Auch der Klimaschutz wird stark, wenn wir ihn in unseren Markt integrieren â nicht gegen, sondern mit der Wirtschaft. Darum geht es jetzt. Ehrgeiz ja, aber mit AugenmaÃ, Freiheit und Vertrauen in unsere eigene StÃ¤rke.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Aleksandar Nikolic (PfE). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, chers collÃ¨gues, avant que vous partiez Ã  l'autre bout du monde en avion dans vos cocktails mondains Ã  BelÃ©m pour discuter de vos nouvelles taxes carbone et de vos Ã©nergies renouvelables qui feront encore exploser les coÃ»ts de l'Ã©nergie et de vos futures normes dÃ©lirantes, je vais vous ramener Ã  quelque chose de trÃ¨s concret.

On vient d'apprendre que la France a battu cette annÃ©e un triste record. En un an, il y a eu plus de 60Â 000dÃ©faillances d'entreprises, dont 6Â 800 pour le seul mois de septembre. Ce sont nos PME qui sont le plus impactÃ©es, avec une augmentation de plus de 13Â % des procÃ©dures en douze mois. C'est simple, on assiste Ã  la mort lente de nos emplois et de notre appareil productif, bref, de notre avenir.

On nous avait promis une Europe de paix et de prospÃ©ritÃ©. On a finalement une vÃ©ritable tiers-mondisation de la France, comme le disait d'ailleurs notre Ã©phÃ©mÃ¨re premier ministre Bayrou. Macronistes, socialistes et rÃ©publicains, vous avez confiÃ© notre avenir Ã  des technocrates bruxellois hors sol. Ce bilan, c'est le vÃ´tre. Votre COP 30 ne fera qu'aggraver le problÃ¨me. J'espÃ¨re qu'entre deux cocktails au BrÃ©sil, vous penserez aux milliers de chefs d'entreprises et d'employÃ©s que vous sacrifiez. Car eux, ils ne vous oublieront pas dans les urnes.

Â Â Stephen Nikola Bartulica (ECR). â PoÅ¡tovana predsjedavajuÄa, kolegice i kolege, nametanjem tzv. zelene agende Europska unija uniÅ¡tava vlastitu proizvodnju i industriju i pokuÅ¡ava plaÅ¡iti ljude narativom o klimatskoj katastrofi. Klimatski alarmizam uzeo je maha, ali zdrav razum na kraju Äe prevladati. Europska komisija nije u stanju zaÅ¡tititi europske granice niti rijeÅ¡iti, primjerice, migrantsku krizu, a misli da moÅ¾e spasiti planet.

Apsurdno je da se Europa koja proizvodi tek 7Â %Â svjetskih emisija ponaÅ¡a kao da sudbina Zemlje ovisi o njoj. Dok se Europska unija bavi plastiÄnim Äepovima i ograniÄavanjem fosilnih goriva, ostatak svijeta se tome podsmjehuje. Kina, Amerika pod Trumpom i druge zemlje ne mare za zelenu agendu i nastavljaju rasti dok se Europa guÅ¡i u vlastitoj birokraciji. Samo jedan primjer, najsiromaÅ¡nija savezna drÅ¾ava Mississippi u Americi je gotovo prestigla jednu NjemaÄku. Ovo viÅ¡e nije politika, nego nova religija, zelena religija, koja ne spaÅ¡ava planet, nego uniÅ¡tava Europu. Go green, go bankrupt.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â JÃ¼ri Ratas (PPE). â Madam President, dear Commissioner, dear colleagues, in just a few weeks, world leaders will meet in BelÃ©m for COP30. This will be an important moment for global climate action. The European Union has always been a leader in setting strong climate goals, but our credibility depends on what we can truly deliver. We need to make sure that economic growth, innovation and climate responsibility can go hand in hand. Even inside the EU, it is not easy to find a common position. Some countries are moving faster, others are still catching up. We have different levels of innovation and technology.

When we speak about clean industry, it is not only about the environment. It is also about a fair, realistic balance, where moving towards our climate ambitions is driven by a strong economy, innovation and new technologies â not working against them.

How can we make sure that this balance is a real part of European climate strategy, so that the green transition supports competitiveness, create jobs and keeps Europe as a leader in global innovation? One question more: how can we bring other major economies to join us on this and share this responsibility?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â GÃ¼nther Sidl (S&D). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, Herr Kommissar! FÃ¼r spÃ¼rbare Verbesserungen, die auch global glaubhaft sind, mÃ¼ssen wir den Klimaschutz endlich in allen Bereichen mitdenken, auf die die EU Einfluss hatÂ â angefangen bei der nÃ¤chsten Gemeinsamen Agrarpolitik bis hin zur FÃ¶rderung unserer Industrie. Nachhaltige Landwirtschaft heiÃt gleichzeitig mehr Artenvielfalt und bessere Chancen fÃ¼r die kleinbÃ¤uerlichen Strukturen. Mehr saubere Produktion in Europa heiÃt gleichzeitig weniger internationale Transportwege, sichere europÃ¤ische ArbeitsplÃ¤tze und mehr WertschÃ¶pfung und Wohlstand.

Wer glaubt, weniger Klimaschutz sichert unsere Industriebetriebe, irrt gewaltig. Nur wenn wir die TechnologiefÃ¼hrerschaft vor allem bei Umwelttechnologien wieder weltweit Ã¼bernehmen, kÃ¶nnen wir ArbeitsplÃ¤tze und Produktionsstandorte absichern. Klimaschutz kann ein Jobmotor sein. Mit der Masse, schlechten Arbeitsbedingungen und veralteten Technologien werden wir die nÃ¤chsten Herausforderungen nicht meistern kÃ¶nnen. Das wÃ¤re eine starke Botschaft an die Welt, die wir bei der COP 30 in Brasilien platzieren kÃ¶nnten.

Â Â

Â Â Tobiasz BocheÅski (ECR). â Pani PrzewodniczÄca! Szanowni PaÅstwo! To jest po prostu niebywaÅe. Wy dalej o tym samym: zielony Åad, polityka klimatyczna. Ludzie, ktÃ³rzy robiÄ caÅy czas to samo i liczÄ na inny rezultat, objawiajÄ siÄ szaleÅstwem. I trzeba jasno i kategorycznie powiedzieÄ: doÅÄ! Ta polityka, ktÃ³rÄ uprawiacie, to sÄ miliardy euro pompowane w korporacje, pompowane w think tanki, ktÃ³re sÄ z wami powiÄzane. To sÄ miliardy euro, ktÃ³re sÄ marnowane w kaÅ¼dej chwili. ChiÅczycy i Amerykanie rozwijajÄ sztucznÄ inteligencjÄ, rozwijajÄ innowacyjnoÅÄ, rozwijajÄ gospodarkÄ. A wy gdzie nas prowadzicie? Prowadzicie nas do skansenu, ktÃ³ry bÄdzie odwiedzany przez turystÃ³w z Chin i StanÃ³w Zjednoczonych. NadszedÅ najwyÅ¼szy czas, Å¼eby to powiedzieÄ: doÅÄ tej szalonej, obÅÄkaÅczej polityce! Europa siÄ budzi i rozliczy was za to marnotrawstwo.

Â Â

Â Â RaÃºl de la Hoz Quintano (PPE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, una nueva CP y una nueva oportunidad, una nueva carrera para demostrar lo comprometidos que estamos todos en la lucha contra el cambio climÃ¡tico; y una nueva CP que, por supuesto, la UniÃ³n Europea quiere liderar.

A la vista de lo escuchado aquÃ­, compruebo quÃ© fÃ¡cil es hablar de grandes objetivos, de pomposos principios, de retos globales, y quÃ© difÃ­cil es plasmarlos despuÃ©s en compromisos concretos, en polÃ­ticas reales que ayuden realmente a avanzar en el proceso de descarbonizaciÃ³n. Y Â¿por quÃ©? Porque, una vez mÃ¡s, vuelve a pasar lo mismo: no somos realistas.

Fijamos objetivos sin tener en cuenta ni las posibilidades reales de nuestra economÃ­a y nuestras empresas ni las necesidades de avanzar econÃ³micamente en la UniÃ³n Europea; ni siquiera escuchamos a la industria, porque adoptamos medidas y fijamos objetivos a sus espaldas.

Ya hemos visto lo que ha pasado en el sector de la automociÃ³n y la situaciÃ³n en la que se encuentra en este momento: no hemos aprendido nada de los errores del pasado, y ahora nos atrevemos a hablar de nuevos y ambiciosos objetivos, de incremento del ritmo de descarbonizaciÃ³n para 2035 y para 2040, cuando estamos lejos âmuy lejosâ de conseguir los objetivos de 2030. A eso yo lo llamo, sinceramente, hipocresÃ­a: hipocresÃ­a climÃ¡tica.

Sinceramente, no hace falta ir mÃ¡s allÃ¡ y fijar nuevos y mÃ¡s ambiciosos objetivos. CentrÃ©monos en hacer posible que nuestra economÃ­a alcance los objetivos primitivos, los que inicialmente fijamos para 2040; ayudemos a nuestras empresas, ayudemos a nuestra industria y hagÃ¡moslo compatible con el principio de competitividad de nuestra economÃ­a.

No subordinemos la polÃ­tica industrial a la polÃ­tica climÃ¡tica, aprendamos de los errores del pasado.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â NicolÃ¡s GonzÃ¡lez Casares (S&D). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, ayer tenÃ­amos noticias de que, por primera vez, en Islandia se detectaban mosquitos âunos pocos lugares de la tierra que estaban libresâ. Es decir, los efectos del cambio climÃ¡tico van a suponer mÃ¡s enfermedades, mayores riesgos para la salud. Tenemos mÃ¡s tormentas, mÃ¡s inundaciones. Es decir, el cambio climÃ¡tico acelera. Y, ante esa aceleraciÃ³n, lo que no podemos hacer desde Europa es pisar el freno.

Por eso le digo, seÃ±or comisario: frente al empeÃ±o de Trump en dinamitar el multilateralismo, la UniÃ³n Europea, especialmente esta CÃ¡mara, debe ser la vanguardia de la cooperaciÃ³n internacional. No podemos llegar a la CP30 en BelÃ©n con las manos medio vacÃ­as, porque la alternativa al liderazgo europeo, el vacÃ­o que nosotros dejemos, lo van a ocupar otros.

Y, crÃ©anme, tenemos soluciones tecnolÃ³gicas para luchar contra el cambio climÃ¡tico, para reducir emisiones, para llegar al 90Â % de reducciÃ³n en 2040. Las hay. Y, ademÃ¡s, por ellas pasa la competitividad de Europa. EconomÃ­a y reducciÃ³n de emisiones no estÃ¡n reÃ±idas.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

ZgÅoszenia z sali

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Grzegorz Braun (NI). â Madam President, thank you very much for making my work much easier. This spectacle of nonsense, bringing up the global warming dogma again and again, makes it much easier to convince my compatriots â Polish voters â to vote against your stupid and evil organisation.

'Polexit'! Let's regain our independence. This will be much easier since you keep repeating your false doctrines: Green Deal; rainbow deal; migration pact; warmongering. Thank you very much. Life is going to be much better after we leave the European Union. Down with eurokoÅchoz!

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(Koniec zgÅoszeÅ z sali)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Wopke Hoekstra, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, thank you very much, and many thanks to the honourable Members of this esteemed Parliament for the various questions, suggestions, mild criticism, and ideas that we will take forward to BelÃ©m. Let me reiterate a couple of things that I have said before or in response to the various elements that are brought to the debate.

First, let me reiterate our strong support for the incoming COP presidency. The European Union will continue to engage with all it can to achieve success on all the various levers that are important in BelÃ©m. We will be there. We will be there front and centre. We will be building coalitions around a shared commitment towards the Paris Agreement.

Climate change does not discriminate, and that means this truly should be a joint effort. And again, leadership is about articulating what it is that others can expect from us, but it is also articulating what we expect from others. Many mentioned the numbers again. Europe has done quite a lot in the past. It now stands at 6Â % of global emissions. And that is not to say that we do not need to do more to tackle those 6Â %, but we cannot fix this alone. And we do need to make sure that those responsible for the larger parts of the 94Â % simply do more.

Secondly, and Madam President, I would hope you would allow me to respond to a couple of things that came up frequently in the debate. And first, I'm going to say something that many brought up â MsÂ Pereira, MsÂ Schilling, MrÂ Liese, MrÂ LÃ³pez and others â and that was about, you know, the process, but also the content of the ambition as the European Union is putting it on the table.

Indeed, as quite often is the case in politics, procedures and trajectories and processes are never completely straightforward. And that is what we are also currently experiencing in the debate about 2035 and 2040. And as much as I would like to see a straight line between A and B, that is just simply very often the case. Does it matter? That is also a question that others asked: does it matter internationally? My read is it doesn't, as long as we come up with ambition towards a 2035 target.

That is also my experience from a couple of weeks ago in New York. Processes are not only complicated here in Europe. What people judge is the number we bring to the table. And to all those in the international arena that have asked questions, I am saying: please do your utmost to basically match our ambition, match our range, match our targets, join us in basically tackling this.

Specifically, and we had that conversation already â that will be the second question or set of questions I wanted to respond to â Madam President, is the one Emma Wiesner also asked in the more confined session we had earlier today. And that is something like the global stocktake that has a rhythm of every five years, would it not be good to take stock more frequently? And I think there is merit in that logic, because once in every five years with a problem that is getting so out of hand, it actually does make sense. So I am going to look into it. But I'm also going to say that I first want to see the exact ramifications, because this is, again, something you cannot do alone. You would need to do it with the global community. So maybe there are shortcuts, maybe there are ways to have that debate without all the procedures that are necessary, but I'm at least intellectually open to it.

Third, as Catarina Martins and some others have alluded to, are we speaking to all stakeholders? Absolutely. And we are doing that before the COP, at the COP and again after the COP, with NGOs, with a range of organisations, with businesses who are also on the same page as we are, with politicians domestically and of course internationallyÂ â basically with everyone, from all walks of life, those we very much agree with and those we might not yet agree with. And that is what diplomacy is all about, right? Talking to those you agree with and those with whom you have things to discuss.

Finally, and there were more and more who moved a bit into things that are only loosely related to this debate, but I do want to set the record straight on one thing. Alexandr Vondra and others have asked again about nuclear, and I just want to be very straightforward here. The European Union â and it is up to Member States â but the European Union has embraced and continues to embrace nuclear as part of the solution space. So you can continue to ask for it, but you already have it.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â Madam President, honourable Members, Commissioner, thank you for a good debate. I took note of all of you who call for higher climate ambitions. It's a moment where humanity looks itself in the mirror, as Ms Pereira from the EPP said, but I also took note of all of you who've raised concerns about the consequences of high climate ambitions. Concerns about too many burdens, too high consumer prices. And this illustrates why climate ambitions and competitiveness have to go hand in hand. High climate ambitions should, in fact, work to improve competitiveness and give the basis for investment in new green technologies that will bring us wealth in the future. In the future, we will not be using fossil fuel and that is why it will also be a good business case that Europe developed those new technologies.

I also understand those of you who regret that we have not yet reached an agreement on the 2040 target, but I assure you that that is still the aim of the Danish Presidency. The Presidency has called a possible additional Council meeting on the 4 November. The aim of this meeting will be to reach an agreement on the 2040 target and, subsequently, the NDC, in order to be ready for COP30 â of course, without prejudging the European Council outcome.

We are, as a Danish Presidency, very well aware that COP30 takes place in the context of deepening climate crisis. We see the devastating consequences of climate change across the globe. Drought, floods, rising sea levels and extreme weather events are already disrupting livelihoods for millions of people. Decisive climate action is more urgent than ever. It must be coupled with societal, industrial and technological transition. The EU is well on track to achieve its 2030 climate target and is leading the way in international climate negotiations. But our efforts can only be effective on a global scale if we are joined by others. We will continue our international engagement towards a meaningful outcome of COP30.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

IN THE CHAIR: JAVI LÃPEZ
Vice-President

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â To wind up the debate, I have received one motion for a resolution tabled in accordance with Rule 142(5).

The debate is closed.

The vote will take place tomorrow, 23Â October 2025.

Written Statements (Rule 178)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 		Â 

Â Â Sirpa PietikÃ¤inen (PPE), in writing. â President, dear colleagues,

The EU has failed to show leadership ahead of the upcoming UN Climate Change Conference due to political resistance that denies the urgent need and the economic rationality behind climate action. The European Parliament and the Council have been unable to agree on the positions for the European Climate Law targets for 2040, and the EU has failed to deliver its NDCs for 2035 on time. If the EU does not lead in climate action, who will?

The climate change is not a political decision but a scientific fact. Whether we choose to solve the crisis or not, however, is a political decision. Closing our eyes to the symptoms of accelerating climate change does not make the reality disappear. The climate is changing, and our ecosystems are degrading due to human caused activities.

COP30 will be another chance for the international community to agree on the necessary actions to stop climate change. Nevertheless, this will not be possible if the EU maintains a weak and unambitious position. We need to step up - not only for the EU and its citizens, but also for the international community that relies on the EU.

Â 

20. Key objectives for the CITES COP20 meeting in Uzbekistan (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â President. â The next item is the debate on:

â the oral question to the Council and the Commission on key objectives for the CITES CoP20 meeting in Uzbekistan, tabled by Antonio Decaro, Manuela Ripa, CÃ©sar Luena, Pietro Fiocchi, Michal Wiezik and Maria Ohisalo on behalf of the Committee on the Environment, Climate and Food Safety (O-000035/2025 - B10-0013/25) (2025/2618(RSP));

â the oral question to the Council and the Commission on key objectives for the CITES CoP20 meeting in Uzbekistan, tabled by Antonio Decaro, Manuela Ripa, CÃ©sar Luena, Pietro Fiocchi, Michal Wiezik and Maria Ohisalo on behalf of the Committee on the Environment, Climate and Food Safety (O-000036/2025 - B10-0014/25) (2025/2618(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Manuela Ripa, Verfasserin. â Herr PrÃ¤sident, sehr geehrte Frau Kommissarin, sehr geehrte Frau Ministerin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Die CITES-COP-20âKonferenz zum internationalen Artenschutz ist ebenso wichtig wie die COPÂ 30 zum Klimaschutz. Denn Klimaschutz und Artenschutz sind untrennbar miteinander verbunden. Ohne gesunde Ãkosysteme gibt es kein stabiles Klima und ohne Artenvielfalt keine Zukunft. Die CITES-Konvention, die in diesem Jahr 50Â Jahre alt wird, ist unser wichtigstes Instrument im Kampf gegen den weltweiten Artenverlust. Ohne dieses Abkommen wÃ¤ren viele Arten â wie Tiger, NashÃ¶rner, MeeresschildkrÃ¶ten â schon ausgestorben, denn CITES reguliert den Handel mit mehr als 40Â 000Â gefÃ¤hrdeten Tier- und Pflanzenarten. Dieser Handel umfasst jÃ¤hrlich Milliarden von Dollar und hunderte Millionen Exemplare. Es geht um lebende Tiere und Pflanzen, aber auch eine groÃe Auswahl von Produkten, etwa Holz, Medikamente oder Lederwaren.

Doch der illegale Artenhandel nimmt immer weiter zu. Er ist heute die viertgrÃ¶Ãte Form organisierter KriminalitÃ¤t weltweit. Er zerstÃ¶rt Ãkosysteme, ruiniert LebensrÃ¤ume und beschleunigt das Artensterben. Durch den Onlinehandel ergeben sich leider auch in diesem Bereich viele neue Risiken. Wir mÃ¼ssen CITES daher modernisieren und an die neuen Herausforderungen anpassen. Die EU trÃ¤gt eine besondere Verantwortung: Als einer der grÃ¶Ãten MÃ¤rkte fÃ¼r legale, aber auch illegale Naturprodukte brauchen wir klare Regeln und eine starke Durchsetzung. Der internationale Handel mit wildlebenden Tier- und Pflanzenarten muss legal, nachhaltig und rÃ¼ckverfolgbar sein. Somit ist CITES mehr als nur ein Schutzabkommen. Es ist auch ein Instrument fÃ¼r gerechten, transparenten und nachhaltigen Handel, der lokale Gemeinschaften stÃ¤rkt, ohne die Natur zu zerstÃ¶ren. Doch damit das gelingt, braucht es politischen Willen und eine klare Zielsetzung. Darum fragen wir die Kommission konkret: Welche zentralen strategischen Ziele verfolgt die EU auf der bevorstehenden CITESâKonferenz in Usbekistan? Und wie weit ist die EU mit der Umsetzung des Aktionsplans zur BekÃ¤mpfung des illegalen Artenhandels von 2022? Welche konkreten Fortschritte wurden erzielt, um bestehende MÃ¤ngel zu beheben und die Wirksamkeit zu erhÃ¶hen? Gemeinsam mit meinem sozialdemokratischen Kollegen CÃ©sar Luena werde ich die Delegation des EuropÃ¤ischen Parlaments zur CITES COPÂ 20 in Samarkand leiten.

In der EntschlieÃung, die mit groÃer Mehrheit im Ausschuss angenommen wurde und Ã¼ber die wir morgen abstimmen werden, haben wir klare Forderungen: Bedrohte Tier- und Pflanzenarten mÃ¼ssen besser geschÃ¼tzt werden, wir brauchen rechtsverbindliche Instrumente zur BekÃ¤mpfung des Elfenbeinhandels in der EU. Bisher gibt es noch zu viele freiwillige Regeln. Wir mÃ¼ssen die Digitalisierung und den Datenaustausch fÃ¶rdern, zum Beispiel bei der Ausstellung und ÃberprÃ¼fung von ImportantrÃ¤gen. Im Moment gibt es viel zu viel Doppelarbeit. DafÃ¼r brauchen wir auch eine verbesserte RÃ¼ckverfolgbarkeit von CITES-gelisteten Arten, etwa durch TRACES. Ein solches EUâInformationssystem zum Wildtierhandel wÃ¤re sehr wirkungsvoll bei gleichzeitig geringem Verwaltungsaufwand. Wir mÃ¼ssen die Zusammenarbeit mit anderen internationalen Ãbereinkommen, etwa dem Abkommen von Kunming-Montreal, vertiefen. Wir erneuern unsere Forderung, dass nur solche Wildtiere als Haustiere gehalten werden dÃ¼rfen, die auf einer Liste stehen. Viele BÃ¼rger, die ein exotisches Tier erwerben, sind sich gar nicht darÃ¼ber bewusst, ob es sich um eine geschÃ¼tzte Art handelt. Sie hÃ¤tten damit mehr Klarheit und Sicherheit. Wir mÃ¼ssen die BekÃ¤mpfung des illegalen Artenhandels als Teil unserer Umwelt- und Sicherheitspolitik begreifen. Wir fordern deshalb, grenzÃ¼berschreitende WildtierkriminalitÃ¤t endlich als organisierte KriminalitÃ¤t anzuerkennen. Was mir ganz besonders am Herzen liegt: Arten, die illegal beschafft und nach Europa gefÃ¼hrt werden, dÃ¼rfen nicht legal weiterverkauft werden. Es darf keine SchlupflÃ¶cher geben. In den USA gibt es dieses starke Gesetz Ã¼brigens schon. Es wurde erstmals im Jahre 1900 eingefÃ¼hrt.

Kolleginnen und Kollegen, die wilde Fauna und Flora zu schÃ¼tzen, ist eine WinâWinâSituation fÃ¼r die ganze Welt â nicht nur Ã¶kologisch, sondern auch Ã¶konomisch. Wir mÃ¼ssen wilde Tiere und Pflanzen mehr wertschÃ¤tzen, denn sie leisten einen immensen Beitrag zur BiodiversitÃ¤t, die fÃ¼r unser Ãberleben so entscheidend ist. Die COPÂ 20 bietet uns die Chance, Weichen zu stellenÂ â fÃ¼r einen besseren Schutz bedrohter Arten wie Elefanten, GroÃkatzen, NashÃ¶rner, Korallen, Haie oder Aale, fÃ¼r eine konsequente BekÃ¤mpfung von WildtierkriminalitÃ¤t â online wie offline â und fÃ¼r ein CITES, das fit ist fÃ¼r die Herausforderungen unserer Zeit â Klimawandel, BiodiversitÃ¤tsverlust, Zoonoserisiken. Deswegen bitte ich um Ihre UnterstÃ¼tzung fÃ¼r die EntschlieÃung, damit wir mit starken Forderungen nach Usbekistan zur COP 20 fahren kÃ¶nnen.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, Commissioner, thank you for your question on the key objectives for the 20th Conference of the Parties to CITES.

Our common objectives at the conference follow our broader conservation goals, especially those in the EU biodiversity strategy for 2030, the revised EU action plan against wildlife trafficking and the European Green Deal. The EU's key objectives at CoP20 concern two main areas: to regulate international trade in endangered animal and plant species, pursuing a science-based approach, and to strengthen the response by the international community to wildlife trafficking. When it comes to regulating international trade in endangered animal and plant species, the EU is committed to stronger measures to combat illegal wildlife trade, especially for species at high risk. This includes aquatic species like eels and marine sharks.

The EU position is in line with the principles in the Parliament's draft resolution, notably the focus on stronger law enforcement, greater international cooperation and improved abilities to trace wildlife products. In addition, we will urge all CITES parties to adopt the strongest possible measures to protect species such as the European eel. The Council and the European Parliament agree that the protection of endangered species must be based on science. At CoP20, the EU will prioritise the adoption of proposals based on robust scientific evidence, especially those about species that are facing unsustainable trade levels. We will also consider listing proposals concerning endemic species, which are particularly vulnerable due to their limited geographical distribution and often small population sizes.

When it comes to strengthening the response by the international community to wildlife trafficking, the EU recognises the importance of aligning the CITES objectives with the global biodiversity agenda, particularly the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework. This includes aligning with its targets, stopping human-caused extinction and promoting sustainable wildlife management. We must also increase synergies between CITES and the Convention on Biological Diversity and the UN General Assembly resolutions on illegal trafficking. Last but not least, we recognise the critical role of enforcement authorities and we support capacity-building. We must prosecute wildlife trafficking and improve compliance.

Let me now address the second part of the question on the implementation of the revised EU action plan against wildlife trafficking. Since its adoption in 2022, it has been the cornerstone of our strategy to fight the global crisis of wildlife trafficking. The action plan focuses on four main priorities: preventing wildlife trafficking and addressing its root causes; strengthening the legal and policy framework; enforcing regulations and policies, and strengthening global partnerships. Progress has been made in several areas. Multi-agency cooperation is stronger. As a result, we have more prevention, more detection, more prosecution and more sanction of wildlife crimes. The EU has also taken steps to treat wildlife trafficking as a serious crime. This is complemented by capacity-building and specialisation across the enforcement chain.

Challenges remain, however. Despite increased enforcement, wildlife trafficking is still a serious threat to biodiversity and security. Changes in trading routes and the increased use of online platforms for illegal wildlife trade are new challenges that require new solutions. The EU is committed to addressing these challenges by stronger legal frameworks, enhanced enforcement and global partnerships.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jessika Roswall, Member of the Commission. â Mr President, honourable Members, Minister, in just a few weeks, the international community will gather in Samarkand for 20th meeting of the Conference of the Parties to the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora, CITES. This will be a crucial moment for global efforts to ensure that some trade flows do not threaten the survival of endangered wild species. The European Union will go to Uzbekistan with a clear vision: to keep CITES focused on its core mandate, strengthen the fight against wildlife trafficking and reinforce multilateral cooperation.

Just last week, we secured the G20 ministers' declaration on crimes affecting the environment. AT COPÂ 20, the EU will present proposals to include additional species to better regulate their trade and to protect them from overexploitation. These proposals are grounded in science, backed by data and supported by our shared duty to act before it's too late. But each new listing increases the complexity of enforcement and the burden of authorities. We will therefore call for practical solutions to help parties strengthen compliance, build capacity and ensure that the CITES secretariat and committees can fulfil their mandates effectively.

Honourable Members, the EU's leadership in CITES is not limited to these negotiations. It extends to what we do every day across our Union and with partners worldwide to combat illegal wildlife trade. The first progress report on the implementation of the revised EU actionÂ plan against wildlife trafficking in 2023 shows that the EU and its Member States have made steady progress in translating commitment into actions. Member States have strengthened their legal frameworks, introduced stricter penalties and integrated wildlife crime into judicial training.

Awareness campaigns have reached airports, schools and local communities, reminding citizens that buying illegal wildlife products fuels global crime and harms biodiversity. Let me also thank the Members that have supported this awareness-raising across Europe. The report also highlights major enforcement successes. For example, in 2023, Operation Lake, coordinated by Europol, led to the arrest of 256Â people for trafficking in glass eels, and Operation Thunder mobilised authorities from 133Â countries to tackle forestry and wildlife crimes. These actions show that international cooperation works and must continue.

However, as Ms Ripa said, challenges remain: convictions for financial crimes linked to wildlife trafficking are rare. Cross-border investigations â particularly those targeting online â are too few, and data on prosecutions and penalties remain limited. To address these gaps, the Commission continues to implement the action plan, promote cross-border collaboration and test innovative solutions. For example, a pilot project entrusted by the European Parliament is exploring market-based interventions to reduce demand for illegal sourced exotic pets. Results are expected next year. In 2026, the Commission will also follow up on studies on the criminalisation of illegally sourced wildlife, and an EU-wide positive list for animals allowed to be kept as pets.

President, honourable Members, Minister, CITESÂ COPÂ 20 comes at a pivotal moment and multilateralism must deliver. The EU will go to Samarkand ready to work constructively with all parties to uphold science, enhance enforcement and support countries that need capacity to act. Our message is simple: protecting wildlife is protecting our societies and our future. It safeguards ecosystems that sustain our food, water and climate. It preserves a living legacy for generations to come. So let us continue to stand together â European Parliament, Member States and partners â to ensure that the EU remains a global force for nature and wildlife.

I am confident that with your continued support, the EU will lead by example â standing firm for species protection, sustainable trade and effective multilateralism.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Esther Herranz GarcÃ­a, en nombre del Grupo PPE. â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±ora ministra, seÃ±ora comisaria Roswall, pese a los esfuerzos realizados desde las instituciones y al notable aumento de la concienciaciÃ³n social con respecto a la biodiversidad, con las posibilidades que dan Internet y las redes sociales se ha favorecido clarÃ­simamente un incremento de la demanda de especies silvestres protegidas, prohibidas. En particular a travÃ©s del comercio en lÃ­nea y, lo que es peor, de la caza furtiva de especies amenazadas. Surge la necesidad de actuar en CITES para que el comercio legal sea sostenible, y a largo plazo. Para conseguirlo necesitamos una mayor digitalizaciÃ³n y una mejor trazabilidad que garantice el control del comercio de estas especies silvestres, junto a un mejor control en fronteras y una implementaciÃ³n de medidas coercitivas en caso de incumplimiento, algo que en la actualidad apenas estÃ¡ sucediendo.

Asimismo, no podemos olvidar que un comercio no regulado supone riesgos tanto para la salud humana como para la animal, y tambiÃ©n para la biodiversidad local y global. Por un lado, aumentan los riesgos de propagaciÃ³n de patÃ³genos y la apariciÃ³n de enfermedades zoonÃ³ticas, principalmente por la vÃ­a de la importaciÃ³n de carne de contrabando. Por otro, la importaciÃ³n de especies silvestres sin control puede provocar la apariciÃ³n, directa o indirectamente, de especies invasoras que arrasen la biodiversidad local, como ocurre, por ejemplo, con la avispa velutina, que no solo estÃ¡ reduciendo significativamente la poblaciÃ³n de abejas de la UniÃ³n Europea, seÃ±or presidente, sino que es la responsable de varias muertes humanas. La Ãºltima, la semana pasada en mi paÃ­s.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â CÃ©sar Luena, en nombre del Grupo S&D. â SeÃ±or presidente, a finales del aÃ±o pasado, una operaciÃ³n mundial âcoordinada por Interpol y la OrganizaciÃ³n Mundial de Aduanasâ permitiÃ³ incautar casi 20Â 000 animales vivos protegidos y detener a 365 personas en 138 paÃ­ses.

A la vez, Traffic, la organizaciÃ³n internacional de referencia que combate los delitos contra la naturaleza, alertÃ³ de que la UniÃ³n sigue siendo uno de los principales centros mundiales del trÃ¡fico ilegal de especies silvestres âseÃ±ora comisaria, atenciÃ³n a estoâ con mÃ¡s de 5Â 000 decomisos en 2023 y mÃ¡s de un millÃ³n de especÃ­menes incautados, muchos de ellos vivos. Este comercio ilÃ­cito es la cuarta industria criminal mÃ¡s grande del mundo, solo por detrÃ¡s del trÃ¡fico de armas, drogas y personas.

Por eso, la posiciÃ³n del Parlamento para la CP20 de CITES, que votaremos maÃ±ana, es clara y firme âgracias, Manuela Ripa, por tu trabajo en esta ResoluciÃ³nâ.

Primero, el trÃ¡fico de marfil: en las plataformas digitales de la UniÃ³n, solo un 10Â % de los anuncios de objetos de marfil aportan pruebas de legalidad. Por ello pedimos, seÃ±ora comisaria, que transforme las actuales guÃ­as en legislaciÃ³n vinculante; estÃ¡n bien las guÃ­as, pero legislaciÃ³n vinculante.

Segundo, la UniÃ³n Europea no solo es uno de los principales consumidores de fauna silvestre obtenida ilegalmente, sino que tambiÃ©n se encuentra entre los principales reexportadores de especies de reptiles y anfibios capturados en estado silvestre que no estÃ¡n incluidos en CITES hacia los Estados Unidos. El estudio de viabilidad reciente de la ComisiÃ³n establece que existen soluciones legislativas posibles.

Y tercero, pedimos âde nuevoâ a la ComisiÃ³n una lista positiva de especies permitidas como animales de compaÃ±Ã­a en la UniÃ³n Europea.

Recordemos, seÃ±orÃ­as, que hemos sufrido una pandemia originada por zoonosis y que CITES tambiÃ©n protege la salud global. Esto es muy importante: la UniÃ³n debe liderar esta integraciÃ³n del enfoque de Â«Una sola saludÂ», que une la salud humana, animal y ambiental en todas sus polÃ­ticas.

Me quedan algunos segundos, asÃ­ que moltes grÃ cies, seÃ±or president. SÃ© que alguna vez le preguntan por quÃ© le hablo en catalÃ¡n: es porque usted es catalÃ¡n y, en esta CÃ¡mara, deberÃ­a poder hablarse en algÃºn momento en catalÃ¡n, pero tambiÃ©n en gallego y euskera. Esa es la razÃ³n, para que lo sepan.

Â Â

Â Â Anne-Sophie Frigout, au nom du groupe PfE. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, chers collÃ¨gues, comme le rappelle l'Organisation pour l'alimentation et l'agriculture, l'Union europÃ©enne reste l'un des principaux marchÃ©s et points de transit du trafic mondial d'espÃ¨ces sauvages. Ce commerce illÃ©gal dÃ©truit des Ã©cosystÃ¨mes entiers tout en alimentant la criminalitÃ© internationale.

SignÃ©e en 1973, la CITES a permis de rendre illicites des commerces autrefois florissants. Des milliers d'espÃ¨ces ont ainsi pu Ãªtre sauvÃ©es, mais les failles juridiques europÃ©ennes permettent encore la vente lÃ©gale d'animaux capturÃ©s illÃ©galement, surtout lorsqu'ils ne sont pas inscrits Ã  la CITES, et les plateformes en ligne sont devenues des vecteurs privilÃ©giÃ©s de ce commerce. Tant que ces failles ne seront pas comblÃ©es, le trafic restera une activitÃ© Ã  faible risque, mais surtout Ã  forte rentabilitÃ©.

Alors oui, il faut renforcer les contrÃ´les, les sanctions et la coopÃ©ration entre Ãtats. L'humanitÃ© n'est pas l'ennemie du vivant, elle en est le gardien.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Michal Wiezik, on behalf of the Renew Group. â Mr President, wildlife crime is multifaceted: it involves wildlife trafficking, document fraud, tax evasion and money laundering, and one can say that it is huge and everywhere. For instance, in 2023, over 1Â million live eels were seized in the European Union: 1Â million live eels that didn't reach European rivers, eels that never reproduced, eels missing in the ocean ecosystem, eels out of the legal trade, which normally would enrich European communities. That needs to stop.

CITES is an important treaty. It covers roughly 40Â 000 species worldwide, yet countless others don't fall under this regulation. Therefore, we need a strong CITES and we need a strong enforcement chain to tackle illegal trade in wildlife.

We need the European Union to be strong and successful at CoP20. Therefore, Renew Europe supports the final position text. We call for the review of CITES strategic vision to align with Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework and ask to strengthen legal frameworks against wildlife trafficking. If we need to stop wildlife crime, we simply need to act. The time is now!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Tilly Metz, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. â MrÂ President, dear colleagues, dear Commissioner, almost 300Â 000 hunting trophies of wild animals listed under the Convention of International Trade in Endangered Species of wild fauna and flora (CITES) were imported globally between 2013 and 2023, with hunters killing animals simply for personal glory and the sale of their remains. Let me remind you that in 2022, this Parliament clearly demanded a ban on the import of hunting trophies. I therefore urge all my colleagues to support tomorrow strong wording against this practice.

The use and trade of wildlife is not only a matter of animal welfare and biodiversity preservation, but also a major threat to public health, due to the risk of future zoonotic diseases. So a One Health approach must be a priority at the upcoming CoP20 meeting in Uzbekistan. This is closely linked to the urgent need for the EU-wide positive list.

Last but not least, let me say that I am deeply concerned about EU exports of wild animals, including certain species, to the Vantara zoological complex in India. And yet, I call on the Commission to suspend exports to VantaraÂ and push for stricter CITES enforcement at the CoP20.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Martin GÃ¼nther, im Namen der Fraktion The Left. â Herr PrÃ¤sident, sehr geehrte Damen und Herren! Der Schutz bedrohter Arten ist unerlÃ¤sslich. Die Ursachen des Wildtierhandels â Armut, Ungleichheit und Ausbeutung â mÃ¼ssen klar benannt und bekÃ¤mpft werden. Die EU muss soziale Gerechtigkeit und gemeindebasierten Naturschutz ins Zentrum ihrer Strategie stellen. Die EU muss sich bei der Artenschutzkonferenz CITES auf Artenschutz durch AufklÃ¤rung und vor allem die Reduzierung der Nachfrage durch klare Vorschriften einsetzen.

Denn eines ist klar: Die Produktionsweise und der Konsum â vor allem des Globalen Nordens â treiben das Artensterben. Die pure Anerkennung der Rolle indigener VÃ¶lker und lokaler Gemeinschaften reicht nicht. Sie mÃ¼ssen aktiv an Entscheidungen beteiligt werden. Sie verteidigen Artenvielfalt an vorderster Front. Koloniale Naturschutzmodelle, die Natur und Mensch trennen, mÃ¼ssen der Vergangenheit angehÃ¶ren. Es ist inakzeptabel, dass in Tansania Ã¼ber 100Â 000 Massai zwangsweise aus ihrem Land vertrieben werden, um Platz fÃ¼r Natur und Jagdreservate zu schaffen.

Â

Â Â Katri Kulmuni (Renew). â Mr President, a century ago, even Finnish newspapers advertised ivory luxury items. The world has changed indeed, and a more effective ban on ivory trade and all other measures to prevent illegal wildlife trade deserve our support. The importance of the issue is emphasised by the fact that this Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora, is indeed the largest global wildlife conservation agreement between 185Â countries.

It is a completely different matter when it comes to legally hunted animals and their commercial exploitation. Decision-making concerning them must be as local as possible, but this proposal does not apply to them. There is still a lot of work to be done against the illegal wildlife trade. And regarding this, the European Union has a significant role to play globally.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sebastian Everding (The Left). â Herr PrÃ¤sident! Wie kann es sein, dass ein Schmetterling, der nur an einem einzigen Ort auf der Welt vorkommt, nÃ¤mlich in den HÃ¼geln rund um das nordmazedonische Dorf Pletvar, online legal gehandelt werden darf? Der Lebensraum des mazedonischen BlÃ¤ulings umfasst gerade einmal 1,5Â Quadratkilometer. Dieser Schmetterling ist fÃ¼r die lokale BiodiversitÃ¤t von entscheidender Bedeutung, und es existieren gerade einmal noch rund 1Â 000 Exemplare. WÃ¤hrend Nordmazedonien das Fangen und den Export radikal verbietet, ist der Handel innerhalb der EU aufgrund von LÃ¼cken im CITESâSystem legal.

Im Vorfeld der COPÂ 20 haben viele Staaten VorschlÃ¤ge gemacht, um ihre einheimischen, oft endemischen Arten zu schÃ¼tzen. Deswegen meine Frage: Wird die Kommission diese BemÃ¼hungen unterstÃ¼tzen und anerkennen, dass internationale Zusammenarbeit auÃerhalb der EU unerlÃ¤sslich ist, um den Handel effektiv zu kontrollieren und die Artenvielfalt zu schÃ¼tzen? Wir dÃ¼rfen nicht zulassen, dass rechtliche SchlupflÃ¶cher Arten gefÃ¤hrden, die fÃ¼r ganze Ãkosysteme relevant sind. CITES muss weiterentwickelt werden, um so noch mehr Arten weltweit zu schÃ¼tzen.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sirpa PietikÃ¤inen (PPE). â MrÂ President, we know that climate change worsens the biodiversity threat, and we have the Kunming Biodiversity Framework to actually prevent the loss of biodiversity. So these two factors need to be fed into CITES in the future, in a science-based approach when judging the species. And I hope you could go in front of this.

My second point is the better enforcement that the Council and the Commission already highlighted. This needs more resources. This really needs resources also under the upcoming MFF, and we need special targeted resources for online crime, better engagement of OLAF on an EU base to search the cases, also when it comes to transit.

Last but not least, on traceability: trophy hunting is one dangerous factor in wildlife crime. It catches in the wild the breed.Â So I just appeal to the Commission to put in force the ban on trophy hunting and any trophies to be transited through the EU or to be taken to the EU. The EU needs to lead in ambition.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

Catch-the-eye procedure

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI). â Herr PrÃ¤sident! Eines Tages, wenn jeder LÃ¶wenkopf, der an irgendeiner Wand hÃ¤ngt, oder jedes Elfenbeinhorn, das in einer TrophÃ¤enkammer liegt, zu Staub zerfallen wird, wenn kein Schmetterling mehr Ã¼ber Blumen fliegt und keine Echse mehr durch den Urwald kriecht, wenn wir Menschen unter Glaskuppeln leben, weil wir unseren Planeten, unsere Natur und unsere Lebensgrundlage zerstÃ¶rt haben, werden wir uns fragen, was wir hÃ¤tten anders tun kÃ¶nnen.

Oder eines Tages, wenn wir unseren Planeten gerettet haben, wenn wir den Respekt vor der Natur und ihrer SchÃ¶nheit, die uns heutzutage nur noch indigene VÃ¶lker zeigen, verstanden haben, werden wir zurÃ¼ckschauen und uns freuen, dass wir zusammengefunden haben in dieser Stunde der Not unseres Planeten und das Ruder herumgerissen haben â fÃ¼r uns, fÃ¼r die Tiere und fÃ¼r kommende Generationen.

Ich wÃ¼nsche Ihnen allen, werte Kolleginnen und Kollegen, den Vertretern von Kommission und Rat, unseren drei GÃ¤sten und auch den Leuten in den Ãbersetzerkabinen eine gute Nacht.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jessika Roswall, Member of the Commission. â Mr President, honourable Members, Minister, today's debate that all of you have shared underlines how essential it is to keep global attention on the protection of wildlife and the integrity of ecosystems that sustain us all. As the world prepares for CITES CoP20, our task is to ensure that commitments translate into real impact, enforcement in partnership and in the daily work of those protecting species on the ground.

The EU's credibility rests not only on the positions we bring to Samarkand, but also on the consistency of our actions at home: strengthening governance, reducing demand and aligning trade with sustainability. This is how we demonstrate that biodiversity policy is not an isolated agenda, but also a cornerstone of our environmental, economic and security priorities.

In the weeks ahead, we will require unity, pragmatism and resolve. Europe's voice will be strongest when it speaks clearly, united â like today â and with the high ambition that the scale of the crisis demands. So let us carry that spirit to Samarkand, confident in the thought that through science, partnership and determination, we can help ensure international trade that supports rather than undermines the survival of wild species.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â Mr President, honourable Members, Commissioner, I heard a lot of agreement during this debate, and I can assure you that the Council is working on the EU position for the CoP20 and will adopt the decision next month. The Parliament will be informed about this decision. I am pleased to see that our key objectives for CITES CoP20 are aligned with those expressed in the European Parliament's draft resolution.

At the conference in Samarkand, the Danish Presidency will do our best to have the EU proposals adopted. We are also ready to give briefings on the spot, as far as possible, to the Members of this House present at the conference.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â To wind up the debate, I have received one motion for a resolution tabled in accordance with Rule 142(5).

The debate is closed.

The vote will take place tomorrow, 23 October 2025.

Â 

21. Ending all energy imports from Russia to the EU and closing loopholes through third countries (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â President. â The next item is the debate on the Council and Commission statements on ending all energy imports from Russia to the EU and closing loopholes through third countries (2025/2918(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Dan JÃ¸rgensen, Member of the Commission. â Mr President, honourable Members, on 6Â May we sent a very clear message to Russia: no more. No more will Europe be exposed to the weaponisation of energy, no more will we allow our economies to be blackmailed, no more will we indirectly help finance Putin's war.

Today our message is the same. Our resolution holds firm. Delivering on this commitment is not only vital for Europe's energy security, it is vital for a cleaner energy system and a more competitive economy. It accompanies and accelerates our efforts to roll out more clean energy, to improve energy efficiency and to build a truly integrated and interconnected energy union.

It is not simply about closing the tab on Russia. It is about opening the flow of home-grown, affordable, clean energy across our continent. Our roadmap outlines the steps that must be taken to achieve these goals, and it is essential that we take these steps together.

Therefore, I want to sincerely thank you, the honourable Members of this House, for your commitment and your efforts on this crucial file. In particular, I want to thank the ITRE and the INTA teams for their good cooperation, and I congratulate you on your swift adoptionÂ of the Parliament's position.

As you know, the Council reached a general approach two days ago: both co-legislators deserve huge credit for advancing these files so quickly. I fully share the view that we need robust monitoring and enforcement to avoid any attempts at circumvention, and I am fully aware that this can be done without creating unnecessary administrative burdens or barriers to gas imports from non-Russian suppliers.

Building on another point raised, it is also important to ensure a gradual and coordinated phase-out of remaining fossil fuel imports from Russia. Even if Russian oil only accounts for 3Â % of our imports in Europe, that is still 3Â % too much. It is important to note that each of our Member States who continue to import Russian oil have viable alternatives. The Commission will support those Member States to embrace these alternatives and to fully phase out the reliance on Russian oil by working through their mandatory diversification plans. Additionally, work on our proposal for nuclear is ongoing, and we are engaged in close dialogue with Member States and key stakeholders.

Honourable Members, let me thank you once again for your commitment on this file. We now have strong political momentum, it is essential that we maintain this momentum so that we can deliver on our shared ambition to adopt this proposal by the end of the year. My team and I are ready to fully commit to work with you on achieving this ambition. I am confident that we will reach fair compromises, find good solutions and fulfil our shared goal for a more secure, sustainable and competitive Europe.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Przyjmujemy sankcje za rok, za dwa. W miÄdzyczasie kupujemy poprzez inne firmy, inne kierunki. DziÄkujÄ bardzo, ale to tak naprawdÄ jest skandal.

Preto je veÄ¾mi dÃ´leÅ¾itÃ© maÅ¥ odvahu prijÃ­maÅ¥ zodpovednÃ© rozhodnutia. Som veÄ¾mi rada, Å¾e vÃ¤ÄÅ¡ina EurÃ³pskeho parlamentu si to uvedomuje. Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ville NiinistÃ¶, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. â MrÂ President, for Russia and for Putin, export of fossil fuels has never been about trade. For Russia, it is about power, control, extortion and coercion. In short, about geopolitics.

We are now finally ready in Europe to forever untie ourselves from this dependency. That is why this legislation to ban Russian gas imports and also the Parliament's inclusion to ban also oil imports is historic. In 2024, we were still importing fossil fuels from Russia to the value of EURÂ 22Â billion â income that Russia uses to feed its war economy and its atrocious war on Ukraine. This is unacceptable. We must use sanctions to stop Russia's war, but we also must go beyond sanctions.

The European Parliament has adopted a position that not only bans forever Russian gas and oil imports, but it also does it with strict timetables, strict enforcement and closing of all loopholes and without exemptions, targeting also the shadow fleet and other efforts of circumvention by Russia. This is timely and this is super important. At the same time, we need to build the future as we quit the past. We need to build a sustainable European energy system based on renewables, energy efficiency, electrification and other clean European solutions. This is also about competitiveness, it is about climate action and it is about protecting democracy against the fossil dictators.

We need to convince the Council in the trilogues to have the same ambition to quit Russian fossil fuels once and for all.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hanna Gedin, fÃ¶r The Left gruppen. â Herr talman! FÃ¶r drygt tvÃ¥ veckor sedan satt jag i ett skyddsrum i den ukrainska staden Lviv. Att kÃ¶pa rysk energi Ã¤r att bekosta krigsbrott mot Ukraina, skrev den ukrainska utrikesministern dagen efter angreppet, och jag kan bara hÃ¥lla med. I dag kÃ¶per EU-lÃ¤nder rysk gas fÃ¶r mer pengar Ã¤n vad EU ger i ekonomiskt stÃ¶d till Ukraina. Det fÃ¶rsta halvÃ¥ret i Ã¥r kÃ¶pte EU rysk flytande gas fÃ¶r kostnaden motsvarande 140 000 drÃ¶nare.

Att fortsÃ¤tta sÃ¥ hÃ¤r Ã¤r att hÃ¤lla ner pengar i Rysslands krigskassa. Det Ã¤r att fÃ¶rlÃ¤nga kriget, och det Ã¤r dessutom att ge Putin makten Ã¶ver europeiska energipriser. Nu Ã¤r det slut pÃ¥ det! Jag Ã¤r sÃ¥ vÃ¤ldigt glad att detta parlament har tagit ett tydligt stÃ¤llningstagande fÃ¶r att stoppa rysk gas och olja och fÃ¶r Ã¥tgÃ¤rder mot den ryska skuggflottan. EU ska inte bekosta krigsbrott. Punkt.

Â Â

Â Â Arno Bausemer, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. â Herr PrÃ¤sident, meine Damen und Herren! Zu spÃ¤ter Stunde heiÃe ich Sie willkommen in der bunten energiepolitischen Fantasiewelt der grÃ¼nen Weltverbesserer. Dort freut man sich Ã¼ber die Sonne, die scheint, Ã¼ber den Wind, der weht, und Ã¼ber den Strom aus der Steckdose, der so wenig kostet, denn Sonne und Wind stellen ja keine Rechnung. Und um die Welt noch ein wenig besser zu machen, da dreht man dem Russen den Gashahn ab und sorgt dafÃ¼r, dass der Russe vor der EuropÃ¤ischen Union zu Boden geht.

Wer unserer Debatte hier folgt, der muss denken, in diesem Parlament ist der Wahnsinn ausgebrochen. Haben Sie auch nach mehr als drei Jahren nicht verstanden, dass immer neue Sanktionspakete unsere eigenen Volkswirtschaften schÃ¤digen und allein in Deutschland aktuell 10Â 000Â IndustriearbeitsplÃ¤tze jeden Monat verschwinden? Die sind weg. Haben Sie nicht verstanden, dass sich Russland einfach neue Abnehmer sucht? Durch die neue Pipeline SibirienÂ 2 werden kÃ¼nftig 50Â Milliarden Kubikmeter Erdgas jÃ¤hrlich nach China flieÃen.

PutinâPartei, RusslandâFreunde, fÃ¼nfte Kolonne Moskaus â beleidigen Sie die Kritiker Ihrer verfehlten Politik nur immer weiter. Das neue alte Europa der VaterlÃ¤nder wird wieder im Sinne und zugunsten dieser VaterlÃ¤nder und seiner BÃ¼rger handeln, und dazu gehÃ¶ren dann auch wieder wirtschaftliche Beziehungen mit Russland.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Andrea Wechsler (PPE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, sehr geehrte Frau Gedin! Wir befinden uns hier nicht in einer Phantasiewelt, sondern anders als Sie schreiben wir hier Geschichte. So wie in Europa einst aus Kohle und Stahl Frieden geschmiedet worden ist, so schmieden wir heute aus Wind, Sonne und Vernunft unsere Energiesicherheit und unsere Freiheit. Das Ende russischer Energieimporte ist mehr als Energiepolitik. Es ist ein Aderlass fÃ¼r eine Kriegswirtschaft, die mit jeder Kilowattstunde aus Europa lÃ¤nger atmet. Wir stehen zu unserer Verantwortung gegenÃ¼ber unseren BÃ¼rgerinnen und BÃ¼rgern, gegenÃ¼ber der Ukraine und gegenÃ¼ber unserer eigenen SouverÃ¤nitÃ¤t.

Aber: Energie in Europa muss auch bezahlbar sein. Und BÃ¼rokratie darf nicht zum Sand im Getriebe unserer Energieversorgung werden. Wir bauen DÃ¤mme gegenÃ¼ber Erpressung, nicht gegenÃ¼ber unseren PartnerlÃ¤ndern, die Energie nach Europa importieren, und nicht gegen die, die in Europa investieren, diversifizieren, sparen und erneuerbar produzieren. Daher ist unser Kompass in den nun folgenden Verhandlungen sehr klar: Unsere energiepolitischen Entscheidungen mÃ¼ssen die Lichter in Europa an halten, die Heizungen warm, unsere Industrie stark, aber zugleich den Geldhahn gegenÃ¼ber dem Kreml zudrehen.

Mit dieser Gesetzgebung, meine sehr geehrten Damen und Herren, haben wir die Chance, Geschichte zu schreiben. Lassen Sie uns diese Chance ergreifen â fÃ¼r Energiesicherheit und fÃ¼r Europa!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Vytenis Povilas Andriukaitis (S&D). â Mr President, Commissioner, Russia has deliberately exploited our energy dependence as an attack on European security and prosperity. By advancing the full ban on Russian oil to JanuaryÂ 2026 and on gas to JanuaryÂ 2027, Europe sends a firm message: we will never again be held hostage by Kremlin coercion.

We must close all sanctioned loopholes, target the Russian shadow fleet, and ensure that any gas transiting through Russia is treated as Russian and fully prohibited. Gradually phasing out Russian gas imports, cutting risky dependencies and blocking circumventions strengthens our sovereignty.

This regulation also reinforces import verification and enforcement, including minimum administrative fines of 5Â % of the infringing company's worldwide annual turnover. This is a clear statement: Europe will defend its energy dependency, its economy and its values.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Csaba DÃ¶mÃ¶tÃ¶r (PfE). â ElnÃ¶k Ãr! Nagy egyetÃ©rtÃ©s van itt abban, hogy be kell tiltani az orosz energiaimportot. Ez maguknak itt az ideolÃ³giÃ¡rÃ³l szÃ³l, nekÃ¼nk viszont a gazdasÃ¡gi kÃ¡rokrÃ³l. Nem OroszorszÃ¡gnak, hanem nekÃ¼nk. Ez a lÃ©pÃ©s a mi esetÃ¼nkben, szÃ¡mÃ­tÃ¡sok szerint akkora vesztesÃ©get okoz, mint a GDP 4%-a. Nem mi mondjuk, hanem az IMF.

Ugyanaz fog tÃ¶rtÃ©nni, mint korÃ¡bban a szankciÃ³kkal, a korÃ¡bbi szankciÃ³kkal. OroszorszÃ¡gnak akartak Ã¡rtani, de az eurÃ³pai gazdasÃ¡got lÅttÃ©k lÃ¡bon, vagy lehet, hogy egy kicsit feljebb. EzÃ©rt csÃ¶kken mÃ©g a nÃ©met ipari termelÃ©s is. Ãs mÃ©g valami. A lengyel bÃ­rÃ³sÃ¡g elengedte azt az embert, aki felrobbantotta az Ãszaki Ãramlatot. A lengyel kÃ¼lÃ¼gyminiszter ezt Ã¼dvÃ¶zÃ¶lte, Ã©s azt is remÃ©li, hogy az elkÃ¶vetÅ a BarÃ¡tsÃ¡g olajvezetÃ©ket is felrobbantja. Ez lesz? KulcsfontossÃ¡gÃº lÃ©tesÃ­tmÃ©nyek elleni tÃ¡madÃ¡sokra biztatnak majd Ã¡llami vezetÅk, ami azonnali kÃ¡rokat okoz mÃ¡s tagorszÃ¡gokban?
