Â Â Katri Kulmuni (Renew). â Arvoisa puhemies, vielÃ¤ viime vuonna Euroopan unioniin ostettiin venÃ¤lÃ¤istÃ¤ kaasua yhteensÃ¤ noin 15 miljardin euron edestÃ¤ Eurostatin mukaan. TÃ¤mÃ¤ raha on mennyt VenÃ¤jÃ¤n fossiiliseen energiaan ja heidÃ¤n sotataloutensa tukemiseen. Eurooppa on vÃ¤hentÃ¤nyt venÃ¤lÃ¤istÃ¤ kaasua ja Ã¶ljyÃ¤ verrattuna lÃ¤htÃ¶tilanteeseen ennen Ukrainan sotaa, mutta vielÃ¤ muutama vuosi sitten eniten Ã¶ljyÃ¤ ja maakaasua tÃ¤nne tuli nimenomaan VenÃ¤jÃ¤ltÃ¤, onneksi nÃ¤in ei ole enÃ¤Ã¤.

Kuluvan vuoden ennuste kaasun osalta Euroopan unionin tuonnista kohdistuu enÃ¤Ã¤ muutamiin maihin ja se on 13 prosenttia. Samalla viimeisen kahden vuoden aikana on kuitenkin pystytty vÃ¤hentÃ¤mÃ¤Ã¤n myÃ¶s kaasun kokonaiskulutusta lÃ¤hes viidenneksellÃ¤, mikÃ¤ on tavattoman hienoa.

Olen tyytyvÃ¤inen, ettÃ¤ parlamentilla on tiukka ja laaja kanta sen suhteen, ettÃ¤ ensi vuonna ei enÃ¤Ã¤ tehtÃ¤isi uusia kaasusopimuksia, vuodesta 2027 ei enÃ¤Ã¤ yhtÃ¤Ã¤n kaasumolekyyliÃ¤kÃ¤Ã¤n tulisi tÃ¤nne VenÃ¤jÃ¤ltÃ¤. Ja toivon kyllÃ¤, ettÃ¤ myÃ¶s komissio ja neuvosto tukisivat parlamentin laajaa kantaa, jolle iso osa eurooppalaisista ihmisistÃ¤ on nyt antanut tukensa.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Virginijus SinkeviÄius (Verts/ALE). â MrÂ President, dear colleagues, dear Commissioner, three years into Russia's war against Ukraine, Europe is still paying billions to Moscow. Every euro for Russian gas or oil fuels aggression against Ukraine and supports hybrid attacks against democracies here in the EU, and this must stop now.

We in Parliament choose action over delay and we insist that oil from Russia must stop in 2026. Gas and LNG must cease by 1Â JanuaryÂ 2027, a full year ahead of the original Commission timetable. Our position is clear: no new or amended contracts, no temporary storage, and no more loopholes that allow Russian energy to reach our market through third countries. We also refuse any exemptions from certain Member States, and this ban must apply equally to all.

We ensure robust verification and full transparency and strict penalties. Now, the Council must match our ambitions because Europe's security cannot wait until 2028. Let's turn off the tap of the Kremlin's energy money permanently and prove that Europe stands united and independent.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jussi Saramo (The Left). â Mr President, Russia's war on Ukraine has been going on for 11 years, and this full-force attack for almost 4 years. This is the largest and deadliest war in Europe since World War II. Every single day, every single night, Ukrainians live in fear of missiles and drone attacks.

After all these years, we still finance this killing by buying Russian gas and oil. There have been good developments, as crude oil purchases have declined, but the EU remains the largest buyer of Russian gas. Member States bought EURÂ 22Â billion of Russian oil and gas in the third year of the full-scale war, an amount greater than the EURÂ 19Â billion the EU allocated to Ukraine in financial aid in 2024.

Enough is enough. It is time to make a full stop in buying Russian fuels, and it's time to stop Russian imperialism.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â NicolÃ¡s Pascual de la Parte (PPE). â SeÃ±or presidente, ha llegado la hora de cegar todos los medios de financiaciÃ³n del esfuerzo ruso, por eso apoyamos decididamente la propuesta de la ComisiÃ³n de adelantar, al 1 de enero de 2027, la prohibiciÃ³n total de importaciÃ³n de gas natural en Europa.

Pero, ademÃ¡s, estamos a favor de prohibir las importaciones de otros elementos, por ejemplo, la energÃ­a nuclear o los derivados de los hidrocarburos. Eso cegarÃ¡ la financiaciÃ³n del esfuerzo bÃ©lico ruso y, al mismo tiempo, reducirÃ¡ la dependencia europea de terceros paÃ­ses no democrÃ¡ticos.

Para ello tenemos tambiÃ©n que luchar contra las vÃ­as indirectas de incorporaciÃ³n de gas ruso y, para eso, tenemos que aprobar unos certificados de trazabilidad claros que nos digan a travÃ©s de quÃ© rutas entra indirectamente el gas ruso en Europa.

Y, finalmente, si realmente somos serios respecto a la voluntad de cegar la financiaciÃ³n a Rusia, tenemos que empezar a debatir sanciones econÃ³micas, diplomÃ¡ticas y comerciales a aquellos paÃ­ses terceros que siguen comprando gas y petrÃ³leo rusos. Eso serÃ­a definitivo para demostrar nuestra seriedad con respecto a la crisis de Ucrania.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Niels Fuglsang (S&D). â MrÂ President, dear colleagues, dear Commissioner, congratulations once again with your good proposal to phase out Russian gas, and congratulations to the political groups here in the European Parliament for having been able to keep their heads cool and their hearts warm, and negotiate an agreement that has wide support. I think this is worth noticing in a time where it seems that politics is becoming more and more polarised, that we are not able to agree on many things. But this â this we have been able to agree on from left to right, that it is wrong to keep on financing MrÂ Putin's war, that it is wrong to give Russia an instrument that they can use to put pressure on us, that it is wrong to to spend more on Russian gas than we have spent on helping Ukraine.

It is a great day for us that finally this will come to an end. I think this is about time and congratulations to Europe.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Petra Steger (PfE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident! Ja, die EU will jetzt ein komplettes Verbot russischer Energieimporte und beweist mal wieder, dass wir nur noch von scheinheiligen Moralisten regiert werden, denen die BÃ¼rger in Europa wirklich vollkommen egal sind. Sie stÃ¼rzen damit Europa in die nÃ¤chste Krise, Sie zerstÃ¶ren die Energiesicherheit in Europa, und Sie treiben einzelne Mitgliedstaaten mit voller Absicht in den wirtschaftlichen Ruin. Sie negieren Fakten, geografische Gegebenheiten, und Sie negieren, dass nicht jedes Land so einfach auf Alternativen umsteigen kann.

Sieht so die so dringend notwendige Diversifikation aus? Ein Verbot von Energiequellen â ist das wirklich Ihr Ernst? Reicht Ihnen die Inflation noch nicht? Wollen Sie noch mehr Menschen in die Armut stÃ¼rzen? Genau das werden Sie nÃ¤mlich erreichen, solange es keine kostengÃ¼nstigen Alternativen gibt. Teuerung, Deindustrialisierung und Armut in Europa â und das Schlimmste ist: Sie stehlen auch noch den Mitgliedstaaten ihr Vetorecht. Denn anstatt als Sanktionspaket beschlieÃen Sie das Ganze als REPowerEUâPaket, umgehen so die Einstimmigkeit und brechen europÃ¤isches Recht. Sieht so die Rechtsstaatlichkeit in der EU aus und der Respekt vor nationalstaatlicher SouverÃ¤nitÃ¤t? Eine absolute Schande!

Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren, ohne leistbare und sichere Energieversorgung gibt es keinen funktionierenden und wettbewerbsfÃ¤higen Markt in Europa. Sie fahren Europa mit voller Kraft gegen die Wand.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Adam Bielan (ECR). â Mr President, by continuing imports of Russian oil, Europe is financing Russia's war against Ukraine. How can we claim solidarity with Kyiv when billions of euros still flow to Moscow every month? It is shameful! We are three years into Russia's full-scale invasion and, in 2024, 85Â % of the EU's Russian LNG imports went to France, Belgium and Spain. Every shipment, every payment strengthens the Russian aggressor we claim to oppose.

Poland has proved that it is possible to cut energy ties with Russia. We accomplished it long before, thanks to the Law and Justice government. We built a new LNG terminal and a new pipeline from Norway. We diversified our energy supplies.

How can we demand unconditional support from the United States when our own Member States are still bankrolling Putin's war machine? Europe must act like it truly believes in victory for Ukraine. Cut the imports of Russian energy now, not by 2028. It is high time!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Christophe Grudler (Renew). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, tous les EuropÃ©ens se souviennent de la flambÃ©e de leur facture en 2022. Une crise provoquÃ©e par l'invasion de l'Ukraine et par le chantage Ã©nergÃ©tique de la Russie. En 2014 dÃ©jÃ , Poutine menaÃ§ait de couper le gaz Ã  l'Europe. Enfin un message pour vous, Monsieur le Commissaire: ne remplaÃ§ons pas une dÃ©pendance par une autre. Les engagements pris envers les Ãtats-Unis sont incomprÃ©hensibles. Le GNL amÃ©ricain ne doit pas devenir notre nouveau talon d'Achille. Restons maÃ®tres de notre destin, en EuropÃ©ens.

Â Â President. â I would like to announce that we will close catch-the-eye. We will take the four catch-the-eyes that we have.

We will also close the blue cards.

Â Â MarkÃ©ta GregorovÃ¡ (Verts/ALE). â MrÂ President, dear colleagues, every euro that still flows to Moscow through energy trade is a euro funding Russia's war machine. We have heard today from the far right so much about funding the war â these flows are funding the war, and funding it we are.

In 2024, the EU still imported almost EURÂ 22Â billion worth of fossil fuels from Russia â more than the aid paid to Ukraine in the same period. In the ongoing negotiations on phasing out Russian gas and oil, the Council has included several loopholes. These carve-outs undermine the effectiveness of our sanctions and prolong the war in Ukraine â something everybody in this room claims they do not want.

The Union already has the instruments to support this transition for Slovakia, for Hungary, including the REPowerEU plan, large-scale investments in renewables and improved interconnections between Member States. We as the Czech Republic are very happy to help Slovakia.

To continue as we are is to fund the aggressor. So it is time to close the loopholes and stop the money flows and prove that the EU speaks and acts without hypocrisy.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marina Mesure (The Left). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, l'arrÃªt des importations de gaz russe est une dÃ©cision absolument nÃ©cessaire pour cesser de financer la machine de guerre de Poutine. Â Â Riho Terras (PPE). â MrÂ President, it is no secret that Russia finances its war of aggression against Ukraine by selling fossil fuels. Dependence on Russian gas and oil is a chronic disease for many European countries. Many third countries see this as an opportunity to buy blood gas and oil at a below market price. We all remember the consequences of the recent energy crisis that was very much based on dependency from Russian sources. The European Union, together with the United States, must take stronger actions to encourage third countries to stop financing the Russian war machine. Needless to say, there is no place for Russian gas and oil in Europe. We must not tolerate the fact that the EU Member States pay Russia to kill innocent Ukrainian people.

Â 
Â Â

TÅetÃ­ zemÄ bohatnou na sankcÃ­ch, kterÃ© majÃ­ ekonomicky oslabit Rusko, a Evropa, ztrÃ¡cÃ­ vliv, prÅ¯mysl i dÅ¯vÄru lidÃ­. Pokud chce Komise skuteÄnÄ uzavÃ­rat mezery, aÅ¥ zaÄne u vlastnÃ­ naivity. Evropa potÅebuje energetickou suverenitu, levnÃ© ceny energiÃ­, ne gesta, kterÃ¡ platÃ­me my vÅ¡ichni a kterÃ¡ se reÃ¡lnÄ mÃ­jÃ­ ÃºÄinkem.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Rihards Kols (ECR). â MrÂ President, dear colleagues,Â Europe has come a long way in cutting its dependence on Russia's energy. Gas, oil, coal â weâve reduced it all. But partial progress is not victory.

Every tanker of Russian LNG that still arrives in Europe, every barrel relabelled through India, every Rosatom contract operating quietly here in Europe â they all keep feeding Kremlinâs war machine.Â Dependence on aggressors is not a market choice; itâs a strategic and an expensive mistake. You all know this.

Today, around 20Â % of Europeâs nuclear reactors still depend on Russian nuclear fuel. In 2024 alone, over EURÂ 700Â million worth of nuclear materials came from Russia. Rosatom is operating under the radar in critical parts of our energy sector.Â We must fully phase out all Russian energy imports, including nuclear. We must close loopholes, sanction intermediaries, and put an end to Russiaâs role in Europeâs nuclear supply chains. We must invest in diversification: hydrogen, modular reactors, and new nuclear supply chains built with trusted partners, not authoritarian regimes.

Energy independence is not just a national priority â it is a Union-wide security imperative.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â MichaÅ Kobosko (Renew). â Mr President, dear colleagues, energy imports in Europe are fuelling Russia's war machine. We should take all possible steps to stop buying from Russia and its friends. I am proud of our ITRE and INTA committees for making the first big step beyond sanctions, which have their limits with the unanimity rule being in place. Special thanks to my colleagues Katri Kulmuni and Ä½ubica KarvaÅ¡ovÃ¡, who fought tooth and nail to make sure that the phasing-out of Russian energy imports comes faster than initially planned by the Commission.

On top of it, I am also happy that my proposals â strengthening enforcement, giving Member States specific tools when it comes to introducing penalties and extending the scope of entities covered by the rules â were fully supported. But this job is half done. Now we need to expedite negotiations with the Council, and convince our colleagues in the capitals that expanding the scope of the regulation also to oil and petroleum products from Russia is possible. Even if we still need to introduce some exemptions, I think it is worth taking this first step ahead. Russia's war cannot be financed by us any longer!

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Un visiem Eiropas politiskajiem pensionÄriem, kuri mums mÄÄ£ina stÄstÄ«t, ka ânu, mums jau Å¡Ä·iet, ka Krievija mainÄ«sies, un mÄs taÄu tik labi tirgojÄmiesâ, gribu atgÄdinÄt, kur tad noveda mÅ«s Å¡ieÂ Noardstream projekti un atkarÄ«ba no Krievijas naftas â pie vÄjas Eiropas un pie Krievijas uzbrukuma Ukrainai. Eiropa vienkÄrÅ¡i baroja Å¡o impÄrisko monstru cerÄ«bÄ, ka tas neuzbruks. TÄ ka ne centa Ä¼aunuma impÄrijai â Krievija ir jÄsakauj un tikai tad EiropÄ bÅ«s miers un droÅ¡Ä«ba.

Â Â Danilo Della Valle (The Left). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, state condannando le famiglie e le imprese a pagare i costi della guerra. Grazie alle vostre politiche scellerate, il prezzo medio delle bollette del gas aumenterÃ  di circa 300 EUR l'anno per famiglia.

Abbiamo provato in tutti i modi a farvi ragionare: l'Europa non era pronta a eliminare da un giorno all'altro il gas russo, cosa che non ha fatto neanche l'Ucraina. Avevamo chiesto un cambio graduale. Il piano europeo per il clima parla di 2050 per l'eliminazione di tutte le fonti fossili da Paesi extra-UE, non solo dalla Russia quindi.

E invece, oggi si parla di 2027: Ã¨ una follia. Queste misure non servono a fermare la guerra: Ã¨ propaganda, perchÃ© volete favorire l'acquisto di shale gas dagli Stati Uniti, che costa il triplo ed Ã¨ climalterante quanto il carbone.

Ecco, io ho qui due bollette di una famiglia di pensionati della mia regione, la Campania, una del 2021 e una del 2025. Il costo del gas Ã¨ aumentato in quattro anni del 150% â piÃ¹ del doppio! E intanto pensioni e salari sono fermi al palo.

Ecco, il tempo delle bugie Ã¨ finito e i cittadini hanno capito che state portando l'Europa a schiantarsi contro un muro.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Des sanctions ciblÃ©es peuvent Ãªtre efficaces si elles affaiblissent l'agresseur sans punir nos propres peuples. Or, les sanctions Ã©nergÃ©tiques sur le gaz russe sont un Ã©chec flagrant. Alors soyons clairs: des sanctions, oui, mais utiles. Cessons d'appliquer des mesures qui affaiblissent l'Europe bien plus que la Russie. Â Â Karin Karlsbro (Renew). â Herr talman! Kommissionen. Kollegor. ÃhÃ¶rare. Redan infÃ¶r EU-valet 2009 varnade Liberalerna i Sverige fÃ¶r att Europa skulle gÃ¶ra sig beroende av Putins gas, eftersom det Ã¤r tvÃ¥ problem med rysk gas: den Ã¤r rysk och det Ã¤r gas.

Â Â Jutta Paulus (Verts/ALE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Kommissar, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Dieses Haus will endlich Europas AbhÃ¤ngigkeit von russischem Gas und Ãl beenden â endgÃ¼ltig, kompromisslos, ohne SchlupflÃ¶cher. Viel zu lange hat sich die EuropÃ¤ische Union von Putins Handlangern OrbÃ¡n und Fico erpressen lassen. Sie haben Sanktionen blockiert und dazu beigetragen, Putins Kriegskasse zu fÃ¼llen.

An russischem Ãl und Gas klebt ukrainisches Blut. Damit muss Schluss sein! Und anders als Kommission und Rat wollen wir eben nicht nur an Gas ran, sondern wir wollen, dass russisches Ãl ab Januar 2026, russisches Gas ab Januar 2027 nicht mehr in die EU eingefÃ¼hrt werden darf.

Aber: Angereichertes Uran, BrennstÃ¤be, Nukleartechnologie werden weiter nach Europa importiert. Und noch schlimmer: Dank der engen Kooperation zwischen Framatome und Rosatom gehen russische Ingenieure in sensiblen europÃ¤ischen Anlagen ein und aus. Â Â Costas Mavrides (S&D). â Mr President, this draft legislation to ban Putin's oil and gas imports is not only about the EU, but finally to close the loopholes through third countries towards an energy-independent and stronger Europe.

Regarding third countries, Turkey is a striking scandal. Even recently, Turkish officials stated that they will not comply with any sanctions, just like they do for years. The Commission, particularly the High Representative, pretend they know nothing. They are supposed to be the guardian of our collective EU interests.

Specifically, a European project of common interest ending the energy isolation of Cyprus, connecting it with the European energy grid and strengthening the EU's autonomy by accessing its own energy reserves in the eastern Mediterranean is blocked by Turkey against EU interests, and the Commission considers Turkey a reliable partner for Euro defence.

This is not just hypocrisy, this is bad politics for Europe.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Angelika Winzig (PPE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Kommissar, Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Der Ausstieg Europas aus russischem Gas und Ãl ist mehr als eine energiepolitische Entscheidung. Er ist eine moralische Notwendigkeit. Versorgungssicherheit und PreisstabilitÃ¤t werden natÃ¼rlich jetzt in den Mittelpunkt treten, denn wir werden nicht lÃ¤nger Ihren Krieg finanzieren, Herr Putin.

Klar, Energieversorgungssicherheit muss natÃ¼rlich gewÃ¤hrleistet sein, und Investitionen in erneuerbare Energie mÃ¼ssen ohne zusÃ¤tzliche BÃ¼rokratie verstÃ¤rkt angegangen werden. Leider ist die rechte Seite wieder nicht da, aber eine PVâAnlage hat nichts mit Traumland zu tun. Das ist RealitÃ¤t heutzutage und ganz einfach. Ich wollte es ihnen zeigen, aber sie sind leider nicht mehr da.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Elisabetta Gualmini (S&D). â MrÂ President, dear Commissioner, today it's really an historical moment because we have been talking for many years about the need to increase the strategic autonomy of the European Union, and the European Union has never been a fully-fledged political entity because it has suffered from two dependencies: in the east, on the gas from Russia, and in the west, on the United States for defence. A democracy that depends on an autocracy to heat its homes and companies is not a fully-fledged democracy. This is the point. What kind of Europe do we want? Only a huge commercial space or an autonomous political entity?

So, today we are really on the right track. The full ban on gas in 2028 will give us a freedom and autonomy, and above all, will free us from the deception of financing ourselves the war against the Ukrainians. Freeing ourselves from Putin's gas means freeing ourselves from his hate for our values, democracy, peace and pluralism.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â JÃ¼ri Ratas (PPE). â MrÂ President, Commissioner, dear colleagues, it has now been three years and eight months since Russia launched its brutal war of aggression against Ukraine. And yet, Europe still continues to import Russian energy. This is unacceptable. Stop importing all forms of energy from Russia! Every single euro paid to Moscow helps fund its war machine.

Since the start of the war, EU countries have made more than EURÂ 210Â billion for Russian fossil energy. It is almost as much as Russia has spent on the war itself. This means that even today, European money continues to finance the Kremlin's aggression against Ukraine.

Together with the EPP Group, I firmly stand for ending all Russian energy imports as quickly as possible and for closing energy loopholes through third countries. Words and half measures are not enough. We must take bold action.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â NicolÃ¡s GonzÃ¡lez Casares (S&D). â SeÃ±or presidente, nuestro compromiso con Ucrania estaba cojo y ahora ya no lo estÃ¡. Damos la bienvenida a este amplio acuerdo para acabar con la importaciÃ³n de gas ruso. Y el compromiso serÃ¡ aÃºn mayor si conseguimos que ni un solo euro de la economÃ­a europea fluya hacia las manos de Putin por la compra de combustibles fÃ³siles. Ese debe ser un compromiso aÃºn mayor.

En gallego tenemos la palabraÂ xeito, que es actuar con pragmatismo y sentido comÃºn. Y yo pido tambiÃ©nÂ xeito en esta actuaciÃ³n. Para apostar de verdad por la electrificaciÃ³n, por la eficiencia energÃ©tica y por el potencial renovable de Europa. La mejor molÃ©cula de gas fÃ³sil para Europa es la que no se consume; ni rusa ni de ningÃºn otro lugar. Por lo tanto, tambiÃ©n debemos comprometernos en algo mÃ¡s: en hacer que esto sea permanente. Que nadie vuelva a tener la tentaciÃ³n de conectarse al gas ruso. Europa no serÃ¡ competitiva mientras seamos yonquis del gas ruso o de cualquier otro gas.

Y, por Ãºltimo, dÃ©jeme decir una cosa. Gas ruso, no. PetrÃ³leo ruso, tampoco. Pero el uranio, Â¿para cuÃ¡ndo?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Wouter Beke (PPE). â Voorzitter, commissaris, collegaâs, elke euro die naar Russisch gas of naar de olie gaat in Rusland is een euro die de oorlog van Poetin weet te voeden. Vandaag zeggen we âgenoeg!â. TegenÂ 2027 draaien we de kraan definitief dicht. Niet enkel uit noodzaak, maar ook uit overtuiging.

Dat is een historische beslissing, want die gaat niet alleen over energie, maar ook over morele verantwoordelijkheid en strategische onafhankelijkheid. Europa neemt die verantwoordelijkheid, want het gaat over waarden, over vrede en over veiligheid. En ik hoop dat de Raad ook die verantwoordelijkheid zal nemen.

Maar deze keuze vraagt ook om visie, mijnheer de commissaris. De Commissie moet diversifiÃ«ren en partnerschappen versterken, zodat we onze afhankelijkheid niet gewoon verleggen, maar echt autonomer worden wat onze energie betreft. En ze moet dringend, heel dringend zorgen voor betaalbare energie voor gezinnen en voor bedrijven. Vandaag kiest Europa voor vrede, voor OekraÃ¯ne, voor verantwoordelijkheid en voor een grotere autonomie.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Bruno Tobback (S&D). â Voorzitter, mijn gelukwensen aan commissaris JÃ¸rgensen dat er gedaan is wat er gedaan moest worden, wat al lang gedaan had moeten zijn. Want ook na de agressie op de Krim vanÂ 2014, na de invasie inÂ 2022 en tot op de dag van vandaag blijven we als Europeanen miljarden bijdragen aan de Russische oorlogsmachine die ons bedreigt. Het geeft een heel nieuwe invulling aan het begrip âje eigen graf gravenâ en het is hoog tijd dat het stopt. Maar laat ons nu ook de lessen hieruit trekken.

Handel met andere landen is goed, afhankelijkheid is fout. Onze economie bouwen op geÃ¯mporteerde grondstoffen is de kortste weg naar afhankelijkheid. En het dure aardgas is vandaag niet alleen verantwoordelijk voor het gebrek aan concurrentievermogen in onze industrie, het is ook een wapen waarmee Europa vandaag zelf nog wordt gechanteerd. Dus echte onafhankelijkheid voor Europa betekent onafhankelijkheid van alle geÃ¯mporteerde fossiele brandstoffen. Hopelijk hebben we ook daarvoor de komende tijd de moed.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

DependenÈa de lungÄ duratÄ de Rusia ne-a adus aici Èi sÄ nu o schimbÄm cu dependenÈa de Statele Unite, cÄ pe mine m-a Ã®ngrijorat cÄ 45% din importurile de gaz natural lichefiat de anul trecut au fost din Statele Unite. CabhraÃ­onn gach euro a Ã­ocaimid ar fhuinneamh na RÃºise le cogaÃ­ocht Putin a mhaoiniÃº, cogadh atÃ¡ ag cruthÃº gÃ©archÃ©ime san Aontas Eorpach. Â Â

Â Â Grzegorz Braun (NI). â Mr President, colleagues, I see you are preparing a suicide note for Europe. Europe is going to commit to suicide â hara-kiri â by cutting itself from resources of energy, of oil, gas, while everybody around is buying.

Remember last year, an American official â if I remember well, the Secretary of the Treasury â commented on Russian oil being imported to the United States of America through India, and she said that Russian oil refined by India is no longer Russian. So this is a game where everybody is equal, but some are more equal.

I don't want to commit the same suicide together with you. This is your choice, I'm against.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Anna StÃ¼rgkh (Renew). â Herr PrÃ¤sident! Das russische Gas war das teuerste, das wir je gekauft habenÂ â das sagt OMVâChef Stern. Jahrelang hat die FPÃ versucht, uns weiszumachen, dass wir vom gÃ¼nstigen russischen Gas profitierenÂ â eine Unwahrheit, von der nur ein Mensch profitiert, und das ist Putin. Die Wahrheit ist, dass dieses russische Gas das teuerste war, das wir je gekauft haben. Russisches Gas, das uns Milliarden gekostet hat, das direkt aus unseren GeldbÃ¶rsen zu Putin geflossen ist.

Aber es hat uns auch unsere UnabhÃ¤ngigkeit gekostet. Und die Ukraine? Die kostet unser russisches Gas das wertvollste Ã¼berhaupt â Menschenleben. Erst heute hat Russland wieder einen Kindergarten in der Ukraine bombardiert, mit Drohnen, die auch mit europÃ¤ischem Geld finanziert werden. Es ist hÃ¶chste Zeit, dass wir endlich die russischen Gasfesseln ein fÃ¼r alle Mal von uns werfen, ein fÃ¼r alle Mal brechen und sichergehen, dass wir auch in Zukunft nie wieder abhÃ¤ngig sind von dem Kriegstreiber Putin.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Dan JÃ¸rgensen, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, so on one hand, this is a complex debate; on the other hand, it's quite simple. Do we want to allow Putin to weaponise energy against us? Do we want to allow Putin to blackmail Member States of the European Union? Do we want to buy energy and thereby indirectly help finance Putin's war against our friends in Ukraine? No. Not only is it a no, it's a never again.Â This is why, yes, sanctions are necessary and we're working on them also. But this is why we also need a prohibition, legislation that will also stand even when hopefully there is a peace â because we can never allow ourselves to repeat the mistake that we did in the past to become dependent on an enemy.

I used to serve in a previous job as Minister of Energy in Denmark, and I had that responsibility when Putin started his full-scale invasion. And I can share with you that what we did in the ministry the first couple of weeks was night and day making emergency plans for what we would do if Putin turned off the taps for us. Which companies would we force to close down? I'm talking about sending men and women in uniform to close them down. This we would have had to do to safeguard our hospitals and our homes. And we were not even the country that was most exposed. We can no longer and never again make that mistake.

So, friends, I am so grateful for the support that you've given today. So, okay, there's some divergence between our proposal from the Commission side and the general approach from the Council and what's been voted through the Parliament, but there's more common ground, and I'm sure we can find the compromises necessary as fast as possible. Frankly speaking, this has taken too long, but now we're doing it. Now we're acting. And in a turbulent time where also the European Union sometimes is not working as fast as we would all want it to, this is really an example of the opposite. It's an example of what we can do if we want to.

I look forward to working with you again. I stand, of course, at your disposal so we can sort out the differences that we have now and agree on a good compromise.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â The debate is closed.
